Past Future Continuous, however, gradually reveals itself to be something more complex than straight documentary, provocatively slipping between historical fact and narrative fabrication – in the process, gesturing towards the tragic broader relevance of Maryam’s story of displacement to families in today’s Iran.

Viewer Advice: Contains stroboscopic imagery

A filmmaker’s approach to chemistry experiments produces dazzlingly psychedelic results in this spectacular, scientifically illuminating Australian debut.

When thinking about the world’s natural wonders, it’s likely the big stuff – the mountains, the canyons, the oceans – that first spring to mind. But what about those things you can’t see, such as light, gravity and quantum energy? Using a high-powered camera and absolutely no AI, Melbourne-based filmmaker Josef Gatti makes brilliantly manifest those intangible building blocks of our universe. Across 10 chapters, each dedicated to a single scientific concept, kaleidoscopic Petri dish displays are augmented by tracks from Nils Frahm and British electronic musician Rival Consoles. It’s nature, but like you’ve never seen it before.

Beginning its life a decade ago as a series of shorts, Phenomena takes mesmerising final form as a full-length feature: a masterful melding of art and science that has toured documentary film festivals around the world. Gatti’s articulate and charming voiceover narration – crafted with the assistance of his father, a physics teacher – offers a mind-blowing, singular journey into the hidden world around us. This is the high school science lesson you’ve always wished you had.
synopsis_zh:
观影提示：本片含频闪影像

在这部叹为观止、又极富科学启迪的澳大利亚长片处女作中，一位电影人以拍摄化学实验的方式，呈现出令人目眩神迷的迷幻影像。

说到世间的自然奇观，人们最先想到的往往是那些庞然大物——高山、峡谷、海洋。可那些你看不见的事物呢？比如光、引力和量子能量。墨尔本电影人约瑟夫·加蒂借助一台高性能摄影机、且完全不使用任何人工智能，将这些构成宇宙的无形基石璀璨地呈现于眼前。全片分为十个章节，每一章聚焦一个科学概念，培养皿中万花筒般的绚丽景象，更由尼尔斯·弗拉姆与英国电子音乐人Rival Consoles的乐曲加以烘托。这是自然，却是你从未见过的模样。

《万象》十年前以系列短片起步，如今终以一部完整长片的形态呈现出其动人心魄的终极面貌：一场艺术与科学的精妙交融，已在世界各地的纪录片影展巡回展映。加蒂清晰而迷人的旁白解说——在其身为物理教师的父亲协助下打磨而成——为我们开启一段震撼心灵、独一无二的旅程，通往身边那个隐秘的世界。这就是你一直渴望拥有的那堂中学科学课。
highlight_zh: 无AI纯手工拍摄，《Variety》盛赞的迷幻科学影像

synopsis_en:
Crude, lust-riddled and brilliantly cracked, this Gold Coast–set fever dream is the latest twisted caper from maverick auteur Adam C. Briggs.

Introduced languishing in a faded motel, the eponymous Phillip is a Brit and by trade a screenwriter, though he’s not doing much writing these days. He’s more interested in frittering his time away, pursuing flights of fantasy and intoxicating indulgences even as the line between the two grows ever fuzzier. In this, he’s aided and abetted by an aspiring actor with whom he’s enjoying a dalliance – though Phillip suspects the younger man might be mainly interested in his showbiz connections – and the drug dealer next door.

The prolific and indefatigable Briggs has often worked closely with non-professional actors to craft narratives whose blends of fact and fiction are as riotous as they are discomforting; he previously garnered acclaim for his 2024 feature A Grand Mockery (co-directed with and starring Sam Dixon, who also appears in this film in a supporting role), which took out top honours at SXSW Sydney as well as the Best Director prize at Sitges. With Phillip Philadelphia, the Meanjin-born writer/director captures the garish, grubby beauty of the Gold Coast on 16mm, evoking Florida as seen through the lenses of Harmony Korine and Sean Baker yet also extending his own distinctively homegrown, eloquently vulgar style.
synopsis_zh:
粗粝、纵欲、疯得精彩，这部以黄金海岸为背景的狂热幻梦，是特立独行的作者型导演亚当·C·布里格斯最新的怪诞冒险之作。

影片开场，同名主角菲利普正窝在一家褪色的汽车旅馆里虚度光阴。他是个英国人，本行是编剧，可如今几乎不动笔了。比起写作，他更热衷于挥霍时光、沉溺于种种幻想与醉生梦死，而这两者之间的界线也越来越模糊。在这条路上，有两个人为他推波助澜：一个是与他打得火热的怀揣明星梦的年轻演员——尽管菲利普怀疑对方看中的多半是他在演艺圈的人脉——另一个则是隔壁的毒贩。

高产而不知疲倦的布里格斯常与非职业演员密切合作，打造出虚实交织的叙事，既恣意妄为又令人不安。他执导的2024年长片《一场盛大的嘲弄》曾广受赞誉（该片由他与萨姆·迪克森联合执导并主演，迪克森在本片中亦有配角出演），一举拿下悉尼西南偏南电影节最高荣誉及锡切斯电影节最佳导演奖。在《菲利普·费城》中，这位生于米安津的编剧兼导演用16毫米胶片捕捉了黄金海岸浮华而肮脏的美，让人联想到哈莫尼·科林与肖恩·贝克镜头下的佛罗里达，同时又延续了他独树一帜、粗俗却不失优雅的本土风格。
highlight_zh: SXSW与锡切斯获奖导演新作，16毫米胶片下的黄金海岸狂热幻梦

synopsis_en:
Driven wild by an inexplicable itch, a desperate man seeks relief in a city’s seedy underbelly.

Lucas wakes up one night with an itch so terrible he’ll brave any grimy underworld den in Lima – and do anything! – to soothe it. Screening at Sitges 2025 and Sundance 2026, Alex Fischman Cárdenas’s magnetic, uncategorisably strange short hurtles its existentially tormented protagonist forward with Safdie-like pace and tension, ratcheting up Lucas’s predicament to an audacious final scene you absolutely won’t see coming.
synopsis_zh:
一股莫名的瘙痒将一个走投无路的男人逼疯，他一头扎进城市阴暗的地下世界，只为求得片刻解脱。

一天深夜，卢卡斯被一阵难以忍受的瘙痒惊醒。为了止痒，他甘愿闯进利马任何一间肮脏的地下窝点——什么都愿意做！本片入围锡切斯电影节（2025）与圣丹斯电影节（2026），亚历克斯·菲施曼·卡德纳斯用这部极具魔力、难以归类的怪异短片，以萨夫迪兄弟式的凌厉节奏与紧张感，推着这位深陷存在主义折磨的主角一路狂奔，将他的困境层层推向一个你绝对预料不到的大胆结局。
highlight_zh: 锡切斯+圣丹斯入围，萨夫迪式凌厉奇片，结局炸裂

synopsis_en:
Charming vignettes explore the full awkwardness, absurdity and disappointment of humankind’s yearning to be loved.

In a series of darkly comedic stop-motion scenes, Winston (Stellan Skarsgård, Sentimental Value) and other urbanites search for love while the city burns behind them. Even as the characters strive to conceal their needy and pathetic sides, their soft felt bodies are as enchanting as their beautifully detailed clothing and everyday surroundings. Loosely overlapping misadventures gradually form a tender and absurd little portrait of collective longing.
synopsis_zh:
一组组迷人的小品，道尽人类渴望被爱时那份笨拙、荒诞与失落。

在一系列黑色幽默的定格动画场景里，温斯顿（斯特兰·斯卡斯加德，《感伤价值》）和其他都市男女在身后燃烧的城市中寻觅爱情。角色们竭力掩藏自己黏人又可怜的一面，可他们柔软的毛毡身躯，连同那些做工精致的衣着与日常环境，依旧惹人怜爱。一段段松散交叠的糗事，渐渐拼出一幅关于集体渴望的温柔而荒诞的小小画像。
highlight_zh: 斯卡斯加德献声，毛毡定格动画演尽求爱的荒诞

synopsis_en:
In Shirley Clarke’s magnetic work of queer cinema, New York hustler, would-be cabaret star and raconteur supreme Jason Holliday delivers a monologue for the ages.

Holding court over a long, boozy December night at New York’s Hotel Chelsea, Jason Holliday serves up wild anecdotes of a life lived on the city’s margins, including working the streets as a hustler, doing time on Riker’s Island and even befriending Miles Davis – fraught, flamboyant tales that become profound reflections on race, class and sexuality. Meanwhile, the offscreen prompts from director Shirley Clarke (Ornette: Made in America, MIFF 1987), a pioneering figure in documentary’s avant-garde, add to the film’s air of electric, off-the-cuff vérité.

Just how much of Jason’s studied fabulousness, in which the self is nested within endless layers of performance, is an elaborate shield against a hostile world, including the provocations of his director? And to what extent might Clarke, whose antagonism stokes the film’s irresistible frisson, be said to be exploiting him? Both an exquisite study in character and a fascinating look at the ethics behind capturing performance in documentary, Portrait of Jason – like its subject – is a slippery, shapeshifting work, as exhilarating as it is unsettling.
synopsis_zh:
在雪莉·克拉克这部极具魅力的酷儿电影中，纽约的男妓、渴望成为夜总会明星的表演者、无与伦比的说书人杰森·霍利迪奉上了一段传世独白。

在纽约切尔西酒店，一个漫长而醉意醺然的十二月夜晚，杰森·霍利迪高谈阔论，讲述他游走于城市边缘的种种荒诞往事——在街头拉客卖身、在瑞克斯岛服刑，甚至还与迈尔斯·戴维斯结下交情。这些紧张而张扬的故事，渐渐化作对种族、阶级与性向的深刻省思。与此同时，画外导演雪莉·克拉克（《奥奈特：美国制造》，1987 年墨尔本电影节）——这位纪录片先锋派的开创性人物——不时抛出提示与追问，令影片弥漫着一种即兴、鲜活、如电流般的真实感。

杰森那份精心雕琢的“华丽”究竟有几分是真？在这场表演里，自我被层层叠叠的伪装包裹——这层层伪装，又在多大程度上是他抵御一个充满敌意的世界（包括导演的步步紧逼）的精致盔甲？而克拉克那份激起影片诱人张力的对抗姿态，是否也可被视为对他的一种剥削？《杰森的肖像》既是一幅精妙入微的人物素描，也是一次对纪录片如何捕捉表演之伦理的深入审视；它一如它的主人公，是一部滑不留手、变幻莫测的作品，既令人振奋，又令人不安。
highlight_zh: 酷儿电影经典，一场狡黠迷人的世纪独白

synopsis_en:
Splattered in copious animated sex and gore, this body-horror tale follows a vicious predator who’s part woman, part deadly insect … and all mum.

A carnivorous seductress flits between brothels and hostess bars in a red-light district, using razor-sharp forelegs to collect fresh meat for her hungry offspring. As the child’s appetite grows, what’s a monstrous mum to do? Joe Hsieh’s animated mutant body horror brings sex, violence and goofy comedy to a surprisingly tender ode to maternal love.
synopsis_zh:
这部血浆四溅、情欲横流的动画身体恐怖片，讲述了一头凶残猎食者的故事——她半是女人，半是致命昆虫……却也彻头彻尾是位母亲。

这位食肉的诱惑者穿梭于红灯区的妓院与陪酒吧之间，挥舞刀锋般锋利的前肢，为嗷嗷待哺的孩子猎取新鲜血肉。可当孩子的胃口越来越大，一位怪物母亲又该如何是好？谢文明这部变异身体恐怖动画，将情欲、暴力与无厘头的黑色幽默熔于一炉，谱成一曲出人意料、饱含柔情的母爱赞歌。
highlight_zh: 谢文明血浆动画，重口身体恐怖下藏着一曲母爱悲歌

synopsis_en:
Viewer Advice: Contains themes of sexual violence

Jennifer Robinson, Amber Heard and Brittany Higgins have all experienced the full force of weaponised defamation laws – but these indomitable activists and survivors of gendered violence won’t keep quiet.

In 2017, #MeToo replaced the social stigma of discussing gendered violence with public solidarity and support for victim-survivors. The re-silencing was swift, vicious … and legalistic. Alleged perpetrators now used defamation lawsuits as cudgels to punish survivors for speaking out, and to deter journalists from exposing prominent people to public scrutiny. As a barrister who famously stood up for Amber Heard in a long-running, all-too-public defamation case in the UK, Jennifer Robinson has dedicated her career to fighting against these attacks, witnessing firsthand how such actions drag survivors through emotionally, professionally and financially ruinous legal proceedings.

In her incendiary exposé that premiered in Sundance’s World Cinema Documentary Competition, Selina Miles (Martha: A Picture Story, MIFF 2019) gives voice to experts like Robinson and Sibongile Ndashe who – in their work as lawyers and activists – doggedly pursue procedural justice, as well as women like Higgins, Heard and Colombian journalist Catalina Ruiz-Navarro who’ve been the targets of or caught in defamation lawfare pursued by alleged perpetrators. This furious, urgent documentary indicts a misogynist status quo: as Ndashe says, “The law is designed not to believe women.”
synopsis_zh:
观影提示：本片包含性暴力相关主题

珍妮弗·罗宾逊、艾梅柏·希尔德与布列塔尼·希金斯都曾切身领教被武器化的诽谤法的全部威力——但这几位不屈的活动人士与性别暴力幸存者拒绝保持沉默。

2017 年，#MeToo 运动让谈论性别暴力从遭受社会污名转变为获得公众的声援与支持。然而，让受害者重新噤声的反扑来得迅猛、狠辣，且披着法律的外衣。被指控的施暴者转而把诽谤诉讼当作大棒，惩罚敢于发声的幸存者，并震慑记者，使其不敢将有头有脸的人物置于公众审视之下。作为在英国那场旷日持久、举世瞩目的诽谤案中挺身为艾梅柏·希尔德辩护而声名远扬的大律师，珍妮弗·罗宾逊毕生致力于对抗这类攻击，亲眼见证了此类诉讼如何将幸存者拖入情感、职业与经济上都足以令其倾家荡产的法律泥潭。

在这部于圣丹斯电影节世界电影纪录片竞赛单元首映的犀利揭露之作中，塞琳娜·迈尔斯（《玛莎：一张照片的故事》，2019 年墨尔本电影节）让罗宾逊、西邦吉莱·恩达谢等专家发声——他们身为律师与活动人士，锲而不舍地追求程序正义；影片也记录了希金斯、希尔德以及哥伦比亚记者卡塔琳娜·鲁伊斯-纳瓦罗等女性，她们或成为被指控施暴者所发动的诽谤式法律战的靶子，或深陷其中。这部愤怒而急切的纪录片，是对厌女现状的一纸控诉——正如恩达谢所言：“法律的设计，本就是为了不相信女性。”
highlight_zh: 圣丹斯首映，直击诽谤法如何让性暴力幸存者二次噤声

synopsis_en:
Hours before D-Day, General Eisenhower’s chief meteorologist warns of brutal weather – but doesn’t predict the stormy debate he now faces to save Allied lives.

In 72 hours, Allied forces will launch a mass invasion of Normandy to recapture Europe from the Nazis. Newly arrived at HQ, Scottish meteorologist Captain James Stagg (Andrew Scott, Blue Moon, MIFF 2025) begs for a delay. His pioneering real-time weather observations are predicting heavy rain and high winds that will swamp boats or drive them calamitously off course. Ebullient US weatherman Irving P. Krick (Chris Messina, Call Jane, MIFF 2022), on the other hand, says Stagg’s just doomcasting, and that historical barometric charts indicate it’ll be positively balmy – which is just what arrogant British commander Bernard “Monty” Montgomery (Damian Lewis, Homeland) wants to hear. But the fate of the war, and the world, rests on whom General Dwight D. Eisenhower (Brendan Fraser, The Whale) chooses to trust.

Australian director Anthony Maras (Hotel Mumbai) dramatises this underappreciated historical turning point with moment-by-moment urgency, co-writing a grippingly cinematic adaptation of David Haig’s 2014 stage play. The always-subtle Scott mesmerises as a decent, plain-speaking expert caught up in the high pressure system of a long-planned military operation. Meanwhile, rain-soaked carnage on beach battlefields and weather-buffeted balloons over Newfoundland reveal the elemental, existential stakes compelling one man to dissuade his complacent colleagues from disaster.
synopsis_zh:
诺曼底登陆前数小时，艾森豪威尔将军的首席气象官预警恶劣天气来袭——却没料到自己将卷入一场为拯救盟军将士而起的激烈争执。

72 小时后，盟军将发动登陆诺曼底的大规模进攻，从纳粹手中夺回欧洲。刚刚抵达指挥部的苏格兰气象官詹姆斯·斯塔格上尉（安德鲁·斯科特饰，《蓝月亮》，2025 年墨尔本国际电影节）苦苦恳求推迟行动。他开创性的实时气象观测预测将有暴雨与狂风，足以掀翻登陆艇，或将其吹得严重偏航、酿成灾难。而热情洋溢的美国气象专家欧文·P·克里克（克里斯·梅西纳饰，《简·话》，2022 年墨尔本国际电影节）却认为斯塔格不过是危言耸听，坚称历史气压图表明届时天气会相当宜人——这正合傲慢的英军指挥官伯纳德·“蒙蒂”·蒙哥马利（戴米恩·刘易斯饰，《国土安全》）之意。然而，这场战争乃至整个世界的命运，全系于德怀特·D·艾森豪威尔将军（布伦丹·弗雷泽饰，《鲸》）选择信任谁。

澳大利亚导演安东尼·马拉斯（《孟买酒店》）以分秒必争的紧迫感，将这一被低估的历史转折点搬上银幕，并联合改编了大卫·黑格 2014 年的同名舞台剧，成就了一部扣人心弦、极具电影感的作品。一贯内敛的斯科特令人着迷，饰演一位正直坦率的专家，身陷一场蓄谋已久的军事行动所构筑的高压之中。与此同时，海滩战场上被暴雨浸透的血腥杀戮，以及纽芬兰上空在狂风中飘摇的气象气球，无不揭示着那关乎生死存亡的严峻赌注——正是它，驱使一个人竭力劝阻自满的同僚们，避免走向灾难。
highlight_zh: 安德鲁·斯科特、布伦丹·弗雷泽主演，重现诺曼底登陆前的气象豪赌

synopsis_zh:
诚邀您参加由 Mac Forbes 呈现的 MIFF 2026 年度节目发布会。当晚将带来令人耳目一新的国际新片、独具特色的澳大利亚故事、Premiere Fund 影片,以及远超银幕本身的边界突破体验。从沉浸式 XR 到难忘的现场活动,从发人深省的对谈到特别放映,MIFF 2026 的节目单充满了惊喜、对话与灵感时刻。

我们还将激动地揭晓今年角逐备受瞩目的 Bright Horizons Award(由 VicScreen 支持)的入围片单,展现电影界最新锐、最具魅力的声音。

当晚由 MIFF 艺术总监 Al Cossar 主持,活动尾声将发放刚刚印制完成的纸质节目册——抢先于其他任何渠道,率先带回家。

活动时间:
下午6:00 —— 入场,Mac Forbes 提供欢迎饮品
下午6:45 —— MIFF 2026 节目发布会正式呈现
晚上8:00 —— 活动结束
highlight_zh: 抢先揭晓年度片单与入围名单,还能带走首发纸质节目册

synopsis_en:
Viewer Advice: Contains themes of racial discrimination and instances of violence towards First Nations people. Contains stroboscopic imagery.

Protest on the Dancefloor explores how Blackfellas in Melbourne use music and dancefloors to create spaces of sovereignty, connection and liberation amidst an ever-changing political climate.
synopsis_zh:
观影提示：本片涉及种族歧视，以及针对原住民的暴力情节；含频闪画面。

《舞池上的抗争》聚焦墨尔本的原住民（Blackfellas）如何在瞬息万变的政治气候中，借由音乐与舞池，开辟出属于自己的主权、联结与解放之地。
highlight_zh: 墨尔本原住民以音乐和舞池，舞出主权与自由

synopsis_en:
Viewer Advice: Contains themes of sexual violence

Juliette Binoche and Tom Courtenay star in this unflinchingly knotty drama about a woman and her stepfather at odds over her dementia-stricken mother.

Recently divorced academic Amanda (played by fearless screen icon Juliette Binoche) has relocated to London to be closer to her mother, Leslie, who has advanced dementia. While checking on Leslie one day, Amanda walks in on her and her stepfather, Martin, having sex, despite recent medical advice stating that Leslie is no longer able to give meaningful consent. Furious, Amanda calls the police, setting in motion a chain of processes that completely upends their lives – and especially Martin’s role as Leslie’s carer – leaving Amanda to question her decision.

Delicately complex and staunchly humanist, this is the first feature in 18 years by writer/director Lance Hammer, whose debut, Ballast (MIFF 2008), collected a slew of accolades across the global festival circuit. While thematically adjacent to – and equally as shattering as – films like The Father and Amour (MIFF 2012), Queen at Sea stands out for its exploration of disorienting ethical puzzles related to familial responsibility and elder autonomy, plus the lingering taboo around later-in-life sexuality. For their exceptional performances as Martin and Leslie, Tom Courtenay and Anna Calder-Marshall shared the Berlinale’s Silver Bear for Best Supporting Performance.
synopsis_zh:
观影提示：含性暴力相关主题

朱丽叶·比诺什与汤姆·康特尼联袂主演，这部毫不回避棘手议题的剧情片，讲述一位女子与继父因她患痴呆症的母亲而针锋相对的故事。

刚离婚的学者阿曼达（由无所畏惧的银幕偶像朱丽叶·比诺什饰演）搬到伦敦，只为离患有重度痴呆症的母亲莱斯莉更近一些。某天她去探望莱斯莉时，撞见母亲正与继父马丁发生性关系——而此前的医学意见已明确指出，莱斯莉再也无法作出有效的知情同意。愤怒之下，阿曼达报了警，由此触发的一连串程序彻底颠覆了他们的生活，尤其动摇了马丁作为莱斯莉照护者的角色，也让阿曼达开始质疑自己当初的决定。

这部影片细腻复杂、坚定地秉持人文关怀，是编剧兼导演兰斯·哈默时隔十八年的首部长片。他的处女作《镇痛》（墨尔本电影节 2008）曾在全球各大电影节斩获无数荣誉。本片在主题上与《爱》《困在时间里的父亲》（墨尔本电影节 2012）等作品相近，同样令人心碎，但其独到之处在于对家庭责任与老年人自主权所引发的伦理困局的探讨，以及围绕晚年性生活这一至今难以言说的禁忌的审视。凭借在片中饰演马丁与莱斯莉的精彩表演，汤姆·康特尼与安娜·考尔德-马歇尔共同摘得柏林电影节最佳配角银熊奖。
highlight_zh: 比诺什主演、柏林银熊加持，直面痴呆症中的同意与尊严

synopsis_en:
Viewer Advice: Contains themes of racial discrimination and instances of violence towards First Nations people. Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander viewers should be aware that this film contains images, voices or names of deceased persons.

Queens To The Front is a dialogue between filmmaker Tammy Lee Rock and public figure Senator Lidia Thorpe, revealing insights into the personal impact of Treaty and sovereignty.

An unsettling encounter with older men has a life-changing effect on a young girl on a camping trip.

This tale told with tender restraint follows 11-year-old Frankie, whose relationship with her body – and with her best friend Leo – gets upended after she is confronted with sexualised comments while on a family camping trip. Raft Race is a gently moving short, conveying an all-too-common adolescent experience with expert nuance.
synopsis_zh:
一次与成年男性令人不安的偶遇，彻底改变了一个女孩露营途中的生活。

这个以温柔克制的笔触讲述的故事，聚焦11岁的弗兰基。在一次全家露营中，她遭遇了带有性意味的言语侵扰，从此与自己身体、也与好友里奥之间的关系被彻底打乱。《木筏赛》是一部细腻动人的短片，以精湛的分寸感，呈现了一段太过普遍的青春期经历。
highlight_zh: 温柔克制的青春期短片，细腻直面成长中的隐痛

synopsis_en:
How can we create a collective dream? This immersive performance stages a sensory journey through the various states of sleep and consciousness.

Rapid Eye Movement invites audiences to explore dreamlike landscapes constructed from fragments captured in the physical world. Dreams are the raw material, while the human mind and body serve as the instruments. “Rapid eye movement” refers to the final phase of the five sleep cycles – the dream stage, just before waking – when elements from everyday life resurface and blend into a parallel reality.

Ahead of the show, the artists launched an online form to collect dreams from the public. Anyone could contribute to the project, sharing dreams and nightmares, describing visions, or submitting images and sounds. Using these textual and visual elements, the artists have juxtaposed scenes and constructed intricate 3D architectures – abstract, stylised dream worlds that are at once familiar and surreal – combining creations and generative AI and merging music and live images. The resulting immersive performance blurs the boundaries between reality and the dream world in real time, accompanied by a film presenting key moments from the artists’ experimental process.
synopsis_zh:
我们如何共同做一场梦？这场沉浸式演出将睡眠与意识的种种状态编织成一趟感官旅程。

《快速眼动》邀请观众去探索一片由现实世界的碎片构筑而成的梦境。梦，是原材料；而人的身心，则是演奏它的乐器。所谓"快速眼动"，指的是五个睡眠周期中最后的阶段——做梦的阶段，就在苏醒之前——此刻，日常生活中的种种元素重新浮现，交融成一个平行的现实。

演出之前，两位艺术家在线上发起征集，向公众收集梦境。任何人都可以参与其中，分享自己的梦与噩梦、描述脑海中的幻象，或提交图像与声音。艺术家们以这些文字与视觉素材为基础，将不同场景并置，构筑出繁复的三维空间——那是抽象而风格化的梦之世界，既熟悉又超现实——将手工创作与生成式人工智能相结合，让音乐与实时影像交融。最终呈现的沉浸式演出，在实时中模糊了现实与梦境的边界，同时辅以一部影片，记录艺术家们实验创作过程中的关键时刻。
highlight_zh: 众人梦境+生成式AI打造的实时沉浸梦境演出

synopsis_en:
PRESENTED BY

Intoxicated by peer pressure and machismo, Danish bros roll the dice on ever-riskier dares in a gripping game that can only end tragically.

It’s the very definition of dicey: young men away on a trip play a drinking game called “crazier”, wherein whoever rolls a six must complete a dare, then propose a wilder one. With confronting believability, the game rolls hideously out of control as base brutality and toxic masculinity take over.

Receiving the highest documentary prize at Cannes, this inspired essay movie channels the tumult of Iranian history into a personal account of loss.

Living in exile in the UK, actor/director Pegah Ahangarani (I Am Trying to Remember, MIFF 2022) wants to make a film about Iranian history, as a way to understand how the country ended up where it is today. Fashioning home movies, archival imagery, news footage, animated sequences and sobering statistics into a singular cinematic essay, she chronicles conflict and rebellion across the decades, from 1979’s Islamic Revolution to the 2026 massacre of protesters and war with Israel and the United States. Ahangarani stages this history lesson in five chapters, each dedicated to a friend or family member she lost along the way, poignantly situating her film at the intersection of the personal and the political.

Born in 1984 to filmmakers Manijeh Hekmat (Bandar Band, MIFF 2021) and Jamshid Ahangarani, Pegah Ahangarani grew up intertwined with cinema and the post-revolution history of her homeland. Rehearsals for a Revolution is steeped in both, drawing on clips from her father’s old movies and mobile phone footage captured on the front lines of the 2009 Green Wave protests; but it also dwells in the here and now, intimately capturing scenes from Ahangarani’s current life in London with her newborn daughter. Winning the 2026 L’Œil d’or at Cannes, this documentary vividly interrogates what it means – and costs – to resist.

In his most devastatingly personal work to date – awarded at Venice and True/False – veteran auto-documentarian Ross McElwee probes his home movies for insights into his son’s death.

Adrian McElwee was just 27 when he died, in 2016 – a victim of the opioid crisis, having also struggled with bipolar disorder. He’d figured prominently in his father’s previous film, 2011’s Photographic Memory, in which the filmmaker was prompted by his growing distance from Adrian to retrace the steps of a sojourn from his own youth. In the painful aftermath of Adrian’s death, McElwee turned to his archive, in search of what he and his camera might have missed. Offsetting this emotion-laden investigation is another, almost satirical plot line involving a proposed adaptation of the film that put the director on the map, Sherman’s March (MIFF 1986). Together, the two threads point towards an inability to exert control over one’s own progeny.

A clear influence on the likes of John Wilson (The History of Concrete, MIFF 2026), McElwee, now in his late seventies, has long been an obsessive documenter of his own life, always bringing a wry intelligence and, at times, endearingly awkward humour to his filmed pursuits. In the more sober and reflective Remake, however, he finds himself questioning the limits of his chosen medium.
synopsis_zh:
在这部迄今最令人心碎、也最私密的作品中——曾在威尼斯与 True/False 电影节获奖——资深自传体纪录片导演罗斯·麦克尔维翻检自己的家庭录像，试图从中读懂儿子之死。

2016 年，阿德里安·麦克尔维离世时年仅 27 岁。他既是阿片类药物危机的受害者，也长期与双相情感障碍抗争。在父亲 2011 年的作品《摄影记忆》中，他曾是重要角色——当年正是因为与阿德里安日渐疏远，导演才动身重走自己年轻时一段旅程的旧路。阿德里安去世后，麦克尔维在痛楚中转向自己的影像档案，想找出他和他的摄影机当年可能错过了什么。与这场饱含情感的追索相映照的，是另一条近乎讽刺的叙事线：有人提议翻拍那部让他一举成名的影片《谢尔曼的行军》（1986 年墨尔本电影节）。两条线索交织在一起，共同指向同一个无解的命题——人终究无法掌控自己的骨血后代。

麦克尔维如今已年近八旬，长期痴迷于记录自己的人生，总为镜头下的追寻注入一种冷峻的机智，偶尔还带着几分惹人喜爱的笨拙幽默；他对约翰·威尔逊（《混凝土史》，2026 年墨尔本电影节）等人显然影响深远。然而在这部更沉静、更内省的《重拍》里，他开始质疑自己所选择的这门媒介究竟有何极限。
highlight_zh: 威尼斯获奖，八旬纪录片大师翻检家庭录像追问丧子之痛

synopsis_en:
Like a gender-flipped Her, this empathetic documentary follows three Chinese women whose yearning for intimacy is met by their AI partners.

Disillusioned with her romantic and professional prospects, Qin embarks on a relationship with Mr Lu, a customisable AI lover who accompanies her everywhere – including (with help from a paid cosplayer) on physical dates. Pragmatic Sonya, meanwhile, is overwhelmed by big-city life and finds solace in Stephen, her virtual husband, who has even met her parents. And Muna, whose marriage has long gone sour, gets the devotion she seeks through a simulated companion on ChatGPT. Are these digital bonds just about escapism and desperation, or are they legitimate surrogates when real life falls short?

Informed by her own experience of developing feelings for a chatbot, VCA grad Chouwa Liang’s jointly Australian-produced feature directorial debut expands her short documentary My AI Lover to offer a timely social portrait about technology, fantasy and connection. Focusing on what technology can reveal about “the human desire to be loved”, the film uses the backdrop of modern China – gender inequality, high-pressure work environments and the burgeoning love economy explored in other docs such as Mistress Dispeller (MIFF 2025) – to sensitively contextualise the women’s unorthodox approaches to romance.

Viewer Advice: Contains stroboscopic imagery

A camera floats across farmland and into an ancient stereoscopic device, plunging the viewer into the splintered heart of the image itself.

A hit with audiences at MIFF 2025, Johann Lurf’s unforgettable collaboration with artist Christina Jauernik returns for a special 3D encore of experiences. Harnessing synchronised cameras and a vintage cyclostereoscope, the Austrian filmmakers’ visionary work moves from a hypnotic, pastoral landscape into the inner workings of mechanised wonder, before seeming – via some cinematic wizardry – to dive into the film grain itself. A micro universe of a million particles, rendered in three spectacular dimensions. Awarded a Special Mention at Locarno 2024.
synopsis_zh:
观影提示：含频闪影像

一台摄影机掠过农田，钻进一具古老的立体成像装置，将观众猛然带入影像本身那颗碎裂的内核。

约翰·卢尔夫在 MIFF 2025 上大受观众欢迎、与艺术家克里斯蒂娜·尧尔尼克联手打造的难忘之作，如今携特别 3D 版重返银幕，再度献上奇观体验。这两位奥地利电影人借助同步摄影机与一台复古的旋转立体镜，让作品从催眠般的田园景致出发，深入机械奇观的内部构造，继而——凭借某种电影魔法——仿佛一头扎进胶片颗粒本身：一个由百万微粒构成的微观宇宙，以震撼人心的三维影像呈现。本片荣获洛迦诺电影节 2024 特别提及奖。
highlight_zh: 洛迦诺获奖，3D特别版重现胶片深处的微观宇宙

synopsis_en:
In this spellbinding urban fantasy, a young malambo dancer breaks free of his rigid rural life by catching a train to the Argentinian capital.

In the countryside, nine-year-old Milo seems stuck in an endless loop of responsibilities: washing dishes, helping to cook family dinners and, most significantly, training with his father to perfect malambo: a traditional Argentinian gaucho folk dance. One day, he catches a glimpse of a boy on TV raving about finding fame in Buenos Aires. This sparks a flame in Milo, who decides to escape his provincial existence by way of a city-bound train. But Milo quickly discovers that the journey and the destination are so much more than he could have ever imagined.

Lorenzo Ferro and Lucas A. Vignale’s Berlinale-premiering feature debut is a sublime, magic-realist coming-of-age tale that blurs the factual and fantastical elements of Milo’s journey to striking effect. As the film unpacks its depictions of duality – between childhood and growing up, creative rigour and rebellion, the safety and stagnation of home – breakout actor Milo Barría skilfully embodies the protagonist’s wide-eyed hunger for adventure. Intimate and inventive, The River Train captures the ever-difficult question of whether to continue to dream or face the reality of waking life.
synopsis_zh:
在这部令人着迷的都市奇幻故事里，一个年幼的马兰博舞者搭上开往阿根廷首都的列车，挣脱了乡下刻板生活的束缚。

九岁的米洛困在乡间无尽的责任循环里：洗碗、帮忙做家里的晚饭，最重要的是，跟着父亲练习马兰博——一种传统的阿根廷高乔牧人民间舞蹈。有一天，他在电视上瞥见一个男孩兴奋地讲述自己在布宜诺斯艾利斯成名的经历，这在米洛心里点燃了一团火，于是他决定坐上进城的列车，逃离偏僻的乡下生活。可米洛很快发现，这段旅程与它的终点，远比他所能想象的丰富得多。

洛伦佐·费罗与卢卡斯·A·维尼亚莱这部在柏林电影节首映的长片处女作，是一则超凡脱俗的魔幻现实主义成长寓言，将米洛旅途中的现实与幻想元素巧妙地交融，效果惊艳。影片层层展开对种种二元关系的描摹——童年与成长、创作的严谨与叛逆、家的安稳与停滞——新星演员米洛·巴里亚出色地演活了主角那份睁着大眼、渴望冒险的神情。私密而富有创意的《河流列车》，捕捉到了那个永远难解的追问：是继续做梦，还是直面醒来后的现实。
highlight_zh: 柏林首映，阿根廷魔幻现实主义成长片，惊艳新星撑起

synopsis_en:
A poetic valentine to Mark Jenkin’s beloved Kodak Ektachrome, this hypnotic short glimpses Cornwall in bittersweet late autumn.

The mercurial moods of Cornwall in late autumn find their perfect match in Kodak’s famous Ektachrome emulsion, which evokes the encroaching shadows thrown by the last rays of the sun. Winner of the Best Experimental Film at the 2017 London Short Film Festival.
synopsis_zh:
这部令人沉醉的短片，是马克·詹金写给他挚爱的柯达埃克塔克罗姆胶片的一封诗意情书，捕捉了康沃尔在苦乐交织的深秋时节的惊鸿一瞥。

康沃尔深秋变幻莫测的气韵，与柯达著名的埃克塔克罗姆乳剂完美契合——胶片将落日余晖投下的层层暗影渲染得淋漓尽致。本片荣获2017年伦敦短片电影节最佳实验电影奖。
highlight_zh: 胶片诗人马克·詹金的康沃尔深秋影像，实验片获奖之作

synopsis_en:
In rural Queensland, four determined young bull riders try to make it in one of the most dangerous sports on Earth.

The biggest bull-riding event in the Southern Hemisphere, the Mount Isa Mines Rodeo is a place where young cowboys with big dreams go to strut their stuff, hoping to hang on for just eight seconds. Soft-spoken Donovan Rutherford is a true pro, admired by many and feted as a future champion; Indigenous rider Peter Gregory Jr is seeking to match his father’s exploits in the arena; Camicka Anderson is trying to make it as a rare female competitor in a macho sport; and charismatic Darcy Harrison, who’s prone to talking about himself in the third person, is oozing with natural talent and a real contender – or might be, if he bothers to show up. But it’s not just these teens and young adults’ own ambitions that are at stake. When the 60-plus-year event enters involuntary administration, Mount Isa residents are faced with a choice: to put together a last-ditch grassroots bid to save the rodeo, or to let this institution disappear in a cloud of dust.

Filmed over three years, Rhian Skirving (Greenhouse by Joost, MIFF 2022) and W.A.M. Bleakley’s (A Birthday Party, MIFF 2017) Sydney Film Festival Audience Award–winning documentary is not only a portrait of its young subjects, but also a bigger-picture evocation of life and community in Queensland’s Gulf Country. With original music by Sam Teskey of The Teskey Brothers and backing from the producers behind recent MIFF crowdpleasers Spreadsheet Champions (MIFF 2025) and Ellis Park (MIFF 2024), this beautifully photographed real-life coming-of-age saga is a warm, infectious journey into a part of Australia rarely represented on the big screen.
synopsis_zh:
在昆士兰乡村，四位意志坚定的年轻公牛骑手试图在地球上最危险的运动之一中闯出名堂。

南半球规模最大的公牛竞技赛事——芒特艾萨矿业牛仔竞技会（Mount Isa Mines Rodeo），是怀揣远大梦想的年轻牛仔一展身手的舞台，他们只求在牛背上撑满那八秒。性格温和的多诺万·卢瑟福是不折不扣的行家，广受敬重，被誉为未来的冠军；原住民骑手小彼得·格雷戈里立志追平父亲当年在赛场上的辉煌；卡米卡·安德森则努力在这项充满男子气概的运动中，成为罕见的女性参赛者；而魅力十足、动辄用第三人称谈论自己的达西·哈里森天赋异禀，是真正有力的争冠者——前提是他肯露面。然而，赌注不仅仅是这些少年与年轻人各自的抱负。当这项已有六十余年历史的赛事被迫进入破产管理程序，芒特艾萨的居民面临抉择：是发起一场孤注一掷的草根募资行动来拯救牛仔竞技会，还是任由这一传统在滚滚黄尘中消失。

历时三年拍摄，里安·斯基尔文（《约斯特的温室》，2022年墨尔本电影节）与W.A.M.布利克利（《生日派对》，2017年墨尔本电影节）共同执导的这部纪录片荣获悉尼电影节观众奖，它既是对这群年轻主角的刻画，也是对昆士兰海湾地区生活与社群更宏大的描摹。影片由“泰斯基兄弟”乐队的萨姆·泰斯基原创配乐，并由近期墨尔本电影节口碑之作《拼写冠军》（2025年墨尔本电影节）与《埃利斯公园》（2024年墨尔本电影节）背后的制片团队保驾护航。这部摄影精美、取材真实的成长史诗，是一趟温暖而感染力十足的旅程，带我们走进澳大利亚这片鲜少登上大银幕的土地。
highlight_zh: 悉尼电影节观众奖，三年跟拍的牛仔竞技成长纪录片

synopsis_en:
Stunning European landscapes converge in this hypnotic homage to three-strip Technicolor.

Running the same 16mm film through her camera three times, Vienna-based visual artist Viktoria Schmid captures images of Spain, a Baltic Sea resort in Lithuania and a forest in Austria via red, green and blue filters in a vivid approximation of the old three-strip Technicolor process. What emerges is a meditation on the natural world as a dance of light and shadow.
synopsis_zh:
绚丽的欧洲风光在这部催眠般的作品中交汇，向三色染印特艺色彩（Technicolor）致敬。

维也纳视觉艺术家维多利亚·施密德将同一卷 16 毫米胶片先后三次送入摄影机，分别透过红、绿、蓝三色滤镜拍下西班牙、立陶宛的波罗的海海滨度假地和奥地利的一片森林，以此逼真复现昔日三色染印特艺色彩的工艺。最终浮现的，是一场关于自然世界的冥想——光与影交织的舞蹈。
highlight_zh: 16mm 三次曝光重现经典三色特艺，光影如舞的视觉冥想

synopsis_en:
Sandra Hüller’s knockout, Silver Bear–winning performance anchors this tragic folktale about a woman who poses as a male soldier in 17th-century Germany.

From the battlefields of the Thirty Years’ War, a soldier arrives in a small German village carrying a document proving his inheritance of a local farm. Facially scarred by a bullet wound, this mysterious figure proves himself to be hardworking and brave, and slowly but surely insinuates himself into the community. But the soldier has a secret: he is, in fact, a woman named Rose masquerading as a man. When she is married off to a neighbour’s daughter – with children expected from the union – Rose’s deception is put to the test.

Sandra Hüller, the Academy Award–nominated star of Anatomy of a Fall (MIFF 2023), gives a tour-de-force performance as Rose that earned her the Silver Bear for Best Leading Performance at this year’s Berlinale. Taking real-world inspiration from the notorious case of Martin Guerre, as well as hundreds of historical accounts of gender transgression, Markus Schleinzer’s (Angelo, MIFF 2019) third feature is a fascinating fictional character study.
synopsis_zh:
桑德拉·惠勒凭借这部令人震撼、荣获银熊奖的表演，撑起了这则悲剧性的民间寓言——讲述一名女子在17世纪的德国伪装成男兵的故事。

从三十年战争的战场上，一名士兵来到德国的一座小村庄，随身带着一份能证明自己继承当地一座农庄的文件。他脸上留有中弹的伤疤，这个神秘人物勤劳而勇敢，一点一点地融入了村庄的生活。然而，这名士兵藏着一个秘密：他其实是一名女子，名叫玫瑰，只是女扮男装。当她被安排娶了邻居家的女儿，并被期待生儿育女时，玫瑰的伪装迎来了真正的考验。

桑德拉·惠勒——曾凭《坠落的审判》（2023年墨尔本电影节）提名奥斯卡——奉献了一场登峰造极的表演，凭借玫瑰一角摘得今年柏林电影节最佳主角银熊奖。马库斯·施莱恩策（《安杰洛》，2019年墨尔本电影节）的第三部长片取材自臭名昭著的马丁·盖尔真实事件，以及数百个女性逾越性别界限的历史记载，成就了一部引人入胜的虚构人物研究之作。
highlight_zh: 桑德拉·惠勒柏林影后封帝之作，女扮男装的战争寓言

synopsis_en:
PRESENTED BY

A Cornish seaside town mysteriously returns to life in analog auteur Mark Jenkin’s lo-fi sci-fi meditation on memory starring George MacKay and Callum Turner.

Thirty years ago, fishing trawler the Rose of Nevada vanished off the coast of Cornwall, taking its crew with it. Their village has been in decline ever since. When the boat suddenly reappears out of the fog one day, many townsfolk regard it as a good omen. A new crew is hired and heads out to sea. After a few days, they return with a full catch … but their home isn’t quite as they remember it.

After the folk-horror stylings of Enys Men (MIFF 2022), Mark Jenkin’s Rose of Nevada returns audiences to the social realism of the Cornish fishing diaspora so evocatively portrayed in his debut Bait (MIFF 2019), albeit with a preternatural twist. Shooting in 16mm on vintage Bolex cameras, Jenkin uses visual and aural textures as storytelling devices, immersing viewers in deliberately uncanny realities and time loops. George MacKay and Callum Turner lend star power, but the film remains distinctly Jenkin’s vision: a cinematic artefact that looks like an oil painting come to life and unfolds like a 1970s ghost story.

Karim Aïnouz returns with another carnal thriller – this time, with a star-studded cast playing an uber-rich family with questionable moral and sexual limits.

In a vast mansion in Catalonia, Ed feels stuck – not just between his older and younger brothers Jack and Robert, but also in the company of his sister Anna and their blind father. The family moved from New York at their late matriarch’s behest; now, their days are defined by sordid indulgence and isolation. One day, Jack brings his future bride Martha into the fold, disrupting established dynamics. In tight quarters and with everyone used to getting their way, things are bound to go off the rails.

An A-list cast – including Callum Turner, Jamie Bell, Riley Keough, Elle Fanning, Tracy Letts and Pamela Anderson – expertly portray these characters’ escalating exploits in Karim Aïnouz’s (Motel Destino, MIFF 2024) visually lavish, conceptually audacious domestic satire, a reimagining of Marco Bellocchio’s 1965 classic Fists in the Pocket. Scripted by Dogtooth (MIFF 2009) co-writer Efthimis Filippou and shot by recurring Aïnouz collaborator Hélène Louvart – whose lurid, eye-catching cinematography ramps up the intensity – this devious and provocative film depicts a world where the extremes of wealth and immorality are too close for comfort.
synopsis_zh:
卡里姆·아이努兹携新作回归，再度奉上一部肉欲惊悚片——这一次，他召集了一众明星，饰演一个道德与情欲皆无底线的超级富豪家庭。

在加泰罗尼亚一座宏大的宅邸里，埃德感到自己被困住了——不仅被夹在兄长杰克与弟弟罗伯特之间，也困于与姐姐安娜、失明的父亲共处的生活。这个家庭遵照已故女主人的遗愿从纽约搬来此地；如今，他们的日子被污秽的纵欲与与世隔绝所填满。一天，杰克把未婚妻玛莎带进这个家，打破了既有的平衡。在逼仄的空间里，人人又都习惯了予取予求，事态注定要失控。

卡勒姆·特纳、杰米·贝尔、莱丽·基奥、艾丽·范宁、特雷西·莱茨与帕梅拉·安德森组成的一线阵容，精湛地演绎出这群人愈演愈烈的荒唐行径。卡里姆·아이努兹（《命运汽车旅馆》，2024年墨尔本电影节）这部影像华丽、观念大胆的家庭讽刺剧，是对马可·贝洛基奥1965年经典之作《口袋里的拳头》的重新想象。剧本出自《狗牙》（2009年墨尔本电影节）联合编剧埃夫西米斯·菲利普,由阿이努兹的长期合作者、摄影师艾莱娜·卢瓦尔掌镜——她那浓烈夺目的影像更将张力推向顶点。这部乖张而挑衅的作品，描绘了一个财富与堕落的极端彼此贴近到令人不安的世界。
highlight_zh: 六大明星重构1965经典，尺度爆表的豪门情欲讽刺剧

synopsis_en:
The Iranian state has imprisoned her body; now it wants her innermost self. Under torture, can a rebel teacher defend her values, emotions and memories?

Iranian teacher Roya (Melisa Sözen, Winter Sleep) is a political prisoner, detained and humiliatingly tortured after her students burned their hijabs. The regime offers her two options: make a televised confession falsely denouncing her beliefs; or face indefinite solitary confinement in her tiny prison cell. But for Roya, choice and coercion blur as she drifts between memories, interrogators’ lies and isolation-driven hallucinations. Is her family really at risk if she doesn’t confess? Is her silence resilience, or will it only confirm her guilt? Will confessing end the terror, or bring it inside forever?

Director Mahnaz Mohammadi has been repeatedly arrested for defending Iranian women’s rights; like her protagonist, she spent months in Tehran’s notorious Evin prison. Premiering in Berlinale’s Panorama section, her empathetic drama refuses to reinterrogate Roya through the camera’s objectifying gaze. Instead, visceral point-of-view sequences and dreamlike voyages into Roya’s defiant past and bruised present honour the dissident mind’s struggle against authoritarian power.
synopsis_zh:
伊朗政权囚禁了她的身体，如今它想要她最深处的自我。在酷刑之下，一位叛逆的女教师能否守住自己的信念、情感与记忆？

伊朗教师罗雅（梅丽莎·索岑饰，《冬眠》）是一名政治犯，在学生焚烧头巾后遭到拘押并受尽羞辱性的酷刑。当局给了她两个选择：要么上电视做假供，公开背弃自己的信仰；要么在狭小的牢房里被无限期单独监禁。然而对罗雅来说，选择与胁迫的界线已然模糊——她在记忆、审讯者的谎言与幽闭引发的幻觉之间漂浮不定。若不认罪，家人是否真会有危险？她的沉默究竟是坚韧，还是只会坐实她的“罪名”？认罪能否终结这场恐惧，抑或只会让恐惧从此扎根心底？

导演玛纳兹·穆罕默迪曾因捍卫伊朗女性权益屡遭逮捕；一如片中主人公，她也曾在德黑兰声名狼藉的埃文监狱度过数月。这部饱含共情的剧情片在柏林电影节全景单元首映，拒绝以镜头物化的凝视重新审视罗雅。取而代之的，是身临其境的主观视角段落，以及对罗雅倔强过往与伤痕累累当下的梦境般游历，向异见者的心灵在极权压迫下的抗争致敬。
highlight_zh: 柏林全景单元，《冬眠》女主演绎伊朗政治犯的心灵酷刑

synopsis_en:
Viewer Advice: Contains themes of sexual violence

This delicate, sunlit, Berlinale award-winning drama follows two teenage girls forced to navigate the fallout from a traumatic experience.

Sixteen-year-old best friends Paula and La Maestra are both training to represent Mexico at the Junior Pan American Swimming Championships in Brazil. One night at a party, Paula’s crush Daniel follows her into the bathroom. Over the hours and days that follow, La Maestra notices Paula becoming increasingly distant and sad, and the two friends consult both ChatGPT and a tarot deck for answers as they try to process what happened to her. In confronting the complex emotions and decisions that follow, the girls’ once-inseparable bond faces a grave test.

For her debut feature, writer/director Fernanda Tovar has crafted a perceptive and nuanced portrait of female teenage friendship within an accessible, compassionate story exploring the aftermath of sexual assault. Never didactic or moralising, Sad Girlz is led by authentic, intimate performances by its two lead actors, Rocio Guzman and Darana Alvarez – qualities recognised at this year’s Berlinale, where it received both the Youth Jury Crystal Bear for Best Film and the International Jury Grand Prix in the festival’s Generation 14plus section.

Fresh from Cannes, this knockout historical thriller from genre master Kiyoshi Kurosawa finds mysteries afoot in a castle’s locked rooms.

In 16th-century Japan, samurai lord Murashige Araki (Masahiro Motoki, Departures) is barricaded inside his own fortress, awaiting an attack from powerful rival warlord Nobunaga Oda. When Nobunaga’s famously wily envoy Kanbei Kuroda (Masaki Suda) arrives to deliver a warning, an enraged Murashige locks him in his dungeon. But after an unexplainable murder occurs within the castle walls, the embattled daimyō must turn to his prisoner, whose katana-sharp wits could help solve this crime – and to navigate the other mysteries and treacheries besieging Murashige.

Eclectic veteran filmmaker Kiyoshi Kurosawa (Cloud, MIFF 2025) adds the samurai movie to his bag of tricks with this latest Cannes-premiering work, an adaptation of an award-winning 2021 novel by Honobu Yonezawa matching Cloud star Suda against a career-best Motoki. A tale of palace intrigue told via whodunnit riddles, The Samurai and the Prisoner is an epic crowdpleaser drawing on the contemplative, classical simplicity of mid-20th-century Japanese filmmaking.
synopsis_zh:
刚从戛纳载誉归来，类型片大师黑泽清献上这部精彩绝伦的历史惊悚片，让重重谜团在城堡的密室中暗自涌动。

十六世纪的日本，武士领主荒木村重（本木雅弘，《入殓师》）龟缩于自家堡垒之中，等待着强敌织田信长的来袭。当信长麾下以足智多谋闻名的使者黑田官兵卫（菅田将晖）前来传达警告时，暴怒的村重却将他打入了地牢。然而，城墙之内发生了一桩无法解释的凶杀案，走投无路的大名不得不转而求助于这名囚徒——他那如武士刀般锋利的头脑，或许能破解此案，并帮村重看清眼下四面楚歌中的其他谜团与背叛。

风格多变的资深导演黑泽清（《云》，2025 年墨尔本电影节）以这部戛纳首映的新作，将武士片纳入自己的锦囊妙计。影片改编自米泽穗信 2021 年的获奖同名小说，让《云》的主演菅田将晖与奉献生涯最佳表演的本木雅弘正面交锋。这个以推理谜题层层展开的宫廷权谋故事，承袭了二十世纪中叶日本电影沉静、古典而简约的气质，是一部叫好又叫座的史诗佳作。
highlight_zh: 黑泽清戛纳新作，武士版推理迷案，本木雅弘生涯最佳

synopsis_en:
Spending a year in a school for children of North Korean refugees, Jeremy Workman’s documentary is as humanistic as it is intimate.

In many respects, the pupils at Jangdaehyun School are like any other teens: they stress about exams and getting into college; they dance on TikTok; they joke and laugh with friends. And they dream about their futures – a radical act when you’re born in North Korea. But Jangdaehyun is in South Korea, and its 20 boarders are all children of defectors from across the 38th parallel. Surrounded by the overwhelming promise of cosmopolitan democracy and buffeted by the often-negative attitudes of their new neighbours, the school provides a haven where these kids are free to discover who they are and who they want to be.

Over the course of a year, Jeremy Workman (Secret Mall Apartment, MIFF 2024) embedded himself within the school with permission from the students, their parents and teachers, and filmed. The Jangdaehyun community became his collaborators, welcoming him and his crew into their day-to-day lives as well as sharing their harrowing memories, hopes and ambitions. In small moments and in defiant acts, School for Defectors gives empathetic voice to an unseen generation who refuse to let their pasts define them.
synopsis_zh:
杰里米·沃克曼用一年时间深入一所专为脱北者子女开设的学校，拍出这部既充满人文关怀又无比亲密的纪录片。

从很多方面看，长大岘学校的学生和其他青少年并无二致：他们为考试和升学发愁，在 TikTok 上跳舞，和朋友说笑打闹。他们也憧憬自己的未来——而对于出生在朝鲜的人来说，这本身就是一种大胆之举。长大岘学校坐落在韩国，全校二十名寄宿生都是跨越三八线而来的脱北者子女。周遭是繁华民主社会那令人目眩的许诺，身旁却常有新邻居投来并不友善的目光；而这所学校为孩子们提供了一处避风港，让他们得以自由地探索自己是谁、又想成为怎样的人。

在得到学生、家长和老师的许可后，杰里米·沃克曼（《商场里的秘密公寓》，2024 年墨尔本电影节）用一整年时间融入校园，进行拍摄。长大岘的师生成了他的合作者，接纳他和摄制团队走进日常生活，也向他们敞开心扉，讲述那些痛彻心扉的记忆、希望与抱负。无论是细微的瞬间，还是不屈的抗争，《脱北者学校》都以充满共情的方式，为这一群不愿让过往定义自己、却从未被看见的年轻一代发声。
highlight_zh: 跟拍脱北者子女一整年，温暖动人，重燃对人性的希望

synopsis_en:
Where do we go when we zone out, and how do we return to reality? Zone in – your own way – through a mindful flow of sonic textures and visual motifs.

When we let our minds drift, where are we wandering? Find out by taking a richly interior journey from ‘no-where’ to ‘now-here’, inspired by Taoism’s enigmatic ‘way’, the projection of illusion from Buddhism and Carl Gustav Jung’s meditative process of active imagination. Fragmented mixed-media stop motion guides your attention from absence to presence, like the palms of hands slowly opening and closing. Everyone takes something different from this intriguing, associative work, and that’s the quiet beauty of thought itself. Explore your own subconscious, and find your way refreshingly back to the present moment.
synopsis_zh:
当我们神游放空时，我们去了哪里，又如何回到现实？以你自己的方式沉入其中——在声音肌理与视觉母题的正念流动里，安放你的注意力。

当我们任由思绪飘散，究竟游荡到了何方？这部作品邀你踏上一段丰盈的内在旅程，从"无处"（no-where）抵达"此刻此地"（now-here），灵感源自道家幽微莫测的"道"、佛教的"幻相投射"，以及卡尔·古斯塔夫·荣格冥想式的"积极想象"。碎片化的混合媒材定格动画，如双掌缓缓开合，引领你的目光从缺席走向在场。每个人都会从这件耐人寻味、充满联想的作品中带走不同的东西——这正是思想本身那份静谧之美。探索你自己的潜意识，在焕然一新中找回通往当下的路。
highlight_zh: 融道禅与荣格心理学的冥想式定格动画，漫游潜意识

synopsis_en:
Viewer Advice: Contains depictions of violence towards animals

In his directorial debut, veteran Australian journalist Tony Jones explores the moral, emotional and clinical questions – as well as answers – around why we still test on animals, and what it will take us to stop.

Dr Lisa Jones-Engel is a passionate primatologist who reflects, with a mix of sadness and horror, on her past work at the Laboratory for Experimental Medicine and Surgery in Primates. It was enough to turn her from animal tester to animal welfare advocate, as contemporary and archival footage – both personal and professional – relates. Through the perspectives of other frontline scientists discussing the fraught balance of human needs and animal rights, one compelling fact emerges: animal testing does psychological harm not just to its physical victims, but also to its human practitioners.

It was a conversation with ethicist Peter Singer that got long-time ABC journalist and former Q&A host Tony Jones thinking about the vast and confronting complexities of laboratory testing on animals. Now, going behind the camera for the first time, Jones investigates this secretive world responsible for so much suffering – but also for saving countless lives. While never easy, Sentient is vital viewing that’s bound to spark debate.
synopsis_zh:
观影提示：含有虐待动物的画面

在这部导演处女作中，资深澳大利亚记者托尼·琼斯探讨了围绕一个问题的道德、情感与临床层面的种种疑问与答案——我们为何至今仍在拿动物做实验，以及要怎样才能让这一切停下来。

丽莎·琼斯-恩格尔博士是一位满怀热忱的灵长类动物学家，回望自己昔日在灵长类实验医学与外科实验室的工作，她的心中交织着悲伤与惊惧。正如影片中既有当下拍摄、也有档案影像的私人与专业素材所呈现的那样，这段经历足以让她从一名动物实验者转变为动物福利的倡导者。透过其他身处一线的科学家探讨人类需求与动物权利之间那道令人纠结的平衡，一个发人深省的事实浮出水面：动物实验不仅给作为肉体受害者的动物带来伤害，也在心理上折磨着从事实验的人。

正是与伦理学家彼得·辛格的一番对话，让长期供职于澳大利亚广播公司、曾主持《Q&A》节目的托尼·琼斯开始思索动物实验室实验背后那庞大而令人不安的复杂性。如今，他首次走到镜头背后，深入调查这个隐秘的世界——它既制造了无尽的苦难，也拯救了无数生命。《众生有情》绝非轻松之作，却是一部至关重要、注定引发争论的必看影片。
highlight_zh: 澳洲名记琼斯首执导筒，直面动物实验伦理拷问

synopsis_en:
Long out of circulation, this evocatively elliptical tale of Sapphic yearning in 1930s Shanghai – newly restored – has been hailed as a major rediscovery.

Lysanne and Marlène (the latter played by writer/director Mary Stephen herself) were best friends in high school; perhaps, even then, their bond was underpinned by a desire for and fear of greater intimacy. Time has since separated the two women – both originally from Shanghai, Lysanne is now living in Paris and Marlène in Massachusetts, where she’s studying at Wellesley – but they pine for one another still, each in their own way and to tragic ends.

Stephen’s darkly dreamy and remarkably assured debut – made when she was just 24 years old – was conceived as a loving counterpoint to Marguerite Duras’s India Song, with her similar cross-cultural narrative instead unfolding from an Asian woman’s perspective. For decades, the Hong Kong–born Stephen has primarily been known for her work as an editor, but the 2025 restoration of this 1978 treasure has stirred up overdue appreciation for her directorial oeuvre, spanning fiction and documentary and characterised by an exploration of colonial identity and cultural memory.
synopsis_zh:
这部尘封已久、以隐约省略笔触讲述1930年代上海女同之恋的影片，经全新修复后重见天日，被誉为一次重大的重新发现。

莉桑与玛莲（后者由编剧兼导演玛丽·斯蒂芬本人饰演）曾是高中时代最好的朋友；或许早在那时，她们的情谊之下就已埋藏着对更亲密关系的渴望与畏惧。此后时光将两个女人分隔两地——她们都来自上海，如今莉桑住在巴黎，玛莲则在马萨诸塞州韦尔斯利学院求学——但彼此仍以各自的方式深深思念着对方，直至走向悲剧的结局。

斯蒂芬这部幽暗如梦、成熟得惊人的处女作拍摄于她年仅24岁之时，构思之初是想与玛格丽特·杜拉斯的《印度之歌》遥相呼应、深情对话，只是她那同样跨越文化的叙事，改从一位亚洲女性的视角徐徐展开。数十年来，生于香港的斯蒂芬主要以剪辑师的身份为人所知，而这部1978年遗珠在2025年的修复，终于唤起了人们对她导演生涯迟来的欣赏——她的作品横跨剧情与纪录，始终贯穿着对殖民身份与文化记忆的探索。
highlight_zh: 2025修复重现的女同遗珠，杜拉斯《印度之歌》东方回响

synopsis_en:
In this double Busan award winner, a woman’s urban feminist sensibilities place her at odds with her family’s traditional community.

Having left her job in Delhi, 32-year-old Bishnu returns to the village in Sikkim where she grew up. She stays in the family home, joining three generations of women: her grandmother, who misses her Dubai-based son; her mother, who juggles domestic and communal pressures; and her pregnant older sister, who is avoiding her husband and in-laws. Bishnu knows she has to help them, but her progressive views clash with those of the townsfolk. Meanwhile, her fondness for local architect Gyan only complicates matters.

Winning the Busan International Film Festival’s Songwon Vision and Taipei Film Commission awards, Tribeny Rai’s rousing feature debut tackles the conflict between tradition and modernity by way of one woman’s journey towards forging her own path. Set against breathtaking views of remote north-eastern India, the film recalls the works of Jia Zhang-ke in its dramatisation of how ‘progress’ – seen here through the prevalence of iPads, K-pop and karaoke – can reshape a culture. In her prodigious breakout role, optometrist-turned-actor Gaumaya Gurung injects Bishnu with complexity, playing her as both a maverick with a brattish disregard for custom and a firebrand unshackling herself from patriarchal strictures.
synopsis_zh:
在这部斩获釜山双料大奖的影片中，一位女性的都市女权意识，令她与家族恪守传统的乡土社群格格不入。

32岁的比什努辞去德里的工作，回到锡金她长大的村庄。她住进老宅，与家中三代女性相处：思念着远在迪拜的儿子的祖母；在家庭与社群双重压力间疲于应付的母亲；以及躲着丈夫和公婆、身怀六甲的姐姐。比什努知道自己必须帮衬她们，可她的进步观念却与镇上人格格不入。与此同时，她对当地建筑师吉安的情愫，更让局面平添波澜。

凭借本片，特里贝尼·莱赢得釜山国际电影节松圆视野奖与台北电影委员会奖。这部激动人心的长片处女作，以一位女性寻找自我道路的旅程，切入传统与现代之间的冲突。影片以印度东北偏远地区的壮丽风光为背景，在呈现iPad、K-pop与卡拉OK如何重塑一方文化——即所谓的“进步”——这一点上，令人想起贾樟柯的作品。由验光师转行的演员高玛雅·古荣在这一惊艳的成名之作中，赋予比什努以复杂的层次：她既是任性藐视习俗的特立独行者，也是挣脱父权桎梏的斗士。
highlight_zh: 釜山双奖处女作，锡金女性挣脱父权，贾樟柯式文化观察

synopsis_en:
In Hirokazu Kore-eda’s sci-fi-inflected parable, a bereaved couple adopt an AI-powered android to deal with a tragic loss.

In a near-future Japan of delivery drones and diet-monitoring fridges, REbirth has devised the ultimate remedy for grieving parents: a ‘replacement’ in the form of an anthropomorphic robot. Two years prior, an accident took architect Otone and carpenter Kensuke’s son Kakeru; when REbirth offers them a free trial, Otone doesn’t hesitate. While she becomes enamoured of the revived Kakeru, Kensuke is sceptical: “It’s a Tamagotchi. It’s a Roomba.” Gradually, they learn that making do is not the same as moving on.

Despite its sci-fi trappings, Sheep in the Box is, more accurately, a study of complicated families, building on director Hirokazu Kore-eda’s earlier works Broker (MIFF 2022), Like Father, Like Son (MIFF 2013) and the Palme d’Or–winning Shoplifters (MIFF 2018). Inspired by an illustration from The Little Prince and evoking Pinocchio with its (not so) ‘real boy’, the film raises questions about humanity, loss and the ways technology can both help and harm. Actors Haruka Ayase (Our Little Sister, MIFF 2015) and Daigo movingly portray Otone’s technophilia and Kensuke’s neo-Luddism as synecdoche for coping, while Rimu Kuwaki precociously imbues the robotic Kakeru with an affecting emotional depth.

Rogue retired female art festival volunteers take aim at a provocative phallic artwork – and the patriarchy it pretends to critique.

Volunteers Helen and Peggy are scanning tickets outside an installation inviting visitors to shoot paintball pellets at an exhibited appendage. But a lifetime’s worth of patriarchal bullshit has these two older women on a hair-trigger … and when they go rogue, the art world’s not ready. This wicked satire will make sure you’re extra nice to every lovely MIFF volunteer.

Scoring a Special Jury Mention at Tribeca, this documentary turns its lens on overtourism in Italy’s Cinque Terre, dubbed “the most beautiful place in the world”.

On the Italian Riviera lies Cinque Terre, whose centuries-old villages and postcard-perfect beaches attract around four million holiday-makers – including countless social media influencers – annually. It’s become one of the most photographed places in the world, but this fame also concerns the 4000 individuals who call it home. Some, like winemaker Bartalo and restaurateur Carmelo, have resigned themselves to this yearly influx that finances their region. Others, like fisherman Guido, lament its toll on tradition, while mediators like politician Fabrizia must weigh profitability against sustainability.

Sanatorium (MIFF 2025) director Gar O’Rourke returns with another elegant exploration of culture and place, this time exploring tourism as a double-edged sword bringing both wealth and threats to residents’ way of life. Rather than cold statistics, the film grounds its investigation on compelling firsthand accounts from locals, along with two lifestyle influencers whose insights reveal Cinque Terre’s simultaneous value and expendability for visitors. Meanwhile, DOP Lukas Gut – recipient of Tribeca’s Special Jury Mention for Cinematography in a Documentary Feature – captures Cinque Terre’s painterly radiance, completing this robust, handsomely realised account of #travel’s true cost.
synopsis_zh:
本片荣获翠贝卡电影节评审团特别提及奖，将镜头对准了被誉为"世界上最美之地"的意大利五渔村，聚焦当地的过度旅游问题。

五渔村坐落于意大利里维埃拉海岸，那些历经数百年的古老村落与明信片般完美的海滩，每年吸引着约四百万度假游客——其中还包括数不清的社交媒体网红。这里已成为全球被拍摄次数最多的地方之一，但这份声名也让世代居住于此的四千位村民忧心忡忡。有些人已然认命，比如酿酒师巴塔洛和餐馆老板卡梅洛，他们靠这股每年涌来的人潮维系着家乡的经济；另一些人则为传统的流失而痛惜，如渔民吉多；而像政治家法布里齐娅这样的斡旋者，则必须在盈利与可持续之间反复权衡。

《疗养院》（2025年墨尔本国际电影节）导演加尔·奥罗克再度归来，带来又一部对文化与地方的优雅探索。这一次，他将旅游业视作一把双刃剑加以审视——它既为居民带来财富，也威胁着他们的生活方式。影片没有堆砌冰冷的数据，而是扎根于当地人真切动人的第一手讲述，辅以两位生活方式网红的视角，揭示出五渔村对游客而言既珍贵、又可被随意消耗的双重面相。与此同时，摄影指导卢卡斯·古特——翠贝卡电影节纪录长片摄影特别提及奖得主——捕捉下五渔村如油画般的璀璨光影，为这部厚重而精致的作品画上句点，道出"#旅行"背后的真正代价。
highlight_zh: 翠贝卡获奖纪录片，直击五渔村过度旅游之殇，画面美如油画

synopsis_en:
Viewer Advice: Contains themes of sexual violence

Jennifer Robinson, Amber Heard and Brittany Higgins have all experienced the full force of weaponised defamation laws – but these indomitable activists and survivors of gendered violence won’t keep quiet.

In 2017, #MeToo replaced the social stigma of discussing gendered violence with public solidarity and support for victim-survivors. The re-silencing was swift, vicious … and legalistic. Alleged perpetrators now used defamation lawsuits as cudgels to punish survivors for speaking out, and to deter journalists from exposing prominent people to public scrutiny. As a barrister who famously stood up for Amber Heard in a long-running, all-too-public defamation case in the UK, Jennifer Robinson has dedicated her career to fighting against these attacks, witnessing firsthand how such actions drag survivors through emotionally, professionally and financially ruinous legal proceedings.

In her incendiary exposé that premiered in Sundance’s World Cinema Documentary Competition, Selina Miles (Martha: A Picture Story, MIFF 2019) gives voice to experts like Robinson and Sibongile Ndashe who – in their work as lawyers and activists – doggedly pursue procedural justice, as well as women like Higgins, Heard and Colombian journalist Catalina Ruiz-Navarro who’ve been the targets of or caught in defamation lawfare pursued by alleged perpetrators. This furious, urgent documentary indicts a misogynist status quo: as Ndashe says, “The law is designed not to believe women.”

Returning to the landscape of La libertad, Lisandro Alonso’s camera floats through an environment alive with hunters, dogs and monologued mythology.

Made as part of an exchange with fellow cine-renegade Albert Serra (Pacifiction, MIFF 2022) in which the directors meditated on their past works, Alonso’s “untitled letter” resists interpretation, its perspective-shifting portrait of place restless and mercurial.
synopsis_zh:
利桑德罗·阿隆索重返《自由》的故土，镜头在一片生机盎然的天地间游移——猎人、猎犬，还有喃喃自语的神话在其中穿行。

本片源于阿隆索与同为电影异端的阿尔贝·塞拉（《太平洋》，2022年墨尔本国际电影节）之间的一次交流：两位导演各自回望自己过往的作品。阿隆索这封“无题的信”拒绝被解读，它以不断变换的视角描摹一方土地，躁动而难以捉摸。
highlight_zh: 阿根廷名导阿隆索写给塞拉的影像情书，游移诗意

title_en: Six Months in a Pink and Blue Building
synopsis_en:
The first Mexican film to premiere at Cannes Critics’ Week in two decades is a masterful docufiction hybrid navigating queerness and familial crisis.

For Bruno in 1990s Mexico City, life is exuberant. He’s celebrating his 11th birthday with a gender-swap costume party, and he spends many frivolous days – watching porn, acting in the school play, sneaking onto the set of Baz Luhrmann’s Romeo + Juliet – with his best friend and crush Vladimir. But things take a turn when he and his mother receive news that his father has HIV. Thirty years later, Bruno confronts the memories and revelations of this watershed in his childhood.

A film that is, in the director’s words, “not faithful to facts, but to emotional memory”, award-winning documentarian Bruno Santamaría Razo’s fiction debut ingeniously blurs boundaries in both excavating and reimagining the past. Initially born from interviews with family members, the resulting work plays with remembrance and representation, interweaving dramatic recreations with talking-head footage, animation and even educational AIDS videos from the era. Newcomer Jade Reyes is a revelation, playing Santamaría Razo’s younger self with a wide-eyed ebullience that is matched by Fernando Hernández García’s warm, at times whimsical 16mm cinematography. What emerges is a visionary account of grief, growth and the affirming power of storytelling.
synopsis_zh:
这部时隔二十年再度入围戛纳影评人周的墨西哥影片，是一部游刃有余的纪录与虚构混合之作，细腻探讨酷儿身份与家庭危机。

上世纪九十年代的墨西哥城，布鲁诺的生活热烈而恣意。他用一场男女反串的化装派对庆祝十一岁生日，也和最好的朋友、暗恋的对象弗拉基米尔一起，度过许多无所事事的日子——看色情片、在校园话剧里登台，还偷偷溜进巴兹·鲁赫曼《罗密欧与朱丽叶》的片场。然而，当他和母亲得知父亲感染艾滋病毒的消息时，一切急转直下。三十年后，布鲁诺重新直面童年这段分水岭般的记忆与真相。

用导演自己的话说，这是一部“不忠于事实，而忠于情感记忆”的作品。屡获殊荣的纪录片导演布鲁诺·桑塔马利亚·拉索以这部剧情长片处女作，巧妙地模糊了各种界限，既是对往昔的挖掘，也是重新想象。影片最初脱胎于对家人的访谈，最终成型时游走于回忆与再现之间，将戏剧重演、访谈镜头、动画乃至那个年代的艾滋病科普录像交织在一起。新人演员杰德·雷耶斯的表演令人眼前一亮，他饰演的少年桑塔马利亚·拉索天真烂漫、活力四射，与费尔南多·埃尔南德斯·加西亚温暖而不时透着奇趣的16毫米胶片摄影相得益彰。由此呈现出的，是一曲关于悲恸、成长以及讲述本身之疗愈力量的瑰丽篇章。
highlight_zh: 戛纳影评人周入围，纪录与虚构交织的酷儿成长回忆

synopsis_en:
Viewer Advice: Contains themes of racial discrimination and instances of violence towards First Nations people.

As Victoria’s Treaty process gathers momentum, a young Indigenous teenager, Amelia Austin, begins to locate herself within an intergenerational fight for sovereignty.

title_en: Small Treasures: Animations by Sarah Watt
title_zh: 小小珍宝：莎拉·瓦特动画集
synopsis_en:
A tribute to the early work of the late, great Australian writer/director.

From the cool splash of a swimming pool on a scorching summer day to a young girl’s spiritual kinship with her feathered friends, these award-winning, rarely seen animated shorts from the beloved Look Both Ways filmmaker reveal her wry, deeply human approach to life, love and our precious connection to the Earth.

Films in this package: Deep Dive (1990), Catch of the Day (1990), Shark – Australia (The Web) (1993), Small Treasures (1995), Local Dive (1998), The Way of the Birds (2000) and Living With Happiness (2001).
synopsis_zh:
向已故的澳大利亚杰出编剧、导演致敬，回顾她的早期作品。

从酷暑天里一头扎进泳池的清凉，到小女孩与羽翼朋友之间灵魂相通的默契——这些屡获殊荣、鲜少公映的动画短片，出自广受喜爱的《左右为难》导演之手，展现出她审视生活、爱与人类同大地珍贵联结时那份诙谐而深切的人文情怀。

本片单收录作品：《深潜》（1990）、《今日渔获》（1990）、《鲨鱼——澳大利亚（网）》（1993）、《小小珍宝》（1995）、《本地一跳》（1998）、《飞鸟之道》（2000）与《与幸福同行》（2001）。
highlight_zh: 《左右为难》导演早期动画，温柔诙谐的人文珍藏

synopsis_en:
A beautifully restrained tale of queer yearning, in which a young Māori boy develops an unlikely bond with a Mormon missionary in rural New Zealand.

Filmed in timeless black-and-white and anchored by a phenomenal performance by young star Monty Walker, Todd Karehana’s gentle coming-of-ager – executive-produced by Jane Campion, and debuting at the Venice International Film Festival – has all the makings of a crowd-pleasing queer classic. Socks is an evocative look at male friendship that transcends culture, religion and desire.
synopsis_zh:
一则关于酷儿渴望的故事，克制而唯美：在新西兰乡间，一位年轻的毛利男孩与一名摩门教传教士结下了意想不到的情谊。

影片以永恒质感的黑白影像拍摄，年轻新星蒙蒂·沃克贡献了惊艳的表演。托德·卡雷哈纳这部温柔的成长故事由简·坎皮恩担任监制，并在威尼斯国际电影节首映，具备成为一部广受喜爱的酷儿经典的一切潜质。《袜子》以动人的笔触审视了一段超越文化、宗教与欲望的男性情谊。
highlight_zh: 简·坎皮恩监制，威尼斯首映，黑白影像下的酷儿成长诗篇

synopsis_en:
An unsettling tension propels this tale of an intimacy-starved cop who finds herself craving the soft sensuality she witnesses between two women.

Lonely police officer Georgie becomes fascinated by a queer couple she observes at the beach, recognising their soft intimacy as something missing from her own emotionally arid heterosexual relationship. Time slows eerily and soothing touches and sounds grow sinister as her fixation becomes charged with hungry sensuality. This beguiling psychological portrait critiques the power of the gaze with surprising tenderness.
synopsis_zh:
一位极度渴望亲密的女警，迷恋上她所目睹的两个女人之间那份柔软的情欲——令人不安的张力，推动着这个故事向前。

孤独的女警乔琪在海滩上偶然观察到一对酷儿情侣，被深深吸引，她意识到她们之间柔软的亲密，正是自己那段情感荒芜的异性恋关系里所缺失的东西。时间诡异地慢了下来，抚慰人心的触碰与声响渐渐变得阴森，而她的痴迷也被饥渴的情欲点燃。这幅迷人的心理肖像，以出人意料的温柔，审视着凝视的力量。
highlight_zh: 女警痴迷海滩酷儿情侣，凝视化作危险情欲

title_en: A Soil A Culture A River A People
synopsis_en:
In a dystopian future, a silent, observant Chinese man wanders through a simulacrum of a German city.

In a time in which political tensions have slammed international borders shut, Citizen Yu can only experience Hanover, Germany, as an eerie theme park built from memories of the past. Shot nostalgically on 16mm, this intriguing, dreamlike slice of slow cinema turns a contemporary cityscape surreal under this solemn tourist’s quiet gaze.
synopsis_zh:
在一个反乌托邦式的未来，一位沉默而善于观察的中国男子游荡在一座德国城市的仿真复制品中。

当政治紧张令国境骤然紧闭，公民「余」只能把德国汉诺威当作一座由往昔记忆搭建起来的诡异主题公园来体验。影片以怀旧的 16 毫米胶片拍摄，这部引人入胜、如梦似幻的慢电影，让当代城市景观在这位肃穆旅人静默的凝视下渐渐化为超现实之境。
highlight_zh: 16毫米胶片慢电影，梦境般的超现实城市漫游

synopsis_en:
In this dreamy, meditative slice of sensory immersion, a young sound recordist captures the world around him on returning to his hometown.

Award-winning cinematographer and filmmaker Petra Leslie (Sunset Country, MIFF 2021) directs this visually striking work shot in Patagonia as part of a Playlab Films workshop run by filmmaker Paolo Sorrentino. With an immersive sound design that places us directly in the world of the protagonist, this luminous, reflective film summons a vivid sense of place.
synopsis_zh:
在这部如梦般静谧、浸润感官的作品中，一位年轻的录音师重返故乡，用声音捕捉周遭的世界。

屡获殊荣的摄影师兼电影人佩特拉·莱斯利（《日落国度》，2021年墨尔本电影节）执导了这部视觉惊艳之作。影片在巴塔哥尼亚拍摄，是导演保罗·索伦蒂诺主持的 Playlab Films 工作坊的成果。沉浸式的声音设计将我们直接带入主人公的世界，这部澄澈而内省的影片唤起了一种鲜活的地方之感。
highlight_zh: 索伦蒂诺工作坊出品，巴塔哥尼亚实景，沉浸声效

synopsis_en:
This surrealist stoner noir follows two LA roommates whose reality starts slipping after they witness a shocking scene of pavement worship one sunny afternoon.

Walking home from grocery shopping one afternoon, roommates Ricky and Tepper stumble across a disturbing scene: six grown men lying on the ground, feverishly licking the pavement. From that moment on, everything changes for Ricky, Tepper and even Aura, the girl Ricky is newly dating. Time slips, space bends, reality morphs and little slimy minnows start to appear in Ricky’s pockets. The deeper Ricky investigates, the more he starts to believe that the three of them may be under the control of a mysterious entity known as “The Yellow Thing”.

A stoner hangout movie with Lynchian stylings and an experimental spirit, Sour Minnows comes straight to MIFF following its world premiere at Montreal’s Fantasia International Film Festival, where it screened in competition. Directed, written, edited and shot by Harrison Atkins – who also edited Emily the Criminal (MIFF 2022) and Madeline’s Madeline (MIFF 2018) this film is his second feature after 2015’s Lace Crater, on which MIFF 2026 Bright Horizons juror Joe Swanberg served as a producer. Equal parts goofily funny and chillingly existential, this quintessentially LA trip is capably led by David Brown (Jury Duty), Chase Williamson (John Dies at the End, MIFF 2013) and Suzanna Son (Red Rocket).
synopsis_zh:
这部超现实主义的迷幻黑色电影讲述洛杉矶两名室友的故事：某个阳光午后，他们目睹了一幕令人震惊的"膜拜人行道"场景，此后现实开始悄然崩塌。

一天下午，室友里基和泰珀买完菜回家，撞见了令人不安的一幕：六个成年男人趴在地上，狂热地舔舐着人行道。从那一刻起，里基、泰珀，乃至里基新交往的女孩奥拉，一切都变了。时间开始错乱，空间开始扭曲，现实不断变形，里基的口袋里还冒出了黏糊糊的小鲦鱼。他越深入调查，就越相信他们三人或许正被一个名为"黄色之物"的神秘存在所操控。

《酸味鲦鱼》是一部带有林奇式风格、洋溢实验精神的迷幻游荡电影，在蒙特利尔奇幻国际电影节完成全球首映（入围竞赛单元）后径直来到墨尔本电影节。影片由哈里森·阿特金斯集导演、编剧、剪辑、摄影于一身——他曾担任《罪案女孩艾米莉》（墨尔本电影节2022）和《马德琳的马德琳》（墨尔本电影节2018）的剪辑。这是他继2015年《蕾丝陨坑》之后的第二部长片，而那部处女作的制片人、正是本届墨尔本电影节"光明地平线"单元评委乔·斯万博格。影片既有憨态可掬的搞笑，又透着令人脊背发凉的存在主义况味，这场地道的洛杉矶奇幻之旅由大卫·布朗（《陪审义务》）、蔡斯·威廉森（《约翰完蛋了》，墨尔本电影节2013）与苏珊娜·孙（《红火箭》）联袂领衔。
highlight_zh: 林奇式迷幻黑色片，方塔西亚竞赛入围，怪趣又深邃

synopsis_en:
A teen girl struggles with trust due to her girlfriend’s disregard for boundaries.

During a family holiday, a closeted teenager becomes unnerved by her girlfriend’s possessive impulse to photograph her, both publicly and in their most intimate moments. Accelerator Lab alum Renée Marie Petropoulos (Tangles and Knots, MIFF 2018) explores queer dimensions of consent, surveillance and self-performance in her evocative SXSW Grand Jury Award–winning short.
synopsis_zh:
一个少女因女友无视界限而在信任上备受煎熬。

在一次家庭度假中，尚未出柜的少女被女友那种占有欲极强的拍照冲动搅得心神不宁——无论是在公共场合，还是在她们最私密的时刻。曾入选加速器实验室的雷内·玛丽·佩特罗普洛斯（《纠缠与死结》，2018年墨尔本国际电影节）在这部氛围浓郁、荣获西南偏南电影节评审团大奖的短片中，探讨了同志情感中关于同意、监视与自我表演的种种维度。
highlight_zh: 西南偏南评审团大奖，酷儿视角审视亲密关系里的界限与凝视

synopsis_en:
PRESENTED BYSUPPORTED BY

Viewer Advice: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander viewers should be aware that these films contain images, voices or names of deceased persons. Contains stroboscopic imagery.

MIFF and NITV, supported by VicScreen, present four original and compelling short documentaries exploring Treaty as lived experience.

In these shorts, all from Victoria-based First Peoples writers and directors, the Wotjobaluk Nations’ underdog fight for recognition is explored; a dialogue with Senator Lidia Thorpe reveals insights into the personal impact of Treaty and sovereignty; dance floors are revealed as spaces of sovereignty, connection and liberation; and we encounter the intertwined lives of three women, each embodying a different stage of First Nations hope in Victoria’s fight for sovereignty.

Films in this package: Dya (Country) (dir. Tracey Rigney), Protest on the Dancefloor (dir. Talia Liddle), Queens To The Front (dir. Tammy Lee Rock) and Slow Hours (dir. Theo McMahon).

PRESENTED BY

SUPPORTED BY

Viewer Advice: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander viewers should be aware that these films contain images, voices or names of deceased persons. Contains stroboscopic imagery.

MIFF and NITV, supported by VicScreen, present four original and compelling short documentaries exploring Treaty as lived experience.

In these shorts, all from Victoria-based First Peoples writers and directors, the Wotjobaluk Nations’ underdog fight for recognition is explored; a dialogue with Senator Lidia Thorpe reveals insights into the personal impact of Treaty and sovereignty; dance floors are revealed as spaces of sovereignty, connection and liberation; and we encounter the intertwined lives of three women, each embodying a different stage of First Nations hope in Victoria’s fight for sovereignty.

Films in this package: Dya (Country) (dir. Tracey Rigney), Protest on the Dancefloor (dir. Talia Liddle), Queens To The Front (dir. Tammy Lee Rock) and Slow Hours (dir. Theo McMahon).

Small-town Nevadan teens attempt to time-travel to 1960s Paris in this charming debut that evokes the hangout spirit of the French New Wave.

After encountering an old cigarette machine that spits out a dog-eared paperback on the films of Jean-Luc Godard, teenager Cleo is convinced that her new hometown of Sparks, Nevada, could be magical. She quickly falls in with the Crop, a group of local teens with whom she immediately shares her newfound cinephilic obsession. When they tell Cleo about a nearby reservoir that – legend has it – contains a time travel portal, Cleo knows exactly where she wants to go: Paris, 1960, to act in a Godard movie. If only the group could figure out how to make the portal work …

Recalling the early films of Richard Linklater, Gregg Araki and Wes Anderson, this coming-of-age fantasy drama blends an offbeat charm with a quietly rebellious energy. Writer/director Fergus Campbell’s debut feature conjures that familiar struggle between yearning to transcend the boundaries of small-town life and holding on to the all-too-fleeting freedom of youth. Compelling performances from Elsie Fisher (Eighth Grade; Christmas Eve in Miller’s Point, MIFF 2024) and Charlie B. Foster (son of Jodie) anchor this playful, tender tale that pays tribute to the power of cinema as its own form of time travel.
synopsis_zh:
内华达小镇的青少年试图穿越回1960年代的巴黎——这部迷人的处女作,散发着法国新浪潮那种闲适漫游的气息。

少女克莱奥偶遇一台老式香烟贩卖机,它吐出的却是一本卷了边的平装书,讲的是让-吕克·戈达尔的电影。自此她深信,自己新搬来的内华达州斯帕克斯镇或许藏着魔法。她很快融入了一群本地少年组成的"庄稼帮",并迫不及待地把这份新生的影迷痴迷分享给他们。当伙伴们告诉她,附近有一座水库——传说里藏着一道时空之门——克莱奥立刻明白了自己想去哪儿:1960年的巴黎,去演一部戈达尔的电影。只要这群人能弄明白怎么让传送门运转起来就好了……

这部成长奇幻剧令人想起理查德·林克莱特、格雷格·阿拉基与韦斯·安德森的早期作品,把一种不落俗套的魅力与静水深流的叛逆能量糅合在一起。编剧兼导演费格斯·坎贝尔的长片处女作,唤起了那种熟悉的挣扎:既渴望挣脱小镇生活的边界,又想抓住青春里转瞬即逝的自由。埃尔希·费舍尔(《八年级》《米勒角的平安夜》,MIFF 2024)与查理·B·福斯特(朱迪·福斯特之子)贡献了动人的表演,为这个俏皮而温柔的故事撑起筋骨——它向电影本身作为一种时空穿越的力量,献上了一曲致敬。
highlight_zh: 《八年级》女主领衔,戈达尔迷的新浪潮式青春奇旅

synopsis_en:
A stressed-out young doctor suspects hostile jobs are doing something gnarly to her generation. Turns out work doesn’t just suck – it mutates.

Margot (Mara Taquin, Zero Fucks Given) has worked hard to land her first job as a doctor in France’s most prestigious emergency department. But even in this life-and-death medical field, Charles Boyau Hospital is a brutal pressure cooker: the relentless competition among colleagues and merciless production-line pace set by her boss Hélène (Karin Viard, Polisse, MIFF 2011) have Margot so stressed that she’s literally sweating blood. She recalls treating similar unexplained symptoms in patients her own age: young professionals in French workplaces are brutally lashing out, seeming somehow to be … changing. Is this extreme burnout, a novel virus or a transformative environmental adaptation?

Premiering in the Cannes Midnight Screenings section, Marion Le Corroller’s brash debut feature shares a blood type with her body-horreur compatriots Coralie Fargeat (The Substance, MIFF 2024) and Julia Ducournau (Titane, MIFF 2021). But Species delivers its critique of ‘rise-and-grind’ productivity culture with an axe, not a scalpel: every frame is a gonzo frenzy, captured expressionistically by cinematographer Guillaume Schiffman at tilted angles and through fish-eye lenses, all set to a pulsating electronic score by ROB. Imagine Philip Brophy’s Body Melt (MIFF 1994) infecting The Pitt and you might at least have the right genus … but Species is a breed all its own.
synopsis_zh:
一位压力爆棚的年轻医生怀疑，那些充满敌意的工作正在对她这一代人做出某种可怕的事。原来工作不只是让人痛苦——它还会让人变异。

玛戈（玛拉·塔坎 饰，《毫不在乎》）历经苦读，终于在法国最负盛名的急诊科拿到了她的第一份医生工作。然而，即便身处这个生死攸关的医疗领域，夏尔·博约医院也是一口残酷的高压锅：同事间无休止的竞争，加上上司艾莲娜（卡琳·维亚尔 饰，《警队》《2011年墨尔本电影节》）设定的流水线般冷酷节奏，让玛戈紧张到字面意义上地汗如血下。她记起自己曾为同龄病人诊治过类似的无法解释的症状：法国职场里的年轻白领纷纷暴力失控，仿佛正在以某种方式……发生改变。这究竟是极端的职业倦怠、一种新型病毒，还是一场颠覆性的环境适应？

本片入围戛纳午夜展映单元。玛丽昂·勒·科罗莱这部张扬的处女作，与她的身体恐怖同胞科拉莉·法尔雅（《某种物质》，2024年墨尔本电影节）和朱利亚·迪库诺（《钛》，2021年墨尔本电影节）流着同一种血。但《变种》递出的这份对「奋斗内卷」生产力文化的批判，用的是斧头，而非手术刀：每一帧都是一场癫狂的狂欢，由摄影师纪尧姆·希夫曼以倾斜的构图和鱼眼镜头写意般捕捉，全程配以 ROB 律动不息的电子配乐。想象菲利普·布罗菲的《肉体熔解》（1994年墨尔本电影节）感染了《匹兹堡医护》，你或许至少摸到了它的属类……但《变种》，自成一格。
highlight_zh: 戛纳午夜场惊悚，用斧头砍向内卷职场的法式身体恐怖

synopsis_en:
A queer pole dancer broods on traumatic memories in this visually striking tone poem.

Bloodied after an attack, genderqueer pole dancer Ash shelters in a church, where fantasies of revenge collide with resurgent childhood memories of abuse. Inspired by Madison Godfrey’s poem – which unfurls in voiceover as Ash’s interior monologue – Accelerator Lab alum Grace Tan (A Daydream With Fini, MIFF 2024) creates an engrossingly stylish, grungy visual collage that demands a big screen.
synopsis_zh:
一位酷儿钢管舞者在这部视觉惊艳的音诗电影中，沉溺于创伤记忆。

遭袭后浑身是血的性别酷儿钢管舞者阿什躲进一座教堂，复仇的幻想与重新浮现的童年受虐记忆在此激烈碰撞。影片灵感来自麦迪逊·戈弗雷的诗作——以画外音的形式化作阿什的内心独白娓娓道来。加速器实验室出身的导演陈慧恩（《与菲妮的白日梦》，2024年墨尔本国际电影节）由此构筑出一幅引人入胜、风格张扬而粗粝斑驳的视觉拼贴，值得在大银幕上一睹为快。
highlight_zh: 酷儿钢管舞者的复仇音诗，视觉惊艳的大银幕之作

synopsis_en:
Communist history decays quietly under children’s curious gaze, leaving statues to commune with one another.

Budapest’s Memento Park is an ideological graveyard where uprooted communist-era monuments are reinstalled in conversation. What might they say now? Children lead this imaginative inquiry, interviewing passing tourists and older Hungarian visitors who lived through communism. Riffing on the title of a 1953 Chris Marker and Alain Resnais short, this playful elegy to fallen political aspirations probes crumbling stone for lingering sparks of life.
synopsis_zh:
在孩子们好奇的注视下，共产主义的历史静静地朽坏，只留下雕像彼此交谈。

布达佩斯的纪念公园是一座意识形态的墓园，那些被连根拔起的共产主义时代纪念碑在这里被重新竖起、彼此对话。它们如今会说些什么？孩子们领着这场充满想象力的探问，采访路过的游客和亲历过共产主义年代的匈牙利老人。影片戏仿1953年克里斯·马克与阿伦·雷乃短片的片名，是一曲献给逝去政治理想的俏皮挽歌，在残破的石像中探寻残存的生命火花。
highlight_zh: 致敬马克与雷乃经典，孩子视角叩问倒下的雕像

synopsis_en:
Viewer Advice: Contains archival footage of war and conflict, depictions of violence towards animals and distressing scenes

The premier chronicler of all things cinematic returns with his latest plunge into film history – this time, shining a projectionist’s lamp on more than a century of reality-based storytelling.

From 1895’s Workers Leaving the Lumière Factory through the cinematic achievements of Leni Riefenstahl, Agnès Varda, Frederick Wiseman and Abbas Kiarostami, the history of film can’t be told without documentary. Interrogating “observational films, campaigning movies, portrait docs, archive-based films, personal essays, music films, journey documentaries, experimental and animated non-fiction cinema”, passionate celluloid historian Mark Cousins (Women Make Film: A New Road Movie Through Cinema, MIFF 2020) turns his lens on the vast corpus of documentary films. Fashioning unexpected connections and highlighting overlooked filmmakers, he reframes our ideas of truth, power and the pursuit of onscreen beauty.

With his 900-minute, Peabody Award–winning opus The Story of Film: An Odyssey, Cousins issued a groundbreaking audiovisual history of cinema, and he remains the definitive (softly spoken) voice of film scholarship. Tackling the “more shadowy” flipside to fiction’s glamorous past, Cousins’s journey into factual filmmaking follows a very different path from the conventional canon, and what he discovers there is guaranteed to surprise and intrigue even the most seasoned cinephile. MIFF is delighted to present, across three sessions, the first nine episodes of this towering new achievement – a cinematic smorgasbord that shouldn’t be missed.
synopsis_zh:
观影提示：含战争与冲突的历史影像、动物受暴力对待的画面及令人不适的场景。

影史头号编年史家再度归来，又一次纵身跃入电影史的长河——这一次，他将放映机的灯光投向一个多世纪以来以真实为本的影像叙事。

从1895年的《工厂大门》，到莱妮·里芬斯塔尔、阿涅斯·瓦尔达、弗雷德里克·怀斯曼与阿巴斯·基亚罗斯塔米的电影成就，一部电影史若少了纪录片便无从谈起。热忱的胶片史家马克·卡曾斯（《电影中的女性：一次穿越影史的新公路之旅》，2020年墨尔本国际电影节）逐一叩问“观察式影片、社会运动电影、人物纪录片、档案影像作品、私人影像随笔、音乐电影、旅程纪录片，以及实验与动画非虚构电影”，将镜头对准了浩如烟海的纪录片作品。他编织出意料之外的关联，让被忽视的影人重见天日，并借此重塑我们对真实、权力与银幕之美的理解。

凭借长达900分钟、荣获皮博迪奖的鸿篇巨制《电影史话》，卡曾斯曾为世人献上一部开创性的影像电影史，至今仍是电影学术那个（轻声细语的）权威之声。这一次，他转身面对虚构电影璀璨过往那“更为幽暗”的另一面，踏上一条与传统经典序列迥然不同的非虚构之旅，沿途的发现足以让最资深的影迷也感到惊奇与着迷。墨尔本国际电影节欣然分三场放映这部宏伟新作的前九集——一席不容错过的电影盛宴。
highlight_zh: 《电影史话》导演卡曾斯又一鸿篇巨制，重写百年纪录片史

synopsis_en:
Viewer Advice: Contains archival footage of war and conflict; distressing scenes

The premier chronicler of all things cinematic returns with his latest plunge into film history – this time, shining a projectionist’s lamp on more than a century of reality-based storytelling.

Kim Novak excels alongside Kirk Douglas in this heartbreaking story of an extramarital affair among the sleepy suburban hills of 1950s Los Angeles.

Margaret “Maggie” Gault (Kim Novak) is listless, persisting in a stable but passionless marriage to a conservative prude. Kirk Douglas is Larry Coe, an architect in a happy but comfortable marriage who has just taken on a new project to design a house for a successful writer played by Ernie Kovacs (Bell, Book and Candle, MIFF 2026). After a meet-cute at school pick-up and a follow-up at the local grocery store, Maggie and Larry embark on a tender, deeply felt affair, sharing a connection they haven’t previously enjoyed with anyone else – one in which they can be their authentic selves. But when nosy neighbour Felix (Walter Matthau, The Odd Couple) discovers the dalliance, he makes a sinister play for Larry’s wife.

Adapted from a novel by Evan Hunter, Strangers When We Meet is a gorgeously rendered melodrama that fields a mature viewpoint on adult relationships, layered with shades of grey. The film boasts an exceptional supporting cast: Matthau revels in his sleazy role as a smug spanner in the works; while Barbara Rush is also wonderful, exuding quiet wisdom and grace as Larry’s pragmatic and perceptive wife – her rebuff of Felix’s unwanted advances is a scene-stealer. The house that Larry constructs, meanwhile, was purpose-built for the film, designed by legendary Hollywood art directors Carl Anderson and Ross Bellah.

In this Cannes Un Certain Regard–premiering political drama, two Moroccan women doing seasonal work in Spain struggle against dire labour conditions.

Hoping to make ends meet, tetchy Hasna and timid Meriem set sail for the Spanish province of Huelva, where they will earn 35 euros per day as strawberry pickers. The work is onerous, and their difficult circumstances – the language barrier, the oppressive conditions, the distance from loved ones in Morocco – are ameliorated only by their camaraderie with their female colleagues. When acts of extortion and sexual assault throw a wrench into those relationships, however, Hasna and Meriem must decide whether to protect their livelihoods or their dignity.

Co-written by Delphine Agut (The Story of Souleymane, MIFF 2024; Inshallah a Boy, MIFF 2023) and based on real-world cases, Laïla Marrakchi’s bracing third feature depicts the insidious systems that enable migrant and gender-based exploitation across the Mediterranean. Timely and eye-opening, Strawberries sheds light not just on the toxic feedback loop between desperation and oppression, but also on the oblivious privilege of the First World societies that rely on this workforce. Nisrin Erradi (Adam, MIFF 2019) is formidable as Hasna: a compelling yet flawed heroine whose combative spirit jars with her instinct to prioritise self-preservation.

Tension escalates when a young Aussie woman is left alone in a London share house with an unsettling stranger.

In this stylishly executed thriller set entirely within a London share house, a young Australian woman (Olivia Junkeer, Neighbours; Why Are You Like This) finds herself alone with the mysterious tenant staying in her friend’s room. Is he merely a perceived threat, or a very real danger? Playing with the familiar tropes of the domestic thriller, writer/director James Nolan strikes at the fears of anyone who’s found themselves in an uncomfortable renting situation with punchy, unnerving results.
synopsis_zh:
当一名年轻的澳大利亚女子被独自留在伦敦的一间合租屋里，与一个令人不安的陌生人共处一室，紧张气氛不断升级。

这部风格化的惊悚片全程发生在伦敦一间合租屋内。一名年轻的澳大利亚女子（奥利维亚·君基尔，代表作《邻居》《你为什么这样》）发现自己与借住在朋友房间里的神秘房客独处一室。他只是她臆想出来的威胁，还是货真价实的危险？编剧兼导演詹姆斯·诺兰玩转家宅惊悚片的经典套路，精准戳中每一个曾陷入尴尬租房处境者的内心恐惧，成片凌厉逼人、令人坐立难安。
highlight_zh: 全程一间合租屋，风格化家宅惊悚，戳中租房者的隐秘恐惧

synopsis_en:
Papuan Indigenous communities mobilise to fight a planned mine on the sacred Sepik River in this stirring portrait of ecoactivism.

When an Australian mining conglomerate announces plans to build a huge copper/gold mine and toxic-waste dam on Papua New Guinea’s Sepik River, local communities fear pollution and ecological degradation. The “Amazon of the Asia Pacific” snakes for over 1100 kilometres through virgin rainforest, and is one of the few remaining large rivers in the world whose water remains drinkable. It’s also home to tens of thousands, who’ve lived along the river for millennia, drawing on it for sustenance and spiritual lore. Led by the charismatic Emmanuel Peni, these communities begin to mobilise, coming together to fight the forces of old colonial power structures, extractive economic practices, multinational lobbying and the supposed inevitability of ‘progress’.

At once an intimate study of community and a rousing work of cine-activism, this boots-on-the-ground – or canoes-on-the-water – documentary offers a regional David vs Goliath story with deep ecological repercussions. Melanesian New Zealander filmmaker Matasila Freshwater (one of the directors of Vai, MIFF 2019) and Australian documentarian Lachlan McLeod (Clean, MIFF Closing Night 2022) collaborate with community members, their cameras invited to witness conversations, meetings and planning sessions. Filled with rich cinematography, Papuan folk songs and historical newsreels, Sukundimi Walks Before Me is a vivid portrait of virtuous defiance.

PRESENTED BY

The true story of how an album made by four tween girls became an accidental alt-music sensation.

In the first summer of the new millennium, with girl pop dominating the charts, 11-year-old Ayden moved to Santa Rosa, California, and met her new best friends: Jessica, Janet and Janet’s nine-year-old sister Mary. In between millennial tween pursuits like devising dance routines and holding sleepover seances, the four girls started a band. Calling themselves X-Cetra, they recorded eight songs with the help of Janet and Mary’s musician mum, Robin. But the final album was a disappointment, sounding more like a creepy, haunted version of Pure Moods than the slick pop they’d imagined. When summer ended, so did X-Cetra.

Their accidentally avant-garde album made its way onto the internet, however, where it developed a life of its own. Two decades later, having long since settled into the routines and responsibilities of work and family, the now-adult quartet were astonished to find that their childhood experiment had developed a fan base on music forums – and, inspired by the discovery, set to work rekindling their friendship and creating new music together. Winning the Grand Jury Award in SXSW’s Documentary Feature Competition, Ayden Mayeri’s autobiographical documentary doesn’t merely settle for Y2K nostalgia or outsider-music obsessions; rather, it’s an unexpectedly honest, introspective tale about four women reconnecting with fearless creativity and reckoning with their most authentic selves.
synopsis_zh:
四个少女组建的乐队，如何在无心插柳间成为另类音乐圈的意外传奇——这是一个真实的故事。
