新千年的第一个夏天，少女流行乐正霸占各大排行榜。11岁的艾登搬到了加州圣罗莎，结识了她的新挚友：杰西卡、珍妮特，还有珍妮特9岁的妹妹玛丽。在编排舞蹈、开睡衣派对通灵这些千禧一代少女的日常之余，四个女孩组起了一支乐队。她们给自己取名X-Cetra，在珍妮特和玛丽那位音乐人妈妈罗宾的帮助下录制了八首歌。可最终的专辑却令人失望——它听起来不像她们想象中那种精致的流行乐，倒更像一张诡异闹鬼版的《纯净心境》(Pure Moods)。夏天结束，X-Cetra也随之解散。

然而，这张阴差阳错间充满先锋气质的专辑辗转流入了互联网，并在那里有了自己的生命。二十年后，早已被工作与家庭的琐碎日常和责任所填满的四位女性，惊讶地发现她们童年时的这场音乐实验，竟已在音乐论坛上积攒起一群粉丝——受此触动，她们重新拾起彼此的友谊，携手创作起新的音乐。艾登·马耶里(Ayden Mayeri)这部自传性质的纪录片荣获西南偏南电影节纪录长片竞赛单元评审团大奖；它并不止步于对千禧年的怀旧或对局外人音乐的痴迷，而是一个出人意料地坦诚、内省的故事，讲述四个女人如何重新拥抱无所畏惧的创造力，并与最真实的自我坦然相对。
highlight_zh: SXSW纪录片评审团大奖，四少女乐队跨越二十年的重聚

synopsis_en:
In this tribute to one of music’s most enduring, community-minded fan bases, head out on the road with the true believers who follow a Grateful Dead revival act around the US.

Jerry and Annie are young and in love – with each other, and with the music of the Grateful Dead. Setting out on the road to catch every show on successor band Dead & Company’s farewell tour in 2023, they journey across America and deep into the heart of Deadhead fandom. Meeting fans young and old and participating in the marketplaces and gatherings that sprout around the shows, they discover a true community, alternative ways of living and what the music means to a wideranging cross-section of Deadheads.

Mischa Richter’s “love letter” to the communal roadshow of Grateful Dead tours is a deeply personal doc from a committed Dead devotee – as are two of the film’s producers, Chloë Sevigny (Magic Farm, MIFF 2025) and Emily Mortimer (The Party, MIFF 2017). Filmed on 16mm and from deep within the community of fans, Summer Tour isn’t about the band – who are never seen performing – but those who follow them. It adds up to a warm-hearted hymn to hippie culture that doubles as an exploration of how people find meaning, and belonging, in art.
synopsis_zh:
这是一封写给乐坛最长盛不衰、最重社群精神的乐迷群体的情书。跟随那些虔诚的信徒一起上路，追着一支翻唱"感恩而死"的乐队跑遍全美。

杰瑞和安妮年轻而相爱——爱着彼此，也爱着"感恩而死"的音乐。2023年，他们踏上旅途，一场不落地追看继承乐队"Dead & Company"告别巡演的每一场演出，穿越美国大地，深入"感恩而死"铁杆乐迷（Deadhead）文化的腹地。他们结识老老少少的乐迷，参与在演出周边自发形成的集市与聚会，从中发现了一个真正的社群、一种另类的生活方式，也读懂了这音乐对形形色色的乐迷究竟意味着什么。

米沙·里希特为"感恩而死"巡演这场公路上的集体狂欢献上了一封"情书"，这是一部出自铁杆乐迷之手、极为私人化的纪录片——本片的两位监制克洛伊·塞维尼（《魔法农场》，2025年墨尔本电影节）与艾米莉·莫迪默（《宴会》，2017年墨尔本电影节）同样是忠实拥趸。影片以16毫米胶片拍摄，从乐迷社群的最深处取景，《夏日巡演》讲的不是乐队——他们从未在片中登台演出——而是那些追随者。它最终汇成一曲温暖的嬉皮文化赞歌，同时也探问人们如何在艺术中寻得意义与归属。
highlight_zh: 塞维尼、莫迪默监制，16毫米胶片写给"感恩而死"乐迷的深情情书

synopsis_en:
A radiant Dakota Fanning anchors US indie cinema luminary Joe Swanberg’s long-awaited return: a canny and heartfelt love-triangle dramedy set in Alaska.

Wendy (Dakota Fanning, Once Upon a Time in Hollywood, MIFF 2019) is 31 years old and happily in love with Jack (Jake Johnson, Safety Not Guaranteed, MIFF 2012), a divorcee in his 40s. The only snag in their age-gap relationship: she wants to have children, but he doesn’t want more than the two he already has; he’s also leery of getting married again. Hoping to inspire her to outgrow those particular urges, Jack proposes a six-month break in which she might dive into the Anchorage dating pool. What he hasn’t accounted for is the reappearance of Wendy’s handsome ex, a pilot who may prove less flighty than he once was.

The Sun Never Sets marks the first full-length feature in nearly a decade by Joe Swanberg (Drinking Buddies, MIFF 2013), an auteur who helped to forge the mumblecore lexicon – and launch the acting career of Greta Gerwig – with such heavily improvised and incredibly frank flicks as Hannah Takes the Stairs and Nights and Weekends. Though suffused with the same bracing candour and wit as those earlier, lo-fi ventures, Swanberg’s latest is captured on handsome 35mm film.

PRESENTED BY

In a new twist on nature documentary, the old-school format of super-8 offers a stunningly impressionistic, hopeful vision of our footprint upon the world.

Forget ultra-high-definition cameras, macro imagery and drone shots. Compiled exclusively from new super-8 footage shot on vintage cameras in 25 countries across five continents, Super Nature offers an unexpectedly rich, gloriously lo-fi view of the world, captured by 40 local volunteer cinematographers – including Australians Oliver Birt, Caitlin Henderson and Fraser Johnston – who filmed whatever caught their eye, providing commentary as they saw fit. From Welsh puffins and Patagonian whales to Italian mountain ranges and Indigenous Brazilian communities, Super Nature is a global love letter to nature, written in all the textures of the human heart.

It’s a testament to Ed Sayers’s lifelong passion for super-8 that he could craft such a coherent narrative from these disparate vignettes, shot by super-8 aficionados as well as biologists, conservationists and environmental activists. Sharing his vision was Oscar-winning filmmaker Asif Kapadia (Amy; Senna, MIFF 2011), who was so inspired by Sayers’s pitch that he came on board as executive producer. Despite our planet’s obvious challenges, Super Nature is full of compassion and hope for its future, reconnecting us to our world through the nostalgic intimacy and vibrant immediacy of its format.
synopsis_zh:
在自然纪录片的全新演绎中，超8毫米胶片这一老派介质，为我们呈现出一幅印象派般惊艳、饱含希望的图景，映照出人类在这颗星球上留下的足迹。

忘掉超高清摄影机、微距镜头和航拍视角吧。《超凡自然》全片仅由老式相机拍摄的全新超8毫米胶片素材剪辑而成，取景横跨五大洲25个国家，出自40位当地志愿摄影师之手——其中包括澳大利亚的奥利弗·伯特、凯特琳·亨德森和弗雷泽·约翰斯顿。他们拍下一切触动自己的画面，兴之所至便随口讲述，为影片带来意想不到的丰盈质感与恣意的低保真世界景观。从威尔士的海鹦到巴塔哥尼亚的鲸鱼，从意大利的连绵山脉到巴西的原住民部落，《超凡自然》是一封写给大自然的全球情书，字里行间尽是人类心灵的万千纹理。

这些散落各处的短小片段，出自超8毫米胶片发烧友，也出自生物学家、生态保护者与环保行动者之手；埃德·塞耶斯能将它们编织成如此连贯的叙事，正是他毕生痴迷超8毫米胶片的明证。奥斯卡获奖导演阿西夫·卡帕迪亚（《艾米》《塞纳》，2011年墨尔本国际电影节）也认同他的理念——被塞耶斯的构想深深打动的他，欣然加盟担任监制。尽管我们的星球正面临种种显而易见的挑战，《超凡自然》却满怀悲悯与希望，以这一介质怀旧的亲密感与鲜活的即时性，让我们重新与这个世界紧紧相连。
highlight_zh: 奥斯卡得主卡帕迪亚监制，超8胶片写给自然的全球情书

synopsis_en:
After being mistaken for his brother, a young trans man adapts to a precarious new life in his estranged father’s rural town.

City boy Jake is at a loose end. Unemployed, single and holed up in a granny flat behind his family home, he lives in the shadow of his cisgender twin brother, successful real estate agent Sam. On an impulse, he takes a trip to the remote country town of Sweet Milk, where his terminally ill, estranged father (Kieran Darcy-Smith) still lives, totally unaware that Jake has transitioned. When his dad mistakes him for Sam and Jake misses the moment to correct him, an unforeseen opportunity lands in his lap: to fit in as masculine in a place where nobody suspects he’s trans. Adopting Sam’s identity, he manages to connect with his father for the first time, while a budding romance with local Toby (Hunter Page-Lochard, Kid Snow, MIFF 2024) – himself an outsider in this macho setting – offers further reinvigoration. But the longer Jake spends in Sweet Milk, the more tangled the web he weaves. How long can he hold on to his secret, and what will happen if the truth gets out?

Drawing on his own experiences, debut filmmaker Harvey Zielinski also stars in the dual roles of Jake and Sam in this fish-out-of-water dramedy supported by the MIFF Premiere Fund – a raw, sensitive tale about the long road to self-acceptance and the lies we feel we need to tell to fit in.
synopsis_zh:
一个年轻的跨性别男子被错认成自己的兄弟，只得在与他疏远的父亲所在的乡下小镇，慢慢适应岌岌可危的新生活。

城里长大的杰克百无聊赖：失业、单身，蜗居在自家老宅后院的一间小屋里，始终活在顺性别双胞胎哥哥——事业有成的房产经纪人萨姆——的阴影之下。一时冲动之下，他动身前往偏远的乡下小镇甜牛奶，他那身患绝症、与他早已疏远的父亲（基兰·达西-史密斯 饰）仍住在那里，对杰克已经完成性别转换一事全然不知。当父亲把他错认成萨姆、而杰克又错过了纠正的时机，一个意想不到的机会就此落到他手中：在一个没有人会怀疑他是跨性别者的地方，以一个阳刚男性的身份融入其中。顶着萨姆的身份，他第一次与父亲建立起了联结；与此同时，他与本地人托比（亨特·佩奇-洛查德，《童拳小子》，2024 年墨尔本电影节）——一个在这个充满阳刚气息的环境里同样格格不入的外来者——之间萌生的恋情，也为他注入了新的活力。可杰克在甜牛奶待得越久，他编织的谎言之网就越发缠绕难解。这个秘密，他还能守多久？一旦真相败露，又会发生什么？

新锐导演哈维·齐林斯基取材于自身经历，在这部由墨尔本电影节首映基金支持的“离水之鱼”式悲喜剧中一人分饰杰克与萨姆两角。这是一个真挚而细腻的故事，讲述通往自我接纳的漫漫长路，以及我们为了融入而觉得不得不说出的那些谎言。
highlight_zh: MIFF首映基金力作，导演自演跨性别双胞胎，真挚喜剧

synopsis_en:
Viewer Advice: Contains stroboscopic imagery

A brilliantly multilayered, textural homage to a pioneer of queer rights.

Inspired by Filipina American paintbrush maker and queer rights activist Rosalie “Rose” Bamberger, this playful, reflective new work from queer Japanese American artist and scholar TT Takemoto uses an abstract collage of archival images to reanimate a forgotten chapter of history, in which the Daughters of Bilitis (of which Rose was a founding member) became the first US lesbian political rights organisation.

Man meets stork in this heartwarming depiction of rural struggle and rehabilitation from one of the filmmakers behind the Oscar-nominated Honeyland.

Nikola Conev, born into a long lineage of farmers in North Macedonia, is struggling. Harsh economic conditions, the collapse of local markets and the decline of rural living have gripped his local community. His wife, children and grandchildren have all left the country for greater opportunities. At a loss, and filled with loneliness, he brings home a white stork with a broken wing. As he nurses it back to health, Conev experiences his own sense of healing.

Tamara Kotevska’s previous portrait of farming in rural North Macedonia, Honeyland (co-directed with Ljubomir Stefanov, MIFF 2019), received Oscar nominations for both Best Documentary and Best International Feature Film. The Tale of Silyan, which premiered at Venice and won Best Feature Documentary at the International Documentary Association Awards, is equally impressive: a unique modern-day folktale filled with heart, humour and spectacular cinematography.

“An excellent documentary that also happens to be a ravishing work of poetry … The Tale of Silyan is the work of a filmmaker capable of shattering categories and inventing new forms.” – IndieWire

The Monday 17 August screening of this film is a sensory-friendly session. More information about our access offerings is available here.
synopsis_zh:
在这部温情之作中，一个人与一只鹳相遇——影片出自奥斯卡提名纪录片《蜂蜜之地》幕后主创之手，讲述乡村的挣扎与疗愈。

尼古拉·科涅夫出生于北马其顿一个世代务农的家庭，如今却举步维艰。严酷的经济环境、本地市场的崩塌与乡村生活的衰败，紧紧攫住了他所在的社区。他的妻子、儿女和孙辈都已离开故土，去往别处寻求更好的机会。在茫然与孤独之中，他把一只翅膀受伤的白鹳带回了家。在悉心照料它康复的过程中，科涅夫也感受到了自身的治愈。

塔玛拉·科特夫斯卡此前那部描绘北马其顿乡村农耕的作品《蜂蜜之地》（与柳博米尔·斯特凡诺夫联合执导，2019年墨尔本电影节展映），曾同时获得奥斯卡最佳纪录片与最佳国际影片提名。《西利安的传说》毫不逊色：它在威尼斯电影节首映，并荣获国际纪录片协会奖最佳纪录长片——这是一则独一无二的当代民间寓言，饱含真情与幽默，摄影亦令人叹为观止。

“一部出色的纪录片，同时又是一件动人心魄的诗意之作……《西利安的传说》出自一位能够打破类型边界、开创全新形式的电影人之手。”——《IndieWire》

8月17日（周一）本片放映为感官友好场次。有关无障碍观影服务的更多信息请见此处。
highlight_zh: 《蜂蜜之地》导演新作，威尼斯首映，IDA最佳纪录长片

synopsis_en:
Sarah Leavitt’s acclaimed graphic novel about Alzheimer’s is brought to the screen with an exceptional voice cast including Abbi Jacobson, Julia Louis-Dreyfus and Bryan Cranston.

Sarah is in her twenties and embracing life in San Francisco, where she has a job she loves at a queer magazine and a new girlfriend she adores even more. It’s 1999, and the future looks bright. But on a trip back home to the conservative Maine town she only recently escaped, she notices something’s not quite right with her mum, Midge. When the diagnosis comes – early-onset Alzheimer’s disease – Sarah finds herself caught between two worlds, juggling the life she was just beginning with the one she thought she’d left behind.

Working closely with Sarah Leavitt, the author of the graphic novel Tangles: A Story about Alzheimer’s, My Mother and Me, debut director Leah Nelson has created an exceptionally moving tribute – not only to the source material, but also to everyone touched by the ruthless tangles of Alzheimer’s. The gorgeous, mostly black-and-white, hand-drawn animation pays homage to the book while not replicating it, and weaves seamlessly from authenticity to surreal fantasy as the story dictates. Featuring a voice cast that includes Abbi Jacobson, Julia-Louis Dreyfus, Bryan Cranston, Seth Rogen, Samira Wiley, Beanie Feldstein, Pamela Adlon, Sarah Silverman, Bowen Yang and Wanda Sykes, Tangles is a playful, sweet and achingly honest film about family, love and the brutal truths of a cruel disease. Bring tissues.
synopsis_zh:
萨拉·莱维特广受赞誉的阿尔茨海默病主题图像小说被搬上银幕，配音阵容格外亮眼，包括阿比·雅各布森、朱莉娅·路易斯-德利法斯和布莱恩·科兰斯顿。

萨拉二十多岁，在旧金山尽情享受生活——她有一份心爱的工作，供职于一本酷儿杂志，还有一个更让她着迷的新女友。那是1999年，未来一片光明。然而在一次回缅因州老家的旅途中——那个保守的小镇是她不久前才逃离的——她察觉到母亲米姬有些不对劲。当诊断结果出来时——早发性阿尔茨海默病——萨拉发现自己被夹在两个世界之间：一边是刚刚起步的新生活，一边是她以为早已抛在身后的旧日时光。

首执导筒的莉亚·尼尔森与图像小说《纠缠：关于阿尔茨海默病、我母亲和我的故事》的作者萨拉·莱维特密切合作，打造出一部感人至深的致敬之作——不仅献给原著，也献给每一个被阿尔茨海默病无情缠绕的人。影片以精美、几乎全部黑白的手绘动画向原著致敬，却不照搬原作，随剧情需要在真实与超现实幻境之间无缝流转。配音阵容包括阿比·雅各布森、朱莉娅·路易斯-德利法斯、布莱恩·科兰斯顿、塞斯·罗根、萨米拉·怀利、比妮·费尔德斯坦、帕梅拉·阿德龙、莎拉·西尔弗曼、杨伯文和旺达·塞克斯。《纠缠》诙谐、温柔而痛彻心扉地讲述了家庭、爱以及这一残酷疾病的无情真相。记得带上纸巾。
highlight_zh: 全明星配音，笑泪交织的阿尔茨海默病动画杰作

synopsis_en:
Viewer Advice: Contains themes of domestic violence

Grounded by authentic performances, Think Good is a deft psychological thriller about the insidiousness of domestic abuse.

Gil and Jacques meet young, fall head over heels and then marry quickly thanks to an unexpected pregnancy. But as the dust settles, Gil begins to understand that her relationship is dangerously toxic. Jacques is possessive and controlling: he weaponises their faith against her; isolates her from her friends and family; spies on her; and repeatedly gaslights her. As the threat of physical violence escalates, Gil realises that escape may be the only way to save her life.

Through Think Good, actor-turned-director Géraldine Nakache has crafted a vital and unsettling portrayal of coercive control disguised as love. Building extraordinary narrative anxiety through repeating flashbacks, she immerses viewers in the intricate details of the couple’s Orthodox Judaism, offering an uncommon lens through which to explore the urgent topic of gendered violence. French Canadian actors Monia Chokri and Niels Schneider (reunited 16 years after Xavier Dolan’s Heartbeats) pull no punches with their riveting performances: Chokri is breathtaking as Gil is pushed ever further into psychological distress by Jacques; for his part, Schneider had audiences yelling at the screen during the film’s premiere in Cannes.
synopsis_zh:
观影提示：影片含家庭暴力相关内容

凭借真实可信的表演，《好好想想》是一部精妙的心理惊悚片，剖析家庭虐待如何在无声中滋长。

吉尔与雅克年少相识，一见倾心，又因意外怀孕迅速步入婚姻。然而当激情退去，吉尔渐渐意识到，这段关系正朝着危险而有毒的方向滑落。雅克占有欲极强、控制欲十足：他把两人共同的信仰当作胁迫她的武器，将她与亲友隔绝，暗中监视她，并一次次对她进行精神操控。当肢体暴力的威胁不断升级，吉尔终于明白，逃离或许是保住性命的唯一出路。

演员出身的导演热拉尔丁·纳卡什用《好好想想》交出了一幅有力而令人不安的画像，揭示披着爱情外衣的胁迫性控制。她以反复闪回的手法营造出非同寻常的叙事焦虑，让观众沉浸于这对夫妻正统犹太教生活的种种细节之中，为审视性别暴力这一紧迫议题提供了一个罕见的视角。法裔加拿大演员莫妮亚·肖克里与尼尔斯·施奈德（在泽维尔·多兰的《听闻心跳》之后时隔十六年再度合作）的表演毫不留情、扣人心弦：肖克里将吉尔被雅克步步逼入精神崩溃的过程演绎得惊心动魄；而施奈德在戛纳首映时，更是让观众忍不住对着银幕怒吼。
highlight_zh: 《听闻心跳》原班主演时隔16年重聚，戛纳首映引全场怒吼

synopsis_en:
An enigmatic woman with no name books herself a one-way trip in order to drink her way through West Berlin in Ulrike Ottinger’s most revered film.

Dressed to the neuns, Tabea Blumenschein (known only here as “she”) arrives at Tegel Airport with one thing on her mind: alcohol. Setting off on a curious odyssey through Berlin’s bourgeois and bohemian drinking establishments, she invites a woman of very different means (Christine Lutze) to join her for a cognac or 10, and together they rub shoulders – at an aloof distance – with the city’s outré inhabitants (played by an eclectic cast including Nina Hagen, Eddie Constantine and Martin Kippenberger). Intent on her own oblivion, she pays no attention to the Greek chorus following her all the while: three sociologists in houndstooth suits whose conversation revolves around moral judgements and facts about gendered alcoholism.

Director Joanna Hogg (The Eternal Daughter, MIFF 2023; The Souvenir, MIFF 2019) has said that this film had a “decisive influence” on her desire to become a filmmaker, having seen it at age 19 and likening it to her love of the work of both Derek Jarman and Powell and Pressburger. Frequent Ottinger collaborator Blumenschein (star of Madame X: An Absolute Ruler and co-director of The Enchantment of the Blue Sailors) is dazzling in this “portrait of a drinker” (as the original German title translates to), bringing a doomed charisma to a mostly wordless performance. She also designed the glamorously clamorous costumes, and appears briefly in drag.

An adulterous love affair brings tragedy to a traditional village in Burkina Faso in Idrissa Ouedraogo’s masterful, Cannes-awarded fable, now restored in 4K.

When Saga returns home after an absence of two years, he’s dismayed to learn that his fiancée, Nogma, has married someone else – his father. Her feelings for Saga haven’t waned, she reassures him, but she had no choice in the matter. Reunited, they find temptation too great to resist: in the straw hut where Saga is staying, just outside the village, he and his would-have-been bride discreetly consummate their courtship. But this transgression is discovered – per tribal law, treated as not just adultery but also incest – fracturing both family bonds and the broader community.

Like Idrissa Ouedraogo’s two earlier features, Yam Daabo and Yaaba (MIFF 1990), Tilaï features non-professional actors selected from among his family and friends; the film also offers a potent distillation of complex social dynamics set against a backdrop of expansive plains – the filmmaker’s native lands looming mythically in stark compositions – and ancient moral codes. Having originally screened in 1990 at both MIFF and Cannes, where it shared the Grand Jury Prize, Tilaï returns newly restored, its modernist take on a story steeped in patriarchal tradition as striking today as it was decades ago.
synopsis_zh:
在伊德里萨·奥雷德拉奥戈这部斩获戛纳大奖、如今以 4K 修复重现的杰作寓言中，一段不伦之恋为布基纳法索一座传统村落带来了悲剧。

萨加离乡两年后归来，却沮丧地得知未婚妻诺格玛已另嫁他人——嫁的正是他的父亲。诺格玛向他保证，自己对萨加的情意从未消减，只是这桩婚事由不得她做主。重逢之后，两人终究抵挡不住诱惑：在村外萨加暂居的那间草屋里，他与本应成为新娘的女子悄然圆了这段未竟的姻缘。然而这一逾矩之举败露了——按部族律法，它不仅是通奸，更被视作乱伦——由此撕裂了两代人的亲情，也撕裂了整个村落。

如同奥雷德拉奥戈此前的两部长片《选择》与《雅巴》（1990 年墨尔本电影节），《提拉伊》同样起用非职业演员，皆从他的亲友中挑选；影片还将错综复杂的社会张力凝练得力透纸背，背景则是辽阔无垠的平原——导演故乡的土地在冷峻的构图中如神话般巍然矗立——以及古老的道德律令。本片当年曾于 1990 年在墨尔本电影节与戛纳同时亮相，并在戛纳荣获评审团大奖；如今它以全新修复版归来，那份以现代视角重述父权传统故事的力量，在数十年后依旧如初般震撼。
highlight_zh: 戛纳评审团大奖，非洲寓言杰作 4K 修复重现

synopsis_en:
A Melbourne filmmaker returns to the small Chinese town of his childhood, reconnecting with his family under difficult, emotionally profound circumstances.

When his father suffers a stroke, Melbourne-based filmmaker Vee Shi returns to his hometown, Longtian, Fuqing, in south-eastern China, a small rural community that he left behind when he was 13. As the family’s proud, stubborn patriarch struggles with his compromised health and need for care, his estranged wife and daughter – who, due to China’s one-child policy, was given up for adoption prior to the director’s birth – are suddenly entrusted with the duty, and burden, of looking after a man who has often treated them badly. These strained family dynamics evoke a bigger-picture portrait of changing Chinese society and social values, where the once-total dedication to family is falling away.

This unique hybrid documentary, initially conceived of as a scripted film wherein Shi’s family would play themselves, is an at times shockingly intimate portrait of familial love, duty and trauma. The MIFF Accelerator alum’s previous short Jia (MIFF 2023) was a drama inspired by his relationship with his mother, as well as the familial and cultural estrangement of living in a different country in another hemisphere. The Visions du Réel–selected Time and Tide – the first film ever to be shot in Longtian, and only the second to feature the disappearing Fuqing dialect – profoundly and often painfully extends that inquiry to the contested space of a fractured family home.
synopsis_zh:
一位墨尔本电影人回到童年生活的中国小镇，在艰难而深刻的处境中与家人重新连结。

父亲突发中风后，旅居墨尔本的电影人施维（Vee Shi）回到了故乡——中国东南部的福清龙田，一个他十三岁便离开的乡下小镇。当这位骄傲而固执的一家之主为每况愈下的身体和无从回避的照护需求所困，他早已疏远的妻子，以及那个因计划生育政策、在导演出生前便被送养的女儿，突然被推到了照顾者的位置上——去照料一个曾屡屡亏待她们的男人，既是责任，也是负担。这些紧绷的家庭关系，映照出一幅更宏大的图景：中国社会与价值观正在变迁，曾经对家庭无条件的奉献正逐渐消退。

这部独特的混合纪录片最初被构想为一部剧情片，由施维的家人本色出演。它以时而令人震动的亲密视角，呈现了家庭中的爱、责任与创伤。这位 MIFF Accelerator 计划的旧友此前的短片《家》（MIFF 2023）便是一部剧情作品，灵感来自他与母亲的关系，以及身处异国他乡、置身另一个半球所带来的家庭与文化的疏离。《岁月如潮》入选真实影展（Visions du Réel）——它是有史以来第一部在龙田拍摄的影片，也是仅有的第二部收录了正在消失的福清方言的作品——将那份追问，痛切而深刻地延伸至一个破碎家庭内部这片充满张力的空间。
highlight_zh: 真实影展入选，首部龙田实拍、罕录福清方言的私密家庭纪录片

synopsis_en:
Iceland’s glaciers – once imposing, now imperilled – loom over a personal journey through generations in Sara Dosa’s follow-up to Fire of Love.

The first Icelandic glacier was declared lost to climate change in 2019; recent studies project that, in 200 years, the country will be entirely ice-free. For celebrated author Andri Snær Magnason, the layers of history melting along with the ice have a personal connection: in the 1950s, his grandparents were among the first to explore Iceland’s glaciers, capturing their splendour in incredible colour footage that we see alongside Magnason’s own home movies.

Infusing chilly facts with a lyrical sensibility, this awe-inspiring documentary brings big environmental questions into focus through the lens of personal history. It’s a balancing act that director Sara Dosa already perfected in her breakout Fire of Love (MIFF 2022), which shone a new light on the work of pioneering volcanologist couple Katia and Maurice Krafft. Arriving at MIFF via Sundance, SXSW and CPH:DOX, Time and Water is a moving meditation on what once was, and what has already vanished.
synopsis_zh:
冰岛的冰川——曾经巍然耸立，如今岌岌可危——笼罩着一段跨越数代人的私人旅程。这是萨拉·多萨继《火山挚恋》之后的又一部作品。

2019年，冰岛第一座冰川被正式宣告因气候变化而消亡；近期研究预测，200年后，这个国家将彻底告别冰雪。对于著名作家安德里·斯纳·马格纳松而言，随冰川一同消融的层层历史有着一份私人的牵系：上世纪50年代，他的祖父母是最早探索冰岛冰川的先驱之一，用令人惊叹的彩色影像记录下冰川的壮丽——这些画面与马格纳松自己拍摄的家庭录像交织呈现。

这部令人心生敬畏的纪录片将冰冷的事实注入诗意的情怀，透过个人历史的镜头，让宏大的环境议题变得清晰可感。这种平衡的功力，导演萨拉·多萨早已在她的成名作《火山挚恋》（2022年墨尔本国际电影节）中臻于纯熟——那部影片为火山学界的先驱伉俪卡蒂娅与莫里斯·克拉夫特的事业投下了全新的光芒。《时间与水》历经圣丹斯、西南偏南与哥本哈根国际纪录片电影节后来到墨尔本国际电影节，是一曲对逝去之物与已然消失之景的动人沉思。
highlight_zh: 《火山挚恋》导演新作，圣丹斯展映，冰川消融的诗意挽歌

synopsis_en:
Viewer Advice: Contains stroboscopic imagery

The mechanical gaze of a book scanner becomes a hyper-speed collage of anatomy.

The latest in a hypnotic series of stop-motion montages from LA-based visual artist Mungo Thomson, this transformative work uses images from Time Life encyclopedias as raw material for an animated sequence of modelled postures and stances. Collage functions as choreography, and dance as a deceptively giddy refraction of modern life.
synopsis_zh:
观影提示：含频闪影像

图书扫描仪的机械凝视，化作一场超高速的人体解剖拼贴。

这是洛杉矶视觉艺术家蒙戈·汤姆森催眠般的定格蒙太奇系列的最新一作。他以《时代生活》百科全书中的图像为原材料，将其重构为一段由各种姿态与体势构成的动画序列。拼贴在此成为编舞，而舞蹈则是对现代生活一次看似轻快、实则暗藏机锋的折射。
highlight_zh: 洛杉矶艺术家定格蒙太奇，百科图像拼贴成人体之舞

synopsis_en:
How do you separate a Melbourne-born Oz music treasure and cosmopolitan star from a resilient woman? Follow the threads of her story, in her own words.

Tina Arena has been in the music business for 50 years; after 25 albums, she’s had enough eras to make other pop stars envious. Born in suburban Melbourne to Sicilian parents, Filippina Arena became Australia’s darling in 1976, aged eight, on Channel Ten’s prime-time variety show Young Talent Time. Her breakout 1990 pop single ‘I Need Your Body’ raunchily repudiated “Tiny Tina”, and she went on to score a soaring run of 90s hits … but self-doubt, misogyny and tall-poppy syndrome drove her to reinvent herself repeatedly in the UK and France: taking to the stage; seeking adventurous collaborations; and singing in Italian, French and Spanish.

Behind Tina the power balladeer and queer icon is the woman her family still calls Pina. This world-premiering documentary from Adrian Russell Wills (Kindred, MIFF 2023) unravels her vulnerable yet tenacious private self in candid, dryly funny conversation. Also appearing in new and archival interviews are Tina’s two sisters; her mentor Johnny Young; and collaborators and admirers including Céline Dion, Marc Anthony, Ronan Keating, Jessica Mauboy, and Jessica and Lisa Origliasso of The Veronicas. In bringing herself undone, Tina movingly shows how music has made and mended her.

A road movie that goes absolutely nowhere – to hypnotic, hilarious effect.

Demonstrating the playful qualities that once saw him dubbed the enfant terrible of experimental film, Johann Lurf filmed this absurdist 2011 short from a sputtering Vespa as it circled – and circled and circled and circled – 100 different roundabouts in his homeland, Austria. Full of mediocre landscapes, shoddy architecture and novelty sculpture, it’s an endearing homage to human achievement at its least wondrous. Winner of a short film award at the 2011 Viennale.
synopsis_zh:
一部哪儿也没去的公路片——却达到了催眠般、令人捧腹的效果。

约翰·卢尔夫曾因其顽皮的创作风格被称为实验电影界的坏小子，这部2011年的荒诞短片正是这一特质的绝佳展现。他骑着一辆突突作响的Vespa摩托车拍下全片，绕着家乡奥地利的100个环形交叉路口一圈又一圈、一圈又一圈地转个不停。片中尽是平庸的风景、粗劣的建筑和搞怪的雕塑，它以一种惹人喜爱的方式，向人类成就中最不足为奇的那一面致敬。本片荣获2011年维也纳电影节短片奖。
highlight_zh: 绕100个环岛的荒诞公路短片，维也纳电影节获奖

synopsis_en:
With poetic empathy, this Sundance Grand Jury Prize winner maps the love and determination that tie a Montenegrin shepherd to her land.

In Montenegro’s starkly beautiful Sinjajevina alpine range, middle-aged Mileva “Gara” Jovanović herds sheep, milks cows, makes delicious cheese and laughs with her friends, all the while raising adoring teenager Nada to be kind and resilient. Women here have endured generations of patriarchal violence, and Gara works hard to hold her precious space of care and community. As NATO forces plan to exploit these high rolling pastures for military training, Gara stands her ground over a seven-year period, rallying her neighbours to repel repeated incursions into the land and life she holds dear.

The winner of the top prize in Sundance 2026’s documentary competition is uplifting enough as a portrayal of staunch matriarchal activism. But directors Biljana Tutorov and Petar Glomazić are striving to capture something loftier: the mountain of love, grief and determination that Gara holds inside. Gently, elegantly, the film falls in rhythm with her steadfast seasonal labour; it’s beguilingly alert to the expressive power of sensory textures in the manner of Return to Dust (MIFF 2022), Honeyland (MIFF 2019) and In the Land of Wolves (MIFF 2018). Building an emotional cartography all its own, To Hold a Mountain traces the contours of the pure bonds between people and place.

The anxieties and heart-fluttering feelings of first love are given tender, respectful treatment in this charming coming-of-age tale.

Karl is 12, short, insecure … and totally in love. Does the much taller, literally out-of-reach Lea like him back? If so, how can he tell? And how is he going to deal with all the feelings that come with a crush in the meantime? An opportunity to understand these nascent feelings arrives when a teacher encourages Karl and his friends to make a nonfiction short film interviewing people about their first loves. While working on this project, Karl channels his longing into his own creative pursuits – taking photographs that he develops in his homemade darkroom, and composing a love song with his cooler older brother – in the hope of impressing Lea.

A sweet take on a familiar summertime-coming-of-age narrative, this special film dares to regard the normal, everyday emotions of adolescents as meaningful and worthy of exploration, with director Bernd Sahling placing the camera at eye level with his pint-sized protagonist. Free from nostalgia and never melodramatic or preachy, Tomorrow I’ll Be Brave is perfectly attuned to tweenage feelings.

Before disappearing from her life, an undocumented father shares a final moment with the daughter for whom he’s sacrificed everything.

As a young Chinese Australian woman graduates university, her father vanishes, leaving her a pouch of money and a lingering question: Was it all worth it? A work of great emotional precision, this tender short from author-turned-filmmaker Jeff Zechen Hu understands the power of nuance, offering a refreshing perspective on the immigrant experience by focusing on the family that must bear the cost.
synopsis_zh:
在从女儿的生活中消失之前，一位没有合法身份的父亲，与他倾尽所有去成全的女儿共度了最后一刻。

当这位年轻的华裔澳大利亚女子大学毕业时，父亲却不告而别，只留给她一小袋钱，和一个挥之不去的疑问：这一切，真的值得吗？由作家转型导演的胡泽辰以极为细腻的情感掌控，成就了这部温柔的短片。他深谙分寸的力量，将镜头对准那个必须承受代价的家庭，为移民题材带来一种耳目一新的视角。
highlight_zh: 作家跨界导演，细腻短片，从家庭视角重述移民代价

title_en: toward a fundamental theory of physics
title_zh: 迈向物理学的基本理论
synopsis_en:
Viewer Advice: Contains stroboscopic imagery

A uniquely tactile exploration of time and light, captured on 16mm film with hundreds of lenses.

Using a handcrafted time slice camera equipped with 293 lenses, Belgian artist Victor Van Rossem resurrects a vintage photographic technique to create an incredibly immersive depiction of light on film, striking and almost spectral in its strange optical pull. In the film’s otherworldly space – its 3D tangle of colour and movement – light itself appears to become a physical object.
synopsis_zh:
观影提示：本片含频闪影像。

一次对时间与光的独特触觉式探索，以 16 毫米胶片、数百枚镜头拍摄而成。

比利时艺术家维克多·范罗森使用一台手工打造、配备 293 枚镜头的时间切片相机，复活了一种复古摄影技法，创造出对胶片之光的极致沉浸式呈现——它以奇异的视觉牵引力，显得凛冽而近乎鬼魅。在影片那超凡脱俗的空间里——那由色彩与运动交织而成的三维缠结中——光本身仿佛化作了一件实体之物。
highlight_zh: 293枚镜头手工相机，16毫米胶片上的光之奇观

synopsis_en:
A burnt-out Parisian doctor travels to an ultra-remote, penguin-populated South Atlantic isle in search of isolation, salvation and perhaps even love.

Tristan da Cunha is the most remote place of human habitation in the world. A volcanic speck in the South Atlantic, midway between Argentina and South Africa, it’s visited by only six to eight boats per year, its 230-odd inhabitants vastly outnumbered by penguins. Loran Bonnardot, a jaded Parisian physician with a lifetime serving in Médecins Sans Frontières, has spent 30 years visiting Tristan; now, he’s hoping to permanently relocate there to escape the hectic nature of 21st-century living. As Bonnardot explores the island’s wilderness and tries to integrate into its community, he awaits approval for permanent residency.

Swiss filmmaker Tobias Nölle has found acclaim for his experimental, eerie narrative features, from 2016 Berlinale stand-out Aloys to 2024 sci-fi parable Preparations for a Miracle. Here, directing in collaboration with Bonnardot, Nölle mounts a dreamlike, meditative film that blurs lines between ethnography, documentary and fantasy. With cinematography that evocatively brings Tristan da Cunha’s landscape to life, this is a quiet, contemplative study in both the pleasures and the perils of isolation – the perfect film for anyone with a love of exploration or a desire to escape.
synopsis_zh:
一位身心俱疲的巴黎医生远赴南大西洋一座企鹅成群、与世隔绝的偏远小岛，去寻找孤独、救赎，或许还有爱。

特里斯坦-达库尼亚是全世界人类居住地中最偏远的地方。它是南大西洋上一座火山孤岛，位于阿根廷与南非之间，每年只有六到八艘船靠岸，230余名居民的数量远远不及岛上的企鹅。洛朗·博纳尔多是一位对生活心生倦怠的巴黎医生，毕生服务于无国界医生组织，30年来他一直造访特里斯坦；如今，他希望永久定居于此，逃离21世纪生活的喧嚣忙乱。在等待永久居留审批的同时，博纳尔多探索着小岛的荒野，努力融入当地社区。

瑞士导演托比亚斯·诺勒以其充满实验色彩、幽微诡谲的叙事长片广受赞誉，从2016年柏林电影节的亮眼之作《阿洛伊斯》到2024年的科幻寓言《为奇迹做准备》皆是如此。这一次，他与博纳尔多联合执导，打造出一部如梦似幻、静谧沉思的影片，模糊了民族志、纪录片与幻想之间的界限。影片以极富感染力的摄影，让特里斯坦-达库尼亚的风景跃然银幕，是一曲关于孤独之乐与孤独之险的静默沉思——献给每一个热爱探索、或渴望逃离的人。
highlight_zh: 《阿洛伊斯》导演新作，全球最偏远孤岛上的诗意疗愈之旅

synopsis_en:
Viewer Advice: Contains stroboscopic imagery

Hollywood plays itself in this cheeky deconstruction of iconic studio idents.

At once an affectionate ode to Hollywood studios and a playful subversion of global hyper-capitalism, Johann Lurf’s wickedly amusing 2014 short slices and dices pre-feature idents from the familiar likes of Universal, Disney, Paramount and Warner Bros. until they become a glitching symphony of chaos. Seen from the vantage point of the big studio mergers a decade later, Twelve Tales Told becomes a poignant requiem for a once-mighty cinematic model.
synopsis_zh:
观影提示：本片含频闪影像。

好莱坞在这场对经典片头厂标的俏皮解构中出演它自己。

约翰·卢尔夫这部机智诙谐的2014年短片，既是献给好莱坞各大制片厂的深情颂歌，也是对全球超级资本主义的顽皮颠覆。他将环球、迪士尼、派拉蒙、华纳兄弟等我们耳熟能详的正片前厂标切割、重组，直至它们化作一曲错乱失灵的混沌交响。而当我们站在十年后大厂并购潮的视角回望，《十二则故事》又俨然成为一曲哀婉的安魂曲，悼念那曾经威风八面的电影产业模式。
highlight_zh: 实验短片鬼才解构环球、迪士尼经典厂标，戏谑又深情

synopsis_en:
Locarno’s Golden Leopard winner is a sublime nested tale of creativity, loneliness and making new connections.

Screenwriter Li (Shim Eun Kyung), a Korean expat living in Japan, is hard at work on a new idea: a story of a blossoming friendship between Nagisa (Yuumi Kawai) and Natsuo (Mansaku Takada), a young woman and man who meet by chance on a quiet beach. When that story fails to connect with an audience, Li retreats to a remote inn in wintertime. There, she strikes up an unlikely friendship with the taciturn owner, Benzo (Shinichi Tsutsumi), who says he rates films based on “how well they depict human sadness”.

Following his impressive previous pictures Small, Slow but Steady and All the Long Nights, rising Japanese auteur Sho Miyake received Locarno’s top honour (plus an additional prize from the Junior Jury) for this tender exploration of human aches and interpersonal dynamics. Adapting two short stories from legendary 20th-century manga artist Yoshiharu Tsuge, this elusive diptych has earned comparisons to the work of MIFF mainstay Hong Sang-soo.
synopsis_zh:
洛迦诺电影节金豹奖得主，一则关于创作、孤独与建立新连结的精妙嵌套故事。

旅居日本的韩国编剧李（沈恩京 饰）正埋头于一个新构思：一段萌生于渚（河合优实 饰）与夏夫（高田万作 饰）之间的友情——一对青年男女在僻静的海滩上偶然相遇。当这个故事未能引起观众共鸣，李便退隐到一家冬日里偏远的旅馆。在那里，她与寡言的旅馆主人辨造（堤真一 饰）结下了一段意料之外的情谊。辨造说，他评判一部电影的标准，是它「把人的悲伤描摹得有多好」。

继备受赞誉的前作《惠子，凝视》与《漫长的告白》之后，崭露头角的日本作者导演三宅唱凭借这部对人心隐痛与人际互动的温柔探索，摘得洛迦诺电影节最高荣誉（并另获青年评审团奖）。影片改编自20世纪传奇漫画大师柘植义春的两则短篇，这幅难以言尽的双联画，被誉为可与墨尔本电影节常客洪常秀的作品相提并论。
highlight_zh: 洛迦诺金豹奖！三宅唱温柔新作，改编柘植义春漫画

synopsis_en:
In this comedic exploration of intimacy and consent, a sophisticated polyamorous couple spiral after being snubbed by their third.

Dining out for their anniversary, Andy and Scarlett get blanked by a woman they had a threesome with months earlier, prompting them to relitigate the encounter’s consent politics in excruciating detail. Wittily scripted and confidently directed by April-Rose Desalegn, co-writer and star of MIFF 2025 Best Australian Short winner The Eviction, Twosome is a fresh, provocative and very recognisable study of relationship dynamics.
synopsis_zh:
在这部探讨亲密与同意的喜剧中，一对老练的多元之恋伴侣因遭到他们的"第三者"冷落而陷入内耗。

为纪念周年在外用餐时，安迪和斯嘉丽被几个月前曾与他们玩过三人游戏的女子视而不见，这促使二人事无巨细地重新审视那次邂逅中的"同意"边界。本片剧本机智，导演手法笃定自信——出自 April-Rose Desalegn 之手，她正是 2025 年墨尔本国际电影节最佳澳大利亚短片《驱逐》的联合编剧兼主演。《二人成行》以清新、挑衅而又极具共鸣的笔触，剖析了亲密关系中的微妙博弈。
highlight_zh: MIFF最佳短片主创新作，机智辛辣直击亲密关系

synopsis_en:
Suspicion, murder and psychosexual paranoia abound in this twisted thriller from French New Wave titan Claude Chabrol, led by a coolly enigmatic performance from Stéphane Audran.

In this exquisitely calibrated homage to Hitchcock, Parisian insurance executive Charles Desvallées (Michel Bouquet, Night and Fog, MIFF 1966) begins to suspect that his wife Hélène (Stéphane Audran) is having an affair, sending him into an escalating state of paranoia – and towards a plan that will rupture the bourgeois calm of their existence.

With its chilly surfaces, precision staging and dark humour, this deliciously deviant drama finds the great Claude Chabrol (A Story of Women, MIFF 1989; The Butcher, MIFF 1973) on signature form, with the filmmaker’s then-wife Audran (The Discreet Charm of the Bourgeoisie, MIFF 2000; Les Biches) delivering a glamourous, emotionally opaque performance that’s ripe with depth and mystery. Critically acclaimed but initially unsuccessful with audiences, The Unfaithful Wife has gone on to become one of Chabrol’s most enduring and influential works, serving as the inspiration for both Adrian Lyne’s Unfaithful and Andrey Zvyagintsev’s recent Cannes Grand Prix winner Minotaur (MIFF 2026).

“One of Chabrol's mid-period masterpieces, a brilliantly ambivalent scrutiny of bourgeois marriage and murder that juggles compassion and cynicism in a way that makes Hitchcock look obvious.” – Time Out

This screening will be introduced by writer and editor Daniella Shreir (Another Gaze), who will host a panel discussion with critics Philippa Hawker and Indigo Bailey following the film.

Daniella Shreir is the founder and co-editor of film journal Another Gaze and a translator of literature, including work for The Paris Review and Granta and her award-winning translation of Chantal Akerman’s My Mother Laughs. She has served on the selection committee for Cannes Directors’ Fortnight and written for publications such as London Review of Books and Cahiers du Cinéma.

Philippa Hawker is a writer on film and the arts whose recent writing has appeared in Inside Story, The Saturday Paper, The Age and Australian Book Review. She was formerly a film reviewer and arts writer at The Age, film writer at The Australian, literary editor at The Sunday Herald and editor at Cinema Papers, and taught creative writing at RMIT and the School of Studies in the Arts at the Victorian College of the Arts. She is working on a book about Jean-Pierre Léaud.

Indigo Bailey is a Tasmanian critic and fiction writer living in Naarm/Melbourne. She is the managing editor of the online film publication Rough Cut, and has written for HEAT, Meanjin, Kill Your Darlings and Island Magazine, among other publications.
synopsis_zh:
在法国新浪潮巨匠克洛德·夏布洛尔这部扭曲惊悚片中，猜疑、谋杀与情欲妄想交织弥漫，斯特凡·奥德朗贡献了一场冷艳而神秘莫测的表演。

这是一曲向希区柯克致敬、精雕细琢的乐章：巴黎保险公司高管夏尔·德瓦莱（米歇尔·布凯饰，《夜与雾》，1966年墨尔本国际电影节）开始怀疑妻子埃莱娜（斯特凡·奥德朗饰）红杏出墙，猜忌步步升级，最终将他推向一个足以撕裂他们那份中产阶级安宁生活的计划。

凭借冷峻的表象、精准的调度和黑色幽默，这部妙趣而离经叛道的剧情片尽显夏布洛尔（《女人韵事》，1989年墨尔本国际电影节；《屠夫》，1973年墨尔本国际电影节）的招牌功力；而导演当时的妻子奥德朗（《资产阶级的审慎魅力》，2000年墨尔本国际电影节；《母鹿》）则奉上一场光彩照人、情感深不可测的演出，蕴含着丰富的层次与谜团。本片虽广受好评，却在上映之初票房遇冷，如今已成为夏布洛尔最经久不衰、最具影响力的作品之一，并先后启发了阿德里安·莱恩的《不忠》与安德烈·萨金塞夫近期斩获戛纳评审团大奖的《米诺陶洛斯》（2026年墨尔本国际电影节）。

“夏布洛尔中期的杰作之一，对中产阶级婚姻与谋杀作出精妙暧昧的审视，将悲悯与犬儒并置玩转，相形之下连希区柯克都显得直白了。”——《Time Out》

本场放映将由作家兼编辑丹妮拉·施赖尔（《Another Gaze》）作映前介绍，并在影片结束后主持一场对谈，嘉宾为影评人菲莉帕·霍克与因迪戈·贝利。

丹妮拉·施赖尔是电影期刊《Another Gaze》的创始人兼联合主编，也是一位文学译者，曾为《巴黎评论》和《Granta》翻译作品，其对香特尔·阿克曼《我母亲在笑》的译作曾获奖。她曾任戛纳导演双周选片委员会委员，并为《伦敦书评》《电影手册》等刊物撰稿。

菲莉帕·霍克是一位电影与艺术评论作家，近期作品见于《Inside Story》《The Saturday Paper》《The Age》和《澳大利亚书评》。她曾任《The Age》影评人兼艺术撰稿人、《澳大利亚人报》电影撰稿人、《周日先驱报》文学编辑及《Cinema Papers》主编，并在皇家墨尔本理工大学和维多利亚艺术学院艺术研究学院教授创意写作。她正在撰写一本关于让-皮埃尔·利奥的著作。

因迪戈·贝利是一位塔斯马尼亚评论家兼小说作家，现居纳姆/墨尔本。她是在线电影刊物《Rough Cut》的执行主编，作品散见于《HEAT》《Meanjin》《Kill Your Darlings》《Island Magazine》等刊物。
highlight_zh: 夏布洛尔中期杰作，冷艳惊悚，希区柯克式婚姻谋杀寓言

synopsis_en:
Ulrike Ottinger revisits the locations of her famed ‘Berlin Trilogy’, juxtaposing frank documentation with fantasy.

In this exploration of both German urban architecture and her own cinematic work, Ottinger returns to selected locations from her ‘Berlin Trilogy’: Ticket of No Return (1979), Freak Orlando (1981) and Dorian Gray in the Mirror of the Yellow Press (1984). Following a joyous title sequence, industrial scenes and cityscapes are intercut with vignettes from their corresponding film scenes – providing less of a ‘then and now’ than a ‘what if’.

2K Digitisation
synopsis_zh:
乌尔莉克·奥廷格重返她著名的"柏林三部曲"拍摄地，将坦率的纪实影像与奇思幻想并置交织。

在这部同时探索德国城市建筑与她本人电影创作的作品中，奥廷格回到"柏林三部曲"——《单程票》（1979）、《怪胎奥兰多》（1981）与《黄色小报镜中的道林·格雷》（1984）——的若干取景地。在一段欢快的片头字幕之后，工业景象与城市风貌，与其对应影片场景的片段交叉剪辑——它呈现的与其说是"昔与今"的对照，不如说是"如果"的遐想。

2K 数字修复版
highlight_zh: 先锋名导奥廷格重返"柏林三部曲"取景地，纪实与幻想交织

title_en: Vaka Moana: Ancestors of the Future
synopsis_en:
Learn about time-honoured Polynesian conservation practices while exploring the most southerly and remote atoll of the Cook Islands.

Made in collaboration with Indigenous NGO Te Puna Marama Voyaging Foundation on location in the Cook Islands, Vaka Moana: Ancestors of the Future is a 48-minute immersive 360-degree experience that takes viewers on a journey to the remote atoll of Mangaia. We sail on a recreated version of the Vaka Moana (or ocean-going canoes) that a millennia ago brought the first people to the Polynesian Pacific – the very last stage of the great human migrations across planet Earth.

On this journey, we sail with Pwo master Peia Patai, one of only 10 masters of traditional non-instrument navigation (guided by stars, swells, ecological signs). While visiting ancient celestial maps in limestone caves and journeying across the oldest island in Polynesia, we also learn about the Polynesian resource-management strategy of Ra‘ui: to only take what is needed in order to conserve our ecology for the future. Sailing a small boat across the precarious and finite planet Earth, we learn about this ancestral Indigenous sustainable practice that is urgently needed globally. This is an immersive experience with a deep message from Polynesian ancestors and knowledge-holders for the future of us all.

Rotterdam’s Tiger Award winner riffs on the many lives of villagers in a lyrical docufiction fable of a forgotten South African mountain community.

Hettie is a goatherd in South Africa’s remote Kamiesberg mountains. On the eve of her 80th birthday, she’s offered long-overdue reparations for her father’s military service in World War II. The only problem: it’s a scam. As Hettie and her neighbours wait for money that will never come, other villagers search for diamonds, watch rugby, play dominoes or slip into memories. Surrounded by ghosts of past conflicts, in a rural hamlet forgotten by South African society, they are victims of forces large and small – but each has their own rich inner life.

Staged as a series of ‘variations’, this hybrid docufiction takes a literary approach, narrating the thoughts of its subjects in a voiceover by co-director Jason Jacobs. His real-life grandmother plays the leading role of Hettie, and that intimacy is at the centre of an achingly empathetic film blessed with cultural specificity, lyrical imagery and an evocative score by Mikhaila Alyssa Smith. Jacobs and Devon Delmar won Rotterdam’s top prize for this follow-up to their Venice-premiering 2024 debut Carissa, confirming them as vital new voices in South African – and global – cinema.
synopsis_zh:
鹿特丹金虎奖得主以一部抒情的纪录剧情寓言，谱写一座被遗忘的南非山村里众多村民的生命变奏。

海蒂是南非偏远的卡米斯山区的一名牧羊人。在她八十岁生日前夕，有人向她送来了迟到已久的补偿金——用以补偿她父亲在二战中的军旅生涯。唯一的问题是：这是一场骗局。当海蒂和邻居们苦等一笔永远不会到来的钱时，村里其他人则在寻找钻石、看橄榄球、玩多米诺骨牌，或沉入回忆。他们被往昔冲突的幽灵环绕，栖身于一座被南非社会遗忘的乡野小村，成为大大小小各种力量的牺牲品——但每个人都有着自己丰盈的内心世界。

这部纪录与剧情交融的混合之作以一系列「变奏」的结构呈现，采取了文学化的手法，由联合导演贾森·雅各布斯以画外音道出片中人物的所思所想。他现实中的祖母出演女主角海蒂，而这份亲密正是这部影片的核心——它满怀令人心碎的共情，兼具文化的独特质地、诗意的影像，以及米凯拉·阿丽莎·史密斯谱写的动人配乐。雅各布斯与德文·德尔马凭此片摘得鹿特丹最高奖，这是继二人于2024年在威尼斯首映的处女作《卡丽莎》之后的又一力作，确立了他们作为南非乃至全球影坛重要新声的地位。
highlight_zh: 鹿特丹金虎奖，导演亲奶奶主演，感人至深

title_en: Vertical Shapes in a Horizontal Landscape
title_zh: 横向风景中的纵向形态
synopsis_en:
Mark Jenkin travels along the south coast of England on a pilgrimage to the Dungeness home of artist and filmmaker Derek Jarman.

Evoking the artists who lived and worked on England’s south coast, this 2018 travelogue finds typically garrulous director Mark Jenkin en route to the home of none other than the great Derek Jarman, whose 1990 film The Garden inspired him to pick up his first camera. A lovely, melancholic slice of a forgotten world, captured on vivid super-8 film stock.
synopsis_zh:
马克·詹金沿着英格兰南部海岸旅行，踏上一场朝圣之旅，前往艺术家兼电影人德里克·贾曼位于邓杰内斯的家。

这部2018年的旅行随笔追忆了曾在英格兰南岸生活与创作的艺术家们，一如既往健谈的导演马克·詹金正在前往那位伟大的德里克·贾曼的居所——正是贾曼1990年的影片《花园》让他拿起了人生中的第一台摄影机。影片以浓烈鲜明的超8毫米胶片拍摄，是一曲献给逝去世界的动人而忧郁的挽歌。
highlight_zh: 超8胶片朝圣德里克·贾曼故居，追忆英南岸艺术魂

synopsis_en:
Kim Novak’s iconic dual performance in Alfred Hitchcock’s psychological thriller masterpiece, presented in glorious 70mm, has long been regarded as her best.

Former San Francisco detective John “Scottie” Ferguson (James Stewart) is hired by an old college acquaintance to trail his wife, Madeleine Elster (Kim Novak), claiming she might be possessed and suicidal. It’s not long before Scottie is deeply infatuated with his enigmatic mark, then shattered at her untimely death. In the grip of obsession, he meets a woman on the street, Judy Barton, whom he proceeds to make over in order to resurrect Madeleine as he remembers her – leading once again to tragedy.

Initially, Alfred Hitchcock wanted Vera Miles for the dual role of Madeleine and Judy in Vertigo. When she became unavailable, he instead hired Novak, at that time a darling of the American moviegoing public. Though she was only 24 at the time of the film’s production, her performance is that of a much wiser and more experienced actor: while the genius of Hitchcock is undeniably central to the film’s construction and Novak’s presentation within the frame, it’s the latter’s own complexity that brings nuance to the two distinct women she plays. The palpable chemistry between her and Stewart (again on display in Bell, Book and Candle, released the same year) makes the tragedy that unfolds in Vertigo all the more keenly felt.

Named after Simone de Beauvoir’s 1964 memoir, this dreamlike, experimental montage muses on bereavement.

Fifty years ago, Mary Stephen used her editor’s sensibility to meditate poetically on her mother’s death through still images, 16mm film and song. Hilda emerges in oneiric archival glimpses: her stylish youth; waltzing at home with Mary’s young-adult brother; the achingly empty hospital room where she died. Her daughter’s hand intrudes into the frame in a yearning, impossible caress. Originally, Stephen made only four copies of this short for her siblings, but this generous 2025 restoration shares her memories with everyone.
synopsis_zh:
片名取自西蒙娜·德·波伏娃1964年的回忆录，这部如梦似幻的实验蒙太奇作品，是一场关于丧亲之痛的沉思。

五十年前，玛丽·斯蒂芬以剪辑师特有的敏感，借由静态照片、16毫米胶片与歌声，为母亲的离世写下一首诗意的悼念。希尔达在梦境般的档案影像碎片中浮现：她时髦的青春年华、在家中与斯蒂芬正当年少的哥哥翩翩起舞、以及她咽气的那间空荡得令人心痛的病房。女儿的手探入画面，是一次充满渴望却终究无法企及的抚触。斯蒂芬当初只为兄弟姐妹们制作了四份拷贝，而这份慷慨的2025年修复版，让她的记忆得以与所有人分享。
highlight_zh: 剪辑师的私影像悼亡诗，2025修复重现

synopsis_en:
Scream queen Maika Monroe plays an unhinged governess terrorising a posh 19th-century family.

In 1858, Winifred Notty arrives on the Gothic doorstep of Ensor House, a mansion on the Yorkshire moors. Wealthy John and Emily Pounds hire the well-spoken but definitely odd woman as a governess for their young son and daughter. Courtesy of an inner demon she calls “Fred”, the impulsive carnage that’s always followed in Winifred’s wake doesn’t take long to pop up gruesomely again. Given the Poundses’ own depraved lineage, has Winifred finally found her people? But, then again, she is a very unreliable narrator …

Zachary Wigon’s gory Gothic romp – adapted by Virginia Feito from her own novel – is Wuthering Heights slash Jane Eyre slash American Psycho. Demolishing her final-girl persona, Maika Monroe (It Follows, MIFF 2014; Longlegs), exploits eat-the-rich sentiments as her gleeful sociopath, aided by Ruth Wilson (Dark River, MIFF 2018) and Jason Isaacs (The White Lotus) as Winifred’s loathsome employers. There are also fun supporting performances from Thomasin McKenzie (The Justice of Bunny King, MIFF 2021) as the nursemaid who befriends Winifred and Jacobi Jupe (Hamnet) and Evie Templeton (Wednesday) as the Pounds children. What are they learning from their super-callous, pugilistic, exterminatory governess?
synopsis_zh:
尖叫女王玛卡·梦露饰演一位精神失常的家庭女教师，在 19 世纪把一户上流人家搅得天翻地覆。

1858 年，温妮弗雷德·诺蒂来到恩索宅邸——一座矗立在约克郡荒原上的哥特式庄园——门前。富有的约翰与艾米莉·庞兹夫妇雇下这个谈吐得体、却又古怪得离谱的女人，让她照看年幼的儿女。温妮弗雷德体内住着一个被她唤作"弗雷德"的心魔，向来如影随形、说来就来的血腥暴行，很快又血淋淋地爆发了。而庞兹一家自己的血脉也堕落不堪——温妮弗雷德是不是终于找到了同类？可话说回来，她本就是个极不可靠的叙述者……

扎克瑞·维贡这部血浆四溅的哥特狂欢，由维吉尼亚·费托改编自其本人小说，堪称《呼啸山庄》×《简·爱》×《美国精神病人》。玛卡·梦露（《它在身后》，2014 年 MIFF;《长腿》）彻底颠覆了自己"最终女孩"的银幕形象，以一个乐在其中的反社会者尽情释放"仇富"情绪;鲁思·威尔森（《暗河》，2018 年 MIFF）与杰森·艾萨克斯（《白莲花度假村》）出演她那对令人生厌的雇主。配角同样出彩：托玛辛·麦肯齐（《善良的邦尼金》，2021 年 MIFF）饰演与温妮弗雷德结为好友的保姆，雅各比·朱佩（《哈姆尼特》）与伊薇·邓普顿（《星期三》）则饰演庞兹家的孩子。跟着这位冷酷好斗、"斩尽杀绝"的家教，他们又会学到些什么呢？
highlight_zh: 《它在身后》梦露颠覆最终女孩，哥特血浆版《美国精神病人》

synopsis_en:
This painterly, profound adaptation of Georges Bizet’s iconic opera – told from the perspective of children – wowed Cannes crowds with its incredible use of colour.

Salva (voiced by Milo Machado-Graner, Goodbye, Cruel World, MIFF 2026), Belén, Piranha and La Bola are street kids scrapping for survival on the streets of 19th-century Seville. Salva works for the blind knife grinder Antonio, who foretells a mystical prophecy of the death of free-spirited young Romani woman Carmen, who will be killed by the soldier who loves her. When Salva leaps into action in the hope of saving Carmen from her fate, his actions instead set the prophesy into motion.

From Jacques Feyder’s Carmen (MIFF 1981) and Jean-Luc Godard’s Prénom Carmen (MIFF 1985) through to Mark Dornford-May’s U-Carmen eKhayelitsha (MIFF 2005), Georges Bizet’s iconic opera has proved a fertile text for cinematic adaptations. Animator Sébastien Laudenbach (The Girl Without Hands, MIFF 2016) offers a unique take on the classic text, turning the narrative away from the star-cross’d lovers at the centre of its tragedy and instead presenting it from the perspective of children watching fate unfold. Filled with expressive lines and evocative colours, this gorgeously hand-painted work is a sheer delight for those who treasure the animated artform.
synopsis_zh:
这部充满绘画质感、意蕴深远的作品改编自乔治·比才的经典歌剧《卡门》，以孩子的视角重述故事，凭借惊艳的色彩运用征服了戛纳的观众。

萨尔瓦（米洛·马查多-格拉内配音，代表作《残酷世界，再见》，2026 年墨尔本电影节展映）、贝伦、皮兰哈和拉·博拉是一群在 19 世纪塞维利亚街头挣扎求生的流浪儿童。萨尔瓦为盲眼磨刀匠安东尼奥打工，后者道出一则神秘预言：自由不羁的年轻罗姆女子卡门终将殒命，而杀死她的正是那个爱她的士兵。当萨尔瓦挺身而出、想要让卡门逃脱命运时，他的举动反而将预言一步步推向应验。

从雅克·费戴尔的《卡门》（1981 年墨尔本电影节）、让-吕克·戈达尔的《卡门的姓名》（1985 年墨尔本电影节），到马克·多恩福德-梅的《卡门在卡雅利沙》（2005 年墨尔本电影节），乔治·比才这部经典歌剧一直是电影改编取之不竭的源泉。动画大师塞巴斯蒂安·劳登巴赫（《失手的女孩》，2016 年墨尔本电影节）为这一经典文本奉上独特诠释，将叙事焦点从悲剧核心那对命运多舛的恋人身上移开，转而透过一群旁观命运展开的孩子之眼来呈现。线条灵动传神，色彩意韵盎然，这部美轮美奂的纯手绘之作，是每一位珍视动画艺术的观众不容错过的至臻享受。
highlight_zh: 戛纳惊艳，纯手绘重绘《卡门》，色彩美到窒息

synopsis_en:
A nocturnal descent into the lives of Afghan people smugglers living in the forests of the Balkan route, this Venice award winner is a profound, immersive experience.

In the darkness of a forest in northern Serbia, right by the border of Hungary, Afghan passeurs gather around a flickering fire: smoking, baking bread and discussing the best corridors for smuggling people across the border. As gunshots resound in the distance and loudspeakers blast periodic warnings from the Hungarian side, these tiny figures linger in the shadow of the European Union.

Taking place almost entirely in darkness – illuminated only by firelight, torches, phones and distant passing headlights – this immersive, experiential work of direct cinema takes us into the world of the men who facilitate safe passage along the Balkan route. Seeing the work of their often-reviled subjects as “a response to and a consequence of Europe’s exclusionary policies”, Italian directors Federico Cammarata and Filippo Foscarini covertly film from a distance, danger forever in the air. With its nocturnal imagery and heightened quality of sound, Waking Hours plays almost like a slow-burning horror movie: plunging viewers into an unsettling night; cultivating a sense of mounting fear; asking us to imagine what’s lurking outside the frame.

A trailblazing ASL interpreter trains fast-fingered protégés to embody live acoustics in this paean to the Deaf community’s bond with music.

Amber Galloway specialises in live American Sign Language interpretation for music festivals and concerts. You might recognise her hip-hop swagger from viral online videos: she captures complex instrumentation and wordplay with her whole body, using facial expressions to evoke tones and moods. Now, this accessibility trailblazer is training new ASL music interpreters, including striving young Puerto Rican father Julian Ortiz and Angela “AV” Vilavong, a queer Laotian from Oakland. Can they master this complex skill in time to join Galloway onstage at Austin City Limits Music Festival?

As her students wrestle with live interpreting’s complex cognitive demands and poetic creative choices, Galloway’s pedagogical flair is treated with due admiration by French directorial duo Vanessa Dumont and Nicolas Davenel. For their part, Ortiz’s and Vilavong’s gifts are also evident (the former went on to live-interpret Bad Bunny’s Super Bowl half-time show, while the latter has been onstage with Céline Dion and The Chemical Brothers). But this sensitive documentary deals with tough emotions, too: Galloway is grieving her son Elijah, who was himself a promising interpreter, as she tries to pull his surviving twin Joshua from a depressive spiral. Equally, we see how Galloway and her protégés’ proudly queer, ancestral and Deaf identities help them express music’s life-affirming power. Turns out the body doesn’t just keep the score; it keeps the beat.

This poignant Cannes-premiering animation traces the friendship and falling-out of two boys after an incident at school changes their lives forever.

Attending the third grade in rural Japan, awkward Tsubasa has trouble connecting with his peers. Luckily, the gregarious Gyotaro invites him to play, and they quickly become firm friends. However, an unexpected event not only drives a wedge between the pair but also destabilises the school’s social hierarchy. These disruptions go on to mark both boys in profound ways, through their adolescent years and well into adulthood.

Bowing at Cannes Directors’ Fortnight, Kohei Kadowaki’s stunning and stirringly told directorial debut recalls Hirokazu Kore-eda’s Monster (MIFF 2023) in its preoccupation with the impacts of childhood cruelty and Akira Kurosawa’s Rashomon in its bisected structure representing each protagonist’s point of view, filling in gaps of memory and offering alternative perspectives on proceedings. Rendered using a dynamic, at times dreamlike blend of rotoscoped and hand-drawn 2D animation, We Are Aliens is a beautiful, bittersweet meditation on lost innocence and the internecine fractures that can complicate the experience of growing up.
synopsis_zh:
这部入围戛纳首映的动画感人至深，讲述两个男孩之间的友谊，以及一场校园意外彻底改变他们人生后的决裂。

在日本乡下读小学三年级的翼（Tsubasa）性格笨拙，很难融入同龄人。所幸开朗合群的鱼太郎（Gyotaro）主动邀他一起玩，两人很快成了要好的朋友。然而，一桩突如其来的意外不仅在两人之间埋下了嫌隙，也动摇了整所学校的社交秩序。这些变故以深刻的方式在两个男孩身上留下印记，一路延续过青春期，直到成年。

门胁功平（Kohei Kadowaki）这部惊艳而动人的导演处女作曾亮相戛纳导演双周单元。它对童年残酷所造成之影响的执着追问，令人想起是枝裕和的《怪物》（MIFF 2023）；而以两段结构分别呈现每位主角视角、填补记忆空白、为同一事件提供不同解读的手法，又与黑泽明的《罗生门》遥相呼应。影片以转描（rotoscope）与手绘 2D 动画动感十足、时而如梦似幻地交织呈现，《我们都是外星人》是一曲优美而苦甜参半的挽歌，追念逝去的纯真，也审视成长过程中那些足以令人两败俱伤的内在裂痕。
highlight_zh: 戛纳导演双周惊艳处女作，《罗生门》式双视角动画

synopsis_en:
The first Singaporean film to screen in competition in the Berlinale’s 70-plus-year history, Anthony Chen’s latest feature is an epic, tender tale of biological and found family.

Having finished military service, Junyang is unsure what comes next – though this doesn’t stop him from disparaging his father Boon Kiat and the latter’s humble noodle stand. Junyang’s girlfriend Lydia, a piano prodigy with university in her sights, faces her mother’s distaste for their relationship. Meanwhile, Boon Kiat grows fond of ‘beer auntie’ Bee Hwa, who peddles alcohol near his stall. As life throws one curveball after another, it’s not long before these four individuals become deeply intertwined.

Reuniting with Koh Jia Ler and Yeo Yann Yann – who both starred in Ilo Ilo (MIFF 2013) and Wet Season (MIFF 2020), the earlier entries in the director’s ‘Growing Up’ trilogy – Anthony Chen again dissects the intricacies of human relationships, this time also critiquing a Singapore where consumerism and class foment divisions. Recalling Edward Yang’s explorations of family, Chen’s masterful portrait dramatises the yearning masked by tough exteriors and devotion, however imperfectly expressed, for loved ones. Touching and quietly thought-provoking, We Are All Strangers challenges its own title, revealing how shared burdens can break down the walls that keep us apart.
synopsis_zh:
作为柏林电影节七十余年历史上首部入围主竞赛单元的新加坡影片，陈哲艺的这部新作是一则关于血缘亲情与后天羁绊的动人史诗。

刚服完兵役的俊阳对未来一片茫然——可这并不妨碍他看不起父亲文杰和那个不起眼的面摊。俊阳的女友莉迪亚是一位志在考取大学的钢琴天才，却要面对母亲对这段感情的嫌弃。与此同时，文杰渐渐对在摊位旁卖酒的“啤酒阿姨”美华动了心。命运接二连三地抛来变数，这四个人很快便深深纠缠在了一起。

陈哲艺再度携手许家乐与杨雁雁——两人都曾出演导演“成长”三部曲的前两部《爸妈不在家》（MIFF 2013）与《热带雨》（MIFF 2020）——继续剖解人际关系的幽微曲折，这一次更把批判的目光投向一个被消费主义与阶级鸿沟撕裂的新加坡。影片令人想起杨德昌对家庭的探照，陈哲艺以大师笔触，将坚硬外壳下的渴望、以及那份纵然笨拙却真挚的、对亲人的牵挂一一戏剧化地呈现。《我们都是陌生人》温情脉脉又引人深思，它挑战着自己的片名，揭示出共担重负如何能拆掉那些让我们彼此疏离的高墙。
highlight_zh: 陈哲艺“成长”三部曲收官，首部入围柏林主竞赛的新加坡片

synopsis_en:
They were ordinary teen girls forced to form a band by their dad – and, thanks to one unlistenable (to some!) album, became icons of outsider art.

Austin Wiggin’s mother had prophesied his daughters would become a famous band – so, in 1965, he decided to make it happen. He pulled Dot, Helen and Betty out of school; handed them instruments they couldn’t play; suggested they write songs without the first clue how; and named them The Shaggs. For years, they (occasionally accompanied by younger sister Rachel) endured weekly heckling at local gigs in Fremont, New Hampshire, before recording an album, 1969’s Philosophy of the World. When Austin died in 1975, the sisters could finally live regular lives … until the 90s, when they learned The Shaggs had become a cult band whose tastemaker fans included Frank Zappa, Patti Smith and Kurt Cobain. Their grandmother’s prediction had come true, in the most ironic way possible.

One Shaggs evangelist is veteran comedy director Ken Kwapis (Vibes, MIFF 2019; Malcolm in the Middle). His film explores the strangeness of the sisters’ wildly out-of-tune guitars, eccentric rhythms and unorthodox chords, tracing their path from “the worst band ever” to outsider music stars, while music experts champion their intuitive brilliance. Meanwhile, gentle, bemused surviving Shaggs Dot and Betty muse on the phenomenon they’ve been caught up in.

In this scathing, multi-awarded, newly 4K-restored 1974 drama – regarded by Lino Brocka as his “first novel” – an affluent teen disillusioned by privilege befriends two outcasts.

Junior is the only son of Cesar, a lawyer, a failed politician and the richest man in town. This wealth has afforded Junior an ultra-comfortable life – one he spends with his similarly well-off friends and girlfriend. After a series of upsetting discoveries, however, Junior distances himself from this privileged bubble, eventually finding solace in his friendships with leper Berto and ‘village idiot’ Kuala. But the teenager soon learns that beneath the strictures of small-town respectability lurks all manner of ruthlessness.

A pioneering work of the Second Golden Age of Philippine Cinema, set in the province where director Lino Brocka (Manila in the Claws of Light, MIFF 2013) grew up, Weighed but Found Wanting offers a contemporary twist on the biblical account of hubris, hypocrisy and judgement its title references. Released at the peak of Ferdinand Marcos’s ghastly martial-law regime, it was a critical and commercial success, winning six FAMAS Awards and launching the career of “King of Philippine Drama” Christopher de Leon, who debuted here alongside luminaries Eddie Garcia and Lolita Rodriguez. With echoes of Fellini, Bogdanovich and José Rizal’s magnum opus Noli Me Tángere, Brocka’s masterpiece champions empathy and anti-establishment principles during a period of large-scale repression; now, in one of the film’s first international screenings – this brand-new 4K restoration having recently premiered at Il Cinema Ritrovato – it can be discovered afresh.
synopsis_zh:
在这部言辞犀利、屡获殊荣、近期全新 4K 修复的 1974 年剧情片中——林诺·布洛卡视其为自己的"第一部长篇小说"——一个对特权生活心生幻灭的富家少年，与两名被社会遗弃者结下友谊。

小胡尼奥尔是塞萨尔的独子，父亲是一名律师、一位失意的政客，也是镇上最富有的人。优渥的家境为胡尼奥尔换来了极度舒适的生活，他与同样家境殷实的朋友和女友一同挥霍时光。然而，在经历了一连串令人不安的发现之后，胡尼奥尔逐渐疏远了这个特权的泡影，最终在与麻风病人贝尔托和"村中傻子"库阿拉的友谊中寻得慰藉。但这位少年很快意识到，小镇体面的清规戒律之下，潜藏着形形色色的冷酷无情。

作为菲律宾电影第二黄金时代的开创之作，本片取景于导演林诺·布洛卡（《光爪之下的马尼拉》，2013 年墨尔本国际电影节）成长的外省。片名典出《圣经》中关于傲慢、伪善与审判的记载，而影片则为这一古老母题赋予了当代的诠释。它上映于费迪南德·马科斯骇人的军事戒严统治登峰造极之时，却在口碑与票房上双双告捷，一举斩获六项 FAMAS 大奖，并开启了"菲律宾剧情片之王"克里斯托弗·德·莱昂的演艺生涯——他在本片中首次登场，与埃迪·加西亚、罗丽塔·罗德里格斯等巨星同台。影片隐约可见费里尼、博格达诺维奇以及何塞·黎刹的巨著《不许犯我》的回响，布洛卡的这部杰作在大规模压迫的年代高扬同理心与反建制的信念；如今，作为该片首批国际放映之一——这部全新 4K 修复版新近于博洛尼亚电影修复节首映——它得以被重新发现。
highlight_zh: 菲律宾电影大师布洛卡代表作，6项FAMAS奖，4K修复

title_en: What Do the Maknines Dream Of
synopsis_en:
PRESENTED BY

Two young Algerians spend one last sweet day together, hoping to sell a caged bird before flying away to seek new lives.

Lose yourself in the boisterous, labyrinthine streets of Algiers with Kacim – who raises and sells goldfinches, or maknines – and his dear friend Malek, both of whom are about to cross the Mediterranean in pursuit of music careers in France. As they wander affectionately and dance dreamily in the club, the caged bird’s song evokes their warring feelings of escape and captivity.
synopsis_zh:
两个阿尔及利亚青年共度最后一个甜蜜的白日，盼着把笼中的鸟卖掉，便要远走高飞，去寻找新的人生。

随卡西姆一同迷失在阿尔及尔喧闹而曲折的街巷里——他饲养并贩卖金翅雀，当地人称之为「麦克宁」——身边还有挚友马莱克，两人都即将横渡地中海，去法国追逐音乐梦想。他们满含深情地游荡，在夜店里如梦般起舞，而笼中鸟的歌声，唤起了他们心中出走与困守相互撕扯的情绪。
highlight_zh: 阿尔及利亚青春挽歌，笼中鸟映照出走与困守的两难

synopsis_en:
In the quiet chaos of early motherhood, a young woman slips between roles – girl, mother, daughter – that each feel a little out of reach.

Through slice-of-life vignettes, a young mother grapples with her own upbringing to navigate parenthood’s shifting demands, and to find moments for herself. Jess Barclay Lawton’s (Living Room, MIFF 2019; Building Bridges, MIFF 2014) sensitivity for character studies evolves strikingly into this documentary whose slow tenderness testifies to the depth of trust between director and subject.
synopsis_zh:
在初为人母的静默混乱中，一位年轻女性在多重身份间游移——女孩、母亲、女儿——每一个角色都仿佛触手难及。

透过一幕幕生活切片，这位年轻的母亲一边直面自己的成长经历，一边摸索为人父母不断变化的要求，并努力为自己寻得片刻喘息。杰斯·巴克利·劳顿（《起居室》，2019 年墨尔本电影节；《搭建桥梁》，2014 年墨尔本电影节）对人物研究的敏锐，在这部纪录片中演化出惊人的深度；影片舒缓而温柔的笔触，见证了导演与被摄者之间深厚的信任。
highlight_zh: 初为人母的温柔自白，信任凝成的私密影像

synopsis_en:
Strap in for a meditative ride through Australia’s subculture of salt lake speed racing, as daredevil drivers pierce the vast emptiness.

Drivers travel thousands of kilometres to Lake Gairdner in outback South Australia to break Australia’s land speed record on kilometres of hard-packed salt. Captured immaculately on 16mm film with huge telephoto lenses, self-built jet-propelled cars hurtle through soupy mirages of blistering heat to a hypnotic soundtrack. A strangely ethereal silence radiates as everything but momentum falls away.
synopsis_zh:
系好安全带，跟随这场冥想般的旅程，走进澳大利亚盐湖极速竞速的亚文化世界——亡命车手们在无垠的空旷中破空疾驰。

车手们跋涉数千公里，来到南澳内陆的盖尔德纳湖，在绵延数公里、坚硬密实的盐层上冲击澳大利亚陆地极速纪录。影片以16毫米胶片和超长焦镜头精美捕捉，那些自制的喷气动力赛车在灼人热浪蒸腾出的迷蒙幻景中呼啸穿行，配以催眠般的原声音乐。当一切都消隐、只剩下速度本身时，一种奇异而空灵的寂静油然弥漫开来。
highlight_zh: 16毫米胶片捕捉盐湖极速竞速，光影与寂静交织的冥想之旅

synopsis_en:
The winner of the main prize at CPH:DOX, this impressionistic hybrid documentary follows a teenage girl on a quest to find a ceremonial skirt.

Fourteen-year-old Qihuo has just had her first period. In her Nuosu community in south-west China’s remote, mountainous Liangshan district, this milestone signals that she is ready to work in the factories – and, even, for arranged marriage. Tradition also calls for menarche to be commemorated with a “skirt-changing” ceremony, in which garments signifying childhood are replaced. With her parents both working miles away from their village, Qihuo shares her secret with her two best friends; and, together, the girls set out across the mountains to find her a new skirt.

Described by director Dongnan Chen as a “reluctant coming-of-age film”, Whispers in May is a poignant documentary – created collaboratively with its protagonists – that could easily be mistaken for a fictional work. As the girls traverse the rugged landscape, trawl through fabrics at local markets and get their palms read by fortune tellers, we witness this precious and fleeting moment of their lives between childhood and adulthood, between freedom and responsibility. In awarding this dreamlike time capsule, the CPH:DOX jury praised it as “a modern-day fairytale that heeds monsters and factories, alike”.
synopsis_zh:
本片荣获哥本哈根国际纪录片电影节（CPH:DOX）最高奖项，是一部印象派的混合纪录片，讲述一名少女踏上寻找一条祭祀裙的旅程。

十四岁的其伙刚刚来了初潮。在中国西南部偏远山区凉山彝族（诺苏）的社群里，这一成长的里程碑意味着她已经可以进厂打工——甚至可以谈婚论嫁。按照传统，初潮还要以一场“换裙礼”来纪念，用新衣替换象征童年的旧装。父母都在离村庄很远的地方务工，其伙把这个秘密告诉了两位最要好的朋友；三个女孩一起翻山越岭，为她寻找一条新裙。

导演陈东楠称这是一部“不情愿的成长电影”。《五月私语》是一部动人的纪录片——与片中主角共同创作而成——真实得几乎会被误认为剧情片。当女孩们穿行于崎岖的山野、在乡镇集市翻找布料、请算命人为自己看手相时，我们目睹了她们生命中这段珍贵而短暂的时光：介于童年与成年之间，介于自由与责任之间。CPH:DOX 评审团在授奖时称赞它是“一则同时容纳了怪兽与工厂的当代童话”。
highlight_zh: CPH:DOX最高奖，凉山彝族少女寻裙的成长童话

synopsis_en:
This César Best Documentary–winning French box-office smash, with a score featuring Warren Ellis, is a stunning visual and aural immersion into the misty Vosges mountains.

Three generations of nature lovers set out to find the western capercaillie amid the densely wooded and visually spectacular Vosges, a French mountain range close to the German border. Award-winning wildlife photographer and filmmaker Vincent Munier joins his 12-year-old son and father, environmentalist Michel Munier, on a journey through the forest in search of the endangered bird. But even though the capercaillie might be rare here, other wildlife are not. Munier’s camera captures bears, deer, owls and more, in all their visual and sonic glory.

After winning awards in the National History Museum Wildlife Photographer of the Year competition three years in a row in his youth, Vincent Munier has in recent times also turned his hand to the moving image. His first feature documentary, The Velvet Queen, charted his quest to find the elusive snow leopard; Whispers in the Woods brings him back to where he grew up, and to a far more personal quest. While Munier’s images are extraordinary, what sets this film apart is how they combine with the sound design (which also scored a César) to highlight the unique aural vibrations of these woods – all underscored by Warren Ellis, Dom La Nena and Rosemary Standley’s score, which itself earned a Lumière Award for Best Music. An exquisite love letter to family and to the wonderful world we live in, Whispers in the Woods is best experienced on the big screen.
synopsis_zh:
这部荣获凯撒奖最佳纪录片、在法国创下票房佳绩的作品，配乐由沃伦·埃利斯（Warren Ellis）等人操刀，是一场深入雾气缭绕的孚日山脉、震撼视听的沉浸之旅。

三代自然爱好者踏上寻找西方松鸡的旅程，走进法德边境附近、林木葱郁而景色壮丽的法国孚日山脉。屡获殊荣的野生动物摄影师兼电影人文森特·穆尼耶（Vincent Munier）与他 12 岁的儿子、以及身为环保人士的父亲米歇尔·穆尼耶（Michel Munier）一同穿越森林，追寻这种濒危鸟类的踪迹。松鸡在这里或许难得一见，其他野生动物却并不稀少——穆尼耶的镜头捕捉到熊、鹿、猫头鹰等众多生灵，尽展它们的视觉之美与声音之妙。

年少时，穆尼耶曾连续三年在英国自然史博物馆「年度野生动物摄影师」大赛中获奖，近年他又将才华转向动态影像。他的首部长片纪录片《雪豹》记录了他追寻神出鬼没的雪豹的历程；而《森林的低语》则将他带回成长的故土，踏上一段更为私人的旅程。穆尼耶的影像固然非凡，但真正让本片脱颖而出的，是这些画面与音效设计（同样斩获凯撒奖）的交融，凸显出这片森林独一无二的声音震颤——再辅以沃伦·埃利斯、多姆·拉·内娜（Dom La Nena）与罗斯玛丽·斯坦德利（Rosemary Standley）的配乐，这段配乐本身也赢得了卢米埃奖最佳音乐。这是一封写给家人、也写给我们所栖居的美妙世界的动人情书，《森林的低语》最宜在大银幕上细细品味。
highlight_zh: 凯撒奖最佳纪录片，《雪豹》导演回归故土的沉浸自然之旅

synopsis_en:
A quiet musician accused of a fraudulent marriage must make a profound decision, but his inner truth remains elusive.

A stoic wedding-band keyboardist is dragged from a gig to face court: the adult children of his much older wife say he exploited their mother’s dementia to sell her properties, but will drop fraud charges if he divorces her. Like the frankincense he burns for his ailing wife, truths about devotion, dignity and loss waft mysteriously through Mo Harawe’s (The Village Next to Paradise, MIFF 2024) observational drama, supported by Cate Blanchett’s Displacement Film Fund.
synopsis_zh:
一位沉默寡言的音乐人被指控骗婚，他必须做出一个艰难的抉择，然而他内心的真相却始终难以捉摸。

一名沉静内敛的婚礼乐队键盘手在演出途中被带上法庭：他年长许多的妻子，其成年子女指控他趁母亲患有痴呆症之机，诱使她变卖名下房产；但只要他与母亲离婚，他们便愿意撤销诈骗指控。正如他为病中妻子点燃的乳香，关于忠诚、尊严与失去的种种真相，在莫·哈拉维（《天堂隔壁的村庄》，2024 年墨尔本国际电影节）这部冷静观察式的剧情片中，如烟般幽微飘散。本片由凯特·布兰切特设立的流离影像基金支持拍摄。
highlight_zh: 布兰切特基金力挺，冷峻观察一桩骗婚疑云下的忠诚与尊严

synopsis_en:
PRESENTED BY

Welcome to the Masters of Musical Whistling, where the competitors require monumental skill, massive determination and a mighty wind.

The Masters of Musical Whistling is an international competition that celebrates, well, whistling – at an elite level. Every two years, dedicated and supremely talented whistlers converge on Hollywood to showcase their art and, hopefully, whistle up a coveted trophy. The 2023 competitors include global whistling superstar Molly Lewis, who features on the Barbie soundtrack; Spaniard Ayna, who’s inherited her circus-performing parent’s charismatic stage presence; and Yuki, a Japanese whistling prodigy sick of being the runner-up. They’re all here because Carole Anne Kaufman, founder of the Masters and two-time world champion, wants to change how the world thinks about whistling.

Thanks to Australian director Christopher Nelius, she just might. With genuine affection and care, Nelius (Girls Can’t Surf; Buoy, MIFF 2017) documents high and low notes for Kaufman and her competitors, on and offstage. Nelius clearly understands the visual language of the mockumentary; but while many of his subjects wouldn’t be out of place in a Christopher Guest film, there’s no mockery here. Instead, he respectfully centres this community’s incredible artistry through all the tears, bruised egos, heartaches and soaring joys. Funny, poignant and wonderfully weird, Whistle is a treat from start to finish – and, perhaps, just what the world needs right now.
synopsis_zh:
欢迎来到"音乐口哨大师赛"——在这里，选手们需要非凡的技巧、坚定的决心，还有一口气势磅礴的气息。

"音乐口哨大师赛"是一项国际赛事，专门礼赞这门技艺——没错，就是吹口哨——而且是顶尖水准的口哨。每隔两年，一群执着而天赋异禀的口哨高手齐聚好莱坞，一展绝艺，争夺那座人人垂涎的奖杯。2023 年的参赛者中，有蜚声全球的口哨巨星莫莉·刘易斯（她曾参与《芭比》原声带的录制）；有继承了马戏团父母超凡舞台魅力的西班牙姑娘艾娜；还有厌倦了年年屈居亚军的日本口哨神童由纪。他们齐聚于此，都是因为大赛创办人、两届世界冠军卡罗尔·安妮·考夫曼，想要改变世人对口哨的看法。

多亏了澳大利亚导演克里斯托弗·尼利厄斯，她或许真能如愿。尼利厄斯（代表作《女孩不会冲浪》《浮标》，后者入选 2017 年墨尔本电影节）怀着真挚的深情与关切，记录下考夫曼与选手们台前幕后的高音与低谷。他显然深谙伪纪录片的视觉语言；尽管片中不少人物放到克里斯托弗·盖斯特的电影里也毫不违和，这里却没有半分嘲弄。相反，他满怀敬意地聚焦这个群体令人惊叹的艺术才华，将他们的泪水、受挫的自尊、心碎与飞扬的喜悦一一呈现。《口哨》诙谐动人、奇妙无比，从头到尾都是一场享受——或许，也正是此刻这个世界所需要的。
highlight_zh: 《芭比》口哨巨星领衔，笑中带泪的暖心伪纪录片

synopsis_en:
Legendary fashion photographer and photojournalist William Klein exploded into narrative filmmaking with this Prix Jean Vigo–winning mockumentary, now a cult record of groovy 1960s mod fashion.

Trailed by a gormless French film crew through Paris, American model Polly (played by director William Klein’s favourite model, Dorothy McGowan) is a mod Alice weaving her way through a stylishly shambolic Wonderland. Right from the famous opening sequence – a runway show in a Gaudi-esque colosseum in which fashionistas coo over models encased in sheet metal – Klein is fascinated with fashion’s theatre of the absurd. His satirical eye finds something solemn, even religious, in the banal pronouncements of fashion editors and the nonsensical questions of TV interviewers, while Polly seems bemused to find herself an object of worship for trend-hungry teens and lecherous men.

Two years before he would make the unnerving American satire Mr. Freedom, Klein skewers the rarefied world of Parisian haute couture and its hangers-on from his privileged position as one of the most subversive photographers to appear in American Vogue. With his sights on the commercialisation of the ‘60s “youthquake”, he mashes up the French New Wave with Beatles caper A Hard Day’s Night, using playful animation that anticipates Monty Python’s Flying Circus.

“[This] gleeful satire on the fashion industry underlined [William Klein’s] reputation as a brilliant, iconoclastic photographer” – The Guardian

2K Digitisation
synopsis_zh:
传奇时尚摄影师、摄影记者威廉·克莱因以这部荣获让·维果奖的伪纪录片惊艳跻身叙事电影领域，如今它已成为记录 1960 年代摩登时尚风潮的邪典之作。

美国模特波莉（由克莱因最钟爱的模特多萝西·麦高恩饰演）被一支笨手笨脚的法国摄制组一路追拍，穿行于巴黎街头，宛如一位摩登版的爱丽丝，在时髦而混乱的仙境中穿梭游走。从那段著名的开场戏——在一座高迪风格的斗兽场里举行的时装秀上，时尚精英们对着一身金属板包裹的模特啧啧称奇——克莱因便流露出对时尚这场荒诞剧场的痴迷。他以讽刺之眼，从时尚编辑们的陈词滥调与电视主持人的荒唐提问中，捕捉到某种庄严甚至近乎宗教般的意味，而波莉则一脸困惑地发现，自己竟成了追赶潮流的少年与好色之徒顶礼膜拜的对象。

两年后，克莱因将拍出令人不安的美国讽刺片《自由先生》。而此刻的他，凭借身为登上美国版《Vogue》最具颠覆性的摄影师之一的独特位置，对巴黎高级定制时装那个矫饰的世界及其追随者极尽嘲讽。他将矛头对准 60 年代“青年震荡”的商业化，把法国新浪潮与披头士的胡闹喜剧《一夜狂欢》搅拌交融，并运用俏皮的动画手法，预示了日后《巨蟒剧团之飞翔的马戏团》的风格。

“这部对时尚产业尽情调侃的讽刺之作，再次印证了（威廉·克莱因）作为一位才华横溢、离经叛道的摄影师的声誉。”——《卫报》

2K 数字修复版
highlight_zh: 让·维果奖伪纪录片，60年代摩登时尚邪典，新浪潮遇上披头士

synopsis_en:
Olivia Colman and Alexander Skarsgård lead this cheeky, star-studded and irresistible fable about a cranky fisherwoman and the sexy handwoven husband she wishes for.

In a seaside village lost to time, a fisherwoman delivers the food relied on by the townsfolk, who barely tolerate her cantankerous presence. Unexpectedly named the next to wed and sick of the disdain, she commissions the local basketmaker to weave her the perfect husband, and he goes above and beyond. When you’re married to the hottest and handiest man around, however, jealousies inevitably flare up.

Adapting Ursula Wills-Jones’s 2008 short story, this Sundance-premiering twist on a period love story – directed by Australian-born Eleanor Wilson and filmmaking (as well as real-life) partner Alex Huston Fischer, and including MIFF Accelerator alum David Michôd among its producers – is at once ingenious and charmingly irreverent. But it’s no straw-man depiction of middle-aged sexual wish fulfilment: the film uses its whimsical premise to critique small-mindedness, superstition and overzealous conservatism. Olivia Colman goes all-in for a role that recalls her fiery turn in The Favourite, while Alexander Skarsgård (Lee, MIFF 2024) is magnetic as a creature with more humanity than his human counterparts – testament to the practical effects by Joe Dunckley (Avatar) and WETA Workshop. Capped off with transportive cinematography by Oscar winner Lol Crawley (The Brutalist) and counting Peter Dinklage (The Toxic Avenger, MIFF 2025), Richard E. Grant and Melbourne’s own Elizabeth Debicki (The Tale, MIFF 2018) among its cast, Wicker is a fantasy romance that asks whether we truly know our heart’s desire.
synopsis_zh:
奥利维娅·科尔曼与亚历山大·斯卡斯加德领衔主演，一则俏皮、星光熠熠、令人无法抗拒的寓言：一个脾气暴躁的渔妇，和她亲手许愿编织出来的性感丈夫。

在一座被时间遗忘的海边村庄里，渔妇为镇上居民送来赖以为生的食物，人们却几乎无法忍受她那副乖戾模样。她意外被点名成为下一个要出嫁的人，又受够了旁人的白眼，于是委托当地的编篮匠为她编织一个完美的丈夫——而匠人的手艺远远超出了她的期待。然而，当你嫁给了方圆之内最帅气、最能干的男人，妒火终究难免熊熊燃起。

本片改编自厄休拉·威尔斯-琼斯2008年的短篇小说，是一则在圣丹斯电影节首映、别出心裁的古装爱情故事，由澳大利亚出生的埃莉诺·威尔逊与她的创作搭档（也是现实中的伴侣）亚历克斯·休斯顿·费舍尔联合执导，制片团队中还包括MIFF Accelerator计划的往届学员大卫·米肖。影片既巧思满满，又透着一股可爱的离经叛道。但它绝非对中年性幻想的浅薄描摹：它借这个天马行空的设定，讽刺了世人的狭隘、迷信与过度守旧。奥利维娅·科尔曼倾情投入，让人想起她在《宠儿》中那个火爆的角色；亚历山大·斯卡斯加德（《李》，MIFF 2024）则魅力四射，饰演一个比周遭凡人更具人性的造物——这也得益于乔·邓克利（《阿凡达》）与维塔工作室的实景特效。奥斯卡得主洛尔·克劳利（《粗野派》）的摄影更令人沉醉其间，演员阵容还囊括彼得·丁克拉奇（《毒液复仇者》，MIFF 2025）、理查德·E·格兰特，以及墨尔本本土女星伊丽莎白·德比茨基（《故事》，MIFF 2018）。《藤编良人》是一部奇幻爱情片，它叩问的是：我们真的了解自己内心所渴望的吗？
highlight_zh: 科尔曼、斯卡斯加德领衔，圣丹斯首映奇幻爱情寓言

synopsis_en:
A Melbourne doctor spiralling into full midlife crisis sets out on a primal pilgrimage into the Victorian Alps.

Allie’s life seems ideal from the outside: she’s a doctor, living with her teenage daughter and successful husband in an opulent modernist home. But she’s sleeping on the couch, hitting the wine hard, smoking again – prone to making bad decisions and picking fights with anyone who crosses her path. In the wake of a serious car accident, Allie runs. Hitting the road and hitting the booze, she sets off with her trusty dog Stanley and is by chance reunited along the way with her estranged friend Kaz, a sound recordist whose work chronicles environmental degradation. Travelling into increasingly remote stretches of alpine wilderness, her journey becomes primal, confronting the pain of the two women’s past and the unspoken realities of their relationship.

Set in the picturesque surrounds of Victoria’s High Country, Wilderness is at once a midlife crisis movie about women behaving badly and a parable about listening to the environments that surround us. Built around a bold lead performance by Mirrah Foulkes (Animal Kingdom) and strong support from Yael Stone (Blaze, MIFF 2022), Martin McKenna’s first film in over a decade is a thoughtful exploration of the balance between the personal and the ecological, and how urban landscapes can disconnect us from the planet and from ourselves.
synopsis_zh:
一位深陷中年危机的墨尔本医生，踏上了一场重返本真的朝圣之旅，走进维多利亚的阿尔卑斯山脉。

从外人看来，艾莉的生活堪称完美：身为医生，与正值青春期的女儿和事业有成的丈夫同住在一栋华美的现代主义住宅里。可她却睡在沙发上，酗酒无度，重新抽起了烟——动辄做出糟糕的决定，与任何挡在她路上的人争吵。一场严重车祸之后，艾莉选择了逃离。她一路上路、一路买醉，带着自己忠实的狗斯坦利出发，途中又偶然与久已疏远的旧友卡兹重逢——卡兹是一名录音师，以声音记录着环境的退化。随着旅途深入越来越荒僻的高山旷野，她们的旅程回归本真，直面两个女人过往的伤痛，也直面她们之间那些从未言说的现实。

《荒野独行》以维多利亚高地如画的风光为背景，既是一部关于女性肆意妄为的中年危机电影，也是一则关于倾听身边环境的寓言。影片以米拉·福克斯（《动物王国》）大胆的主演表演为核心，辅以雅艾尔·斯通（《焰》，2022 年墨尔本电影节）的有力配戏；这是马丁·麦肯纳十余年来的首部作品，深思熟虑地探索着个人与生态之间的平衡，以及都市景观如何令我们与这颗星球、与自我渐行渐远。
highlight_zh: 《动物王国》米拉·福克斯主演，中年危机遇上荒野生态寓言

synopsis_en:
PRESENTED BY

A genderqueer photographer uses their camera to reframe memories of childhood alienation in this tender tale about the hopeful potential of images to reconcile the past.

Having returned from studying in the UK to attend their father’s funeral in their rural Chinese village, Xiaoxiao seeks to reconnect with their mother through the camera’s viewfinder. Tender, precise and emotionally satisfying, Sun Kun’s semi-autobiographical film uses photographs to express the things we can’t say in words.
synopsis_zh:
一位性别酷儿摄影师用镜头重新框取童年时的疏离记忆——这是一个温柔的故事，讲述影像如何有望与过去和解。

晓晓从英国留学归来，回到中国乡村老家为父亲奔丧，试图透过取景框重新与母亲建立联系。孙堃这部半自传体影片温柔、精准而情感充盈，用一张张照片道出那些无法言说的心事。
highlight_zh: 半自传性别酷儿影像，以镜头温柔和解亲情与过去

synopsis_en:
PRESENTED BY

This SXSW Grand Jury Award–winning high-concept rom-com playfully questions the notion that love is the most powerful force in existence … for better or worse.

Charlie and Julia are deeply in love. They are also fundamentally incompatible – Charlie wants to get married and start a family; Julia doesn’t – and they spend as much time fighting as they do making up. Desperate to fix their relationship, they visit twin TikTok love gurus Tilly and Tilly, whose mysterious counsel reminds the couple how and why they fell for each other. It also unlocks something else, with earth-shattering repercussions … literally.

Cheekily interpreting the real-world implications of Rhonda Byrne’s pseudoscientific self-help hit The Secret, first-time feature director Graham Parkes creates a wonderfully witty, lived-in love story with a supernatural through-line that’s as hilarious as it is horrifying. Utilising ingenious split-screen techniques and capturing the stunning vistas of the Pacific Northwest, Parkes and cinematographer Christopher Ripley ensure the film looks incredible, while Lewis Pullman (The Testament of Ann Lee) and Maya Hawke (Once Upon a Time in Hollywood, MIFF 2019; Stranger Things) are phenomenal as Charlie and Julia, exuding chemistry while anchoring every emotional heartbeat and delivering every punchline to perfection. With a supporting cast that includes Randall Park, Kate Berlant and Jon Hamm as himself in a brilliantly executed recurring bit, Wishful Thinking is a boldly original, funny and insightful study of the wonderful delusion at the heart of all love: that its exhilarating highs and heartbreaking lows can change the world.
synopsis_zh:
这部荣获西南偏南电影节评审团大奖的高概念爱情喜剧，俏皮地叩问了那个被奉为圭臬的信念——爱是世间最强大的力量……而结果，或好或坏。

查理和茱莉亚深深相爱，却又从根本上格格不入——查理想结婚、组建家庭，茱莉亚却不想；两人吵架和好的时间几乎一样多。为了挽救这段感情，他们求助于一对 TikTok 爱情博主、孪生姐妹蒂莉和蒂莉，两人玄之又玄的开导让这对恋人重新想起了当初为何、又是如何爱上彼此。可与此同时，某种别的东西也被一并解开了——并带来了足以撼动天地的后果……字面意义上的。

初执导筒的格雷厄姆·帕克斯，狡黠地将朗达·拜恩那本伪科学励志畅销书《秘密》搬进现实、推演其真实效力，打造出一个妙趣横生、鲜活可感的爱情故事，其中贯穿始终的超自然主线既叫人捧腹又令人毛骨悚然。帕克斯与摄影师克里斯托弗·里普利运用了极具巧思的分屏手法，捕捉太平洋西北地区壮丽的景致，让影片的画面美得惊人；而刘易斯·普尔曼（《安·李的遗言》）与玛雅·霍克（《好莱坞往事》，2019 年墨尔本电影节；《怪奇物语》）饰演的查理与茱莉亚更是光彩夺目，二人化学反应十足，稳稳托住每一处情感起伏，也把每一句妙语都抖得恰到好处。配角阵容包括朴兰道、凯特·柏兰特，以及本色出演、以一段精彩反复出现的桥段现身的乔·汉姆。《痴心妄想》是一部大胆原创、诙谐而深刻的作品，剖析了潜藏在一切爱情核心处的那个美妙的错觉：以为它那令人心醉的高潮与叫人心碎的低谷，真的能够改变世界。
highlight_zh: 西南偏南大奖高概念爱情喜剧，玛雅·霍克主演

synopsis_en:
A young man’s bizarre obsession spurs a family intervention – but what if there really is a mind-controlling worm in his backyard?

Convinced a malevolent worm is controlling his thoughts, Kieran (Joe Bird, Leviticus) hunts it down on his parents’ property, provoking an absurd confrontation. Premiering at Sundance, this raucously silly comedy from Tom Noakes (Nursery Rhymes, MIFF 2018) could be an allegory for addiction, mental health crisis or conspiracy theory indoctrination – or Kieran could be right …
synopsis_zh:
一个年轻人离奇的偏执引发了一场家庭干预——可要是他家后院里真的有一条操控心智的虫子呢？

基兰（乔·伯德饰，《利未记》）坚信有一条恶毒的虫子在操控自己的思想，于是在父母的宅院里四处追猎，掀起一场荒诞的对峙。这部在圣丹斯电影节首映、由汤姆·诺克斯（《童谣》，2018年墨尔本国际电影节）执导的喧闹无厘头喜剧，或许是对成瘾、精神危机或阴谋论洗脑的一则寓言——又或者，基兰是对的……
highlight_zh: 圣丹斯首映，无厘头恐怖喜剧，脑洞暗喻成瘾与阴谋论

synopsis_en:
Films for freakmaxxers, fetishists and other fans of flickering filth.

What do you get when you combine films about a lonely woman’s torrid affair with a severed finger; a dying man who visits a sex worker in search of a graphic birth in reverse; and a vicious predator who’s part woman, part insect and all mother? Why MIFF’s WTF Shorts, of course – the festival’s own garden of unholy cinematic delights.
synopsis_zh:
献给猎奇成瘾者、恋物癖者，以及一切迷恋银幕污秽之光的影迷。

当你把这几部短片搅在一起会发生什么——一个寂寞女人与一根断指的炽热私情；一个濒死男人造访性工作者，只求目睹一场逆向倒放的血腥分娩；还有一头凶残的掠食者，半是女人、半是昆虫，却是彻头彻尾的母亲。答案当然是墨尔本国际电影节的 WTF 短片集——电影节亲手栽种的一座亵渎神明的光影乐园。
highlight_zh: MIFF 招牌猎奇单元，重口猎奇脑洞放送，胆大者请入场

synopsis_en:
This Golden Bear winner is a timely portrait of artists suffering persecution from the filmmaker behind the Oscar-nominated The Teachers’ Lounge.

Playwright Aziz and actor Derya are a husband-and-wife pair known for their avant-garde theatre works. But when they receive menacing ‘yellow letters’ – official government edicts – labelling their work “too political”, they swiftly lose the platform and careers they’ve spent so long building, and their life begins to unravel. As the couple move from Ankara to Istanbul with their 13-year-old daughter, relationships begin to fray and ethics become compromised, the hostility of the Turkish regime infecting the family dynamic.
